<p>AzuraCast is built as a modern web application with a layered architecture that prioritizes containerization, scalability, and flexibility. The system is primarily deployed using Docker, which encapsulates all necessary components in a unified environment while maintaining clear separation of concerns.</p>

<p>AzuraCast's architecture revolves around Docker containers orchestrated through Docker Compose:</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>Web Container (azuracast)</strong>: The main container that hosts multiple services:</p>
<ul>
<li>Nginx web server for handling HTTP requests</li>
<li>PHP-FPM for processing PHP application code</li>
<li>MariaDB for database storage</li>
<li>Redis for caching and message queue</li>
<li>Liquidsoap for AutoDJ functionality</li>
<li>Broadcasting servers (Icecast, SHOUTcast, RSAS)</li>
<li>Centrifugo for WebSocket/real-time updates</li>
<li>SFTPGo for SFTP file transfers</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Updater Container</strong>: Handles system updates using Watchtower</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>The <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">docker.sh</code> utility script provides a simplified interface for managing the Docker environment, handling installation, updates, backups, and other administrative tasks.</p>

<p>The system uses several Docker volumes to maintain persistent data:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>station_data</strong>: Contains station media files and configuration</li>
<li><strong>backups</strong>: Stores system backups</li>
<li><strong>db_data</strong>: Contains the MariaDB database files</li>
<li><strong>www_uploads</strong>: User-uploaded media and files</li>
<li><strong>acme</strong>: SSL certificates managed by ACME</li>
<li><strong>geolite_install</strong>: GeoIP location database</li>
<li><strong>sftpgo_data</strong>: SFTP server configuration and data</li>
</ul>

<ol>
<li><strong>Slim Framework</strong>: Handles HTTP routing and request/response handling</li>
<li><strong>PHP-DI</strong>: Manages dependency injection throughout the application</li>
<li><strong>Doctrine ORM</strong>: Provides object-relational mapping for database access</li>
<li><strong>Symfony Components</strong>: Various Symfony components for:
<ul>
<li>Console commands</li>
<li>Cache management</li>
<li>Event handling</li>
<li>Message queue (Symfony Messenger)</li>
<li>Validation</li>
<li>Serialization</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>

<p>The application uses Doctrine ORM entities to model its domain objects:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Entity Base Classes</strong>: Most entities use traits like <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">HasAutoIncrementId</code> or <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">HasUniqueId</code> for primary key management</li>
<li><strong>Entity Repositories</strong>: Custom repository classes extend Doctrine's EntityRepository for specialized query methods</li>
<li><strong>Entity Serialization</strong>: Entity normalization handled by custom normalizers for API responses</li>
</ul>

<p>The API is organized into several logical sections:</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>Public Endpoints</strong>: Access without authentication</p>
<ul>
<li>Now Playing data</li>
<li>Public station information</li>
<li>Miscellaneous endpoints</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Station Management</strong>: Station-specific operations</p>
<ul>
<li>Broadcasting control</li>
<li>Media management</li>
<li>Playlist management</li>
<li>Podcast management</li>
<li>Mount points and remote relays</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Administration</strong>: System-wide administration</p>
<ul>
<li>User and role management</li>
<li>Settings</li>
<li>Debugging tools</li>
<li>Backup management</li>
<li>Storage locations</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Account Management</strong>: User account operations</p>
<ul>
<li>Profile management</li>
<li>API key management</li>
<li>Two-factor authentication</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>

<p>The core of the streaming system is Liquidsoap, which:</p>
<ol>
<li>Manages media playback through playlists</li>
<li>Handles scheduled events</li>
<li>Processes song requests</li>
<li>Accepts live DJ broadcasts</li>
<li>Applies audio processing (if configured)</li>
<li>Outputs to broadcasting servers</li>
</ol>
<p>Liquidsoap is configured dynamically by AzuraCast based on station settings, with configuration files generated for each station.</p>

<p>AzuraCast supports multiple broadcasting server options:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Icecast</strong>: Default broadcasting server</li>
<li><strong>SHOUTcast</strong>: Optional alternative broadcasting server</li>
<li><strong>RSAS (Rocket Streaming Audio Server)</strong>: Optional commercial alternative</li>
<li><strong>HLS (HTTP Live Streaming)</strong>: For improved compatibility with modern browsers and devices</li>
</ol>
<p>Each broadcasting server exposes one or more mount points that listeners can connect to, with support for different audio formats and bitrates.</p>

<p>Optional audio processing can be applied using:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Stereo Tool</strong>: Commercial audio processing software</li>
<li><strong>master_me</strong>: Open-source audio mastering tool</li>
</ul>
<p>These tools can enhance audio quality, normalize volume levels, and apply effects before broadcasting.</p>

<p>Nginx is configured to:</p>
<ol>
<li>Serve static files directly</li>
<li>Forward PHP requests to PHP-FPM</li>
<li>Proxy WebSocket connections to Centrifugo</li>
<li>Handle SSL/TLS termination</li>
<li>Implement caching for improved performance</li>
<li>Support internal X-Accel-Redirect functionality for protected file access</li>
</ol>
<p>The configuration is templated and customized at container startup based on environment variables.</p>

<ol>
<li>Client requests arrive at Nginx</li>
<li>Static content is served directly</li>
<li>PHP requests are forwarded to PHP-FPM</li>
<li>WebSocket connections are proxied to Centrifugo</li>
<li>Station-specific routes are handled via included configuration files</li>
</ol>
<p>Each station has its own Nginx configuration that defines routes for its streams and mount points.</p>

<p>MariaDB stores all persistent application data, with tables corresponding to entity classes defined in the application. The database schema is managed through Doctrine migrations.</p>
<p>Key components:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Doctrine ORM</strong>: Object-Relational Mapper for database access</li>
<li><strong>Doctrine Migrations</strong>: Handles database schema versioning and updates</li>
<li><strong>Doctrine Extensions</strong>: Provides additional functionality like custom SQL functions</li>
</ul>

<p>Redis serves multiple purposes:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Caching</strong>: Application cache through Symfony Cache component</li>
<li><strong>Message Queue</strong>: Background processing via Symfony Messenger</li>
<li><strong>Session Storage</strong>: Web session storage for authenticated users</li>
<li><strong>Real-time Data</strong>: Now playing data for Centrifugo</li>
</ol>

<p>Each type of storage has a dedicated location type:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Station Media</strong>: Music files for station playlists</li>
<li><strong>Station Recordings</strong>: Recorded broadcasts</li>
<li><strong>Backup Storage</strong>: System backups</li>
<li><strong>Podcast Media</strong>: Podcast episode files</li>
</ol>

<p>Multiple storage adapters are supported:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Local Storage</strong>: Default, files stored on the local filesystem</li>
<li><strong>S3-Compatible</strong>: Amazon S3 or compatible services (DigitalOcean Spaces, etc.)</li>
<li><strong>Dropbox</strong>: Dropbox cloud storage</li>
<li><strong>SFTP</strong>: Remote storage via SFTP protocol</li>
</ol>
<p>Each storage location can use a different adapter, allowing for flexible configuration based on needs and resources.</p>

<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>Priority Queues</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>High Priority: Urgent tasks requiring immediate processing</li>
<li>Normal Priority: Standard operations</li>
<li>Low Priority: Background tasks that can be delayed</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Specialized Queues</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Media Processing: Handling media file processing (metadata extraction, waveform generation)</li>
<li>Podcast Media: Podcast episode processing</li>
<li>Search Index: Updating search indices</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>

<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>Application Logs</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Main application log</li>
<li>Now playing update log</li>
<li>Synchronization task log</li>
<li>Queue worker log</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Service Logs</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Nginx access and error logs</li>
<li>PHP error logs</li>
<li>Service-specific logs (Redis, MariaDB, etc.)</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Station Logs</strong>:</p>
<ul>
<li>Liquidsoap logs</li>
<li>Icecast/SHOUTcast logs</li>
<li>Station configuration logs</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>

<p>Logs can be accessed through:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Admin UI</strong>: Administrators can view all system logs</li>
<li><strong>Station UI</strong>: Station managers can view station-specific logs</li>
<li><strong>API</strong>: Logs are accessible via API endpoints for integration with external tools</li>
</ol>

<ol>
<li><strong>Event Generation</strong>: System events occur (new song playing, configuration changes, etc.)</li>
<li><strong>Event Processing</strong>: Events are processed and formatted for publication</li>
<li><strong>Event Publication</strong>: Events are published to Centrifugo</li>
<li><strong>Event Delivery</strong>: Centrifugo delivers events to connected clients in real-time</li>
</ol>
<p>This architecture enables real-time updates in the web interface without requiring page refreshes, providing a more responsive user experience.</p>

<p>AzuraCast's architecture is designed for modularity, scalability, and ease of deployment. The Docker-based system encapsulates all necessary components while maintaining clear separation of concerns. The PHP backend, coupled with Vue.js frontend, provides a robust foundation for managing web radio stations, while the streaming subsystem handles the technical aspects of audio broadcasting.</p>
<p>Key architectural features include:</p>
<ul>
<li>Containerized deployment with Docker</li>
<li>Modular backend built with modern PHP frameworks</li>
<li>Comprehensive REST API</li>
<li>Flexible storage system supporting multiple backends</li>
<li>Real-time updates via WebSockets</li>
<li>Background processing with message queues</li>
<li>Detailed logging and monitoring</li>
</ul>
<p>This architecture allows AzuraCast to function effectively across diverse hosting environments while providing the performance and features needed for managing radio stations.</p></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="hidden overflow-hidden transition-[border-radius] xl:sticky xl:right-0 xl:top-20 xl:block xl:h-[calc(100vh-82px)] xl:w-64 xl:flex-shrink-0 [util/write_git_info.sh](util/write_git_info.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides a comprehensive overview of AzuraCast's system architecture, core components, and technology stack. It covers the high-level design patterns, major subsystems, and their interactions within the codebase.\n\nFor detailed installation procedures, see [Installation and Deployment](#2). For audio streaming specifics, see [Streaming Engine](#3). For API documentation, see [API and Integration](#4). For frontend architecture details, see [Frontend](#5).\n\n## System Purpose and Architecture\n\nAzuraCast is a self-hosted web radio management suite designed to provide a complete broadcasting solution through a unified Docker container. The system integrates multiple specialized services into a cohesive platform for managing radio stations, media libraries, live streaming, and listener analytics.\n\n### High-Level System Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Container Runtime\"\n        subgraph \"Web Layer\"\n            Nginx[\"nginx\u003cbr/\u003eStatic Assets\u003cbr/\u003eSSL Termination\u003cbr/\u003eReverse Proxy\"]\n            PHP[\"php-fpm\u003cbr/\u003eApplication Logic\u003cbr/\u003eSymfony Framework\u003cbr/\u003eDoctrine ORM\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Audio Engine\"\n            Liquidsoap[\"liquidsoap\u003cbr/\u003eAutoDJ Scheduler\u003cbr/\u003eAudio Processing\u003cbr/\u003eHarbor Input\"]\n            Icecast[\"icecast\u003cbr/\u003eStreaming Frontend\u003cbr/\u003eMount Points\u003cbr/\u003eHLS Support\"]\n            Shoutcast[\"shoutcast2\u003cbr/\u003eAlternative Frontend\u003cbr/\u003eLegacy Protocol\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Data Services\"\n            MariaDB[\"mariadb-server\u003cbr/\u003ePrimary Database\u003cbr/\u003eEntity Storage\u003cbr/\u003eConfiguration\"]\n            Valkey[\"valkey-server\u003cbr/\u003eCache Layer\u003cbr/\u003eSession Storage\u003cbr/\u003eMessage Queue\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Storage Systems\"\n            MediaStorage[\"/var/azuracast/stations\u003cbr/\u003eMedia Files\u003cbr/\u003eRecordings\u003cbr/\u003eStation Data\"]\n            ConfigStorage[\"/var/azuracast/storage\u003cbr/\u003eConfiguration\u003cbr/\u003eUploads\u003cbr/\u003eCertificates\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"External Interfaces\"\n        WebUI[\"Vue.js Frontend\u003cbr/\u003eAdministrative Interface\u003cbr/\u003ePublic Pages\"]\n        RestAPI[\"REST API\u003cbr/\u003eOpenAPI Specification\u003cbr/\u003eReal-time Updates\"]\n        StreamClients[\"Stream Clients\u003cbr/\u003eMedia Players\u003cbr/\u003eBroadcasting Software\"]\n    end\n    \n    WebUI --\u003e Nginx\n    RestAPI --\u003e Nginx\n    Nginx --\u003e PHP\n    PHP --\u003e MariaDB\n    PHP --\u003e Valkey\n    PHP --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e Icecast\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e Shoutcast\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e MediaStorage\n    StreamClients --\u003e Icecast\n    StreamClients --\u003e Shoutcast\n    PHP --\u003e MediaStorage\n    PHP --\u003e ConfigStorage\n```\n\n**Sources:** [Dockerfile:41-187](), [composer.json:2-4](), [util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh:1-38](), [util/docker/web/setup/php.sh:1-18]()\n\n### Core Application Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"App Namespace Structure\"\n        Console[\"App\\Console\\Application\u003cbr/\u003eCLI Commands\u003cbr/\u003eMigration Tools\u003cbr/\u003eSync Tasks\"]\n        Entity[\"App\\Entity\\*\u003cbr/\u003eDoctrine Entities\u003cbr/\u003eDatabase Models\u003cbr/\u003eRepository Classes\"]\n        Controller[\"App\\Controller\\*\u003cbr/\u003eHTTP Controllers\u003cbr/\u003eAPI Endpoints\u003cbr/\u003eAdmin Interface\"]\n        Service[\"App\\Service\\*\u003cbr/\u003eBusiness Logic\u003cbr/\u003eExternal Integrations\u003cbr/\u003eAudio Processing\"]\n        Radio[\"App\\Radio\\*\u003cbr/\u003eLiquidsoap Management\u003cbr/\u003eAutoDJ Logic\u003cbr/\u003eStreaming Control\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Infrastructure Services\"\n        Environment[\"App\\Environment\u003cbr/\u003eConfiguration Manager\u003cbr/\u003eSettings Provider\"]\n        EventDispatcher[\"App\\CallableEventDispatcherInterface\u003cbr/\u003ePlugin System\u003cbr/\u003eEvent Handling\"]\n        MessageQueue[\"App\\MessageQueue\\*\u003cbr/\u003eAsync Processing\u003cbr/\u003eBackground Jobs\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"External Dependencies\"\n        Doctrine[\"Doctrine\\ORM\\EntityManagerInterface\u003cbr/\u003eDatabase Abstraction\u003cbr/\u003eQuery Builder\"]\n        Symfony[\"Symfony Components\u003cbr/\u003eConsole, Cache, Mailer\u003cbr/\u003eSerializer, Validator\"]\n        Slim[\"Slim\\App\u003cbr/\u003eHTTP Framework\u003cbr/\u003eRouting Engine\"]\n    end\n    \n    Controller --\u003e Service\n    Service --\u003e Entity\n    Service --\u003e Radio\n    Console --\u003e Service\n    Service --\u003e Doctrine\n    Service --\u003e EventDispatcher\n    Service --\u003e MessageQueue\n    Environment --\u003e Doctrine\n    EventDispatcher --\u003e Symfony\n    Controller --\u003e Slim\n```\n\n**Sources:** [backend/config/services.php:1-541](), [composer.json:166-169](), [backend/src/Console/Command/MigrateDbCommand.php:1-91]()\n\n## Core Technology Stack\n\n### Backend Infrastructure\n\nAzuraCast's backend is built on **PHP 8.4** with a comprehensive set of modern frameworks and libraries:\n\n| Component | Technology | Purpose |\n|-----------|------------|---------|\n| **Web Framework** | `Slim\\App` | HTTP routing and middleware |\n| **ORM** | `Doctrine\\ORM` + `Doctrine\\DBAL` | Database abstraction and entity management |\n| **Dependency Injection** | `PHP-DI\\Container` | Service container and dependency resolution |\n| **Event System** | `App\\CallableEventDispatcherInterface` | Plugin architecture and event handling |\n| **Message Queue** | `Symfony\\Messenger` + Redis | Asynchronous task processing |\n| **Caching** | `Symfony\\Cache` + Redis/Valkey | Performance optimization |\n| **Serialization** | `Symfony\\Serializer` + `Azura\\Normalizer` | API data transformation |\n\nThe application uses a custom `App\\Doctrine\\DecoratedEntityManager` that wraps the standard Doctrine EntityManager to provide additional functionality like audit logging and automatic change tracking.\n\n**Sources:** [composer.json:12-97](), [backend/config/services.php:115-172]()\n\n### Database Layer\n\nThe system utilizes **MariaDB 11.8** as the primary database with several customizations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Database Configuration\"\n        Connection[\"Doctrine\\DBAL\\Connection\u003cbr/\u003ePDO MySQL Driver\u003cbr/\u003eUTF8MB4 Charset\u003cbr/\u003eCustom Platform\"]\n        Platform[\"App\\Doctrine\\Platform\\MariaDbPlatform\u003cbr/\u003eHigh-precision DateTime\u003cbr/\u003eMariaDB-specific Features\"]\n        EventManager[\"Doctrine\\Common\\EventManager\u003cbr/\u003eAudit Logging\u003cbr/\u003eStation Restart Triggers\u003cbr/\u003eChange Tracking\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Entity Management\"\n        EntityManager[\"App\\Doctrine\\DecoratedEntityManager\u003cbr/\u003eEntity Lifecycle\u003cbr/\u003eRepository Access\u003cbr/\u003eQuery Building\"]\n        Repositories[\"Entity Repositories\u003cbr/\u003eCustom Query Methods\u003cbr/\u003eData Access Layer\"]\n        Entities[\"App\\Entity\\*\u003cbr/\u003eStation, Media, User\u003cbr/\u003eSettings, SongHistory\"]\n    end\n    \n    Connection --\u003e Platform\n    Connection --\u003e EventManager\n    EntityManager --\u003e Connection\n    EntityManager --\u003e Repositories\n    Repositories --\u003e Entities\n    EventManager --\u003e Entities\n```\n\n**Sources:** [backend/config/services.php:62-113](), [util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh:5-6](), [backend/config/services.php:87-89]()\n\n### Audio Broadcasting Engine\n\nThe audio processing pipeline is powered by **Liquidsoap 2.3.2** with multiple streaming frontends:\n\n| Service | Configuration | Purpose |\n|---------|---------------|---------|\n| **Liquidsoap** | Dynamic configuration generation | AutoDJ scheduling, audio processing, live DJ support |\n| **Icecast** | Custom build with KH modifications | Primary streaming server, HLS support |\n| **Shoutcast v2** | Licensed binary support | Legacy protocol compatibility |\n| **RSAS** | Rocket Streaming Audio Server | Drop-in Icecast replacement |\n\nThe system generates Liquidsoap configurations dynamically using template-based approach, allowing for station-specific customization while maintaining centralized management.\n\n**Sources:** [CHANGELOG.md:8-8](), [CHANGELOG.md:78-80](), [Dockerfile:16-16](), [Dockerfile:35-36]()\n\n### Containerization Strategy\n\nAzuraCast uses a **unified Docker container** approach that includes all necessary services:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Docker Image Layers\"\n        BaseImage[\"php:8.4-fpm-bookworm\u003cbr/\u003eDebian Base System\u003cbr/\u003ePHP Runtime\"]\n        Dependencies[\"Dependencies Layer\u003cbr/\u003eMariaDB Binaries\u003cbr/\u003eIcecast Binary\u003cbr/\u003ePHP Extensions\"]\n        Application[\"Application Layer\u003cbr/\u003ePHP Source Code\u003cbr/\u003eComposer Dependencies\u003cbr/\u003eStatic Assets\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Runtime Volumes\"\n        StationData[\"/var/azuracast/stations\u003cbr/\u003eMedia Files\u003cbr/\u003eRecordings\u003cbr/\u003eStation Configs\"]\n        StorageData[\"/var/azuracast/storage\u003cbr/\u003eUploads, Certificates\u003cbr/\u003eSFTP, Plugins\"]\n        DatabaseData[\"/var/lib/mysql\u003cbr/\u003eMariaDB Data\u003cbr/\u003ePersistent Storage\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Process Management\"\n        Supervisor[\"supervisord\u003cbr/\u003eProcess Manager\u003cbr/\u003eService Orchestration\"]\n        Services[\"Web, Database, Audio\u003cbr/\u003eRedis, Nginx\u003cbr/\u003eBackground Workers\"]\n    end\n    \n    BaseImage --\u003e Dependencies\n    Dependencies --\u003e Application\n    Application --\u003e Supervisor\n    Supervisor --\u003e Services\n    Services --\u003e StationData\n    Services --\u003e StorageData\n    Services --\u003e DatabaseData\n```\n\n**Sources:** [Dockerfile:1-187](), [Dockerfile:95-104](), [Dockerfile:109-130]()\n\n## Build and Deployment Pipeline\n\n### CI/CD Architecture\n\nThe project uses **GitHub Actions** for automated testing, building, and publishing:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Source Control\"\n        GitRepo[\"GitHub Repository\u003cbr/\u003eMain Branch\u003cbr/\u003ePull Requests\u003cbr/\u003eRelease Tags\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Testing Pipeline\"\n        PHPTests[\"PHP Test Suite\u003cbr/\u003ePHPStan Analysis\u003cbr/\u003eCode Standards\u003cbr/\u003eParallel Lint\"]\n        NodeTests[\"Node Test Suite\u003cbr/\u003eTypeScript Checks\u003cbr/\u003eESLint Validation\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Build Pipeline\"\n        AssetBuild[\"Asset Compilation\u003cbr/\u003eVite Build System\u003cbr/\u003eTranslation Import\u003cbr/\u003eAPI Documentation\"]\n        DockerBuild[\"Multi-platform Build\u003cbr/\u003eAMD64 + ARM64\u003cbr/\u003eDepot Build System\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Distribution\"\n --\u003e NodeTests\n    PHPTests --\u003e AssetBuild\n    NodeTests --\u003e AssetBuild\n    AssetBuild --\u003e DockerBuild\n    DockerBuild --\u003e DockerHub\n    DockerBuild --\u003e GHCR\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:1-227](), [.github/workflows/default.yml:29-65](), [.github/workflows/default.yml:172-227]()\n\n### PHP Dependency Management\n\nThe application manages dependencies through **Composer** with specific version constraints optimized for stability and security:\n\n- **Core Framework**: Requires PHP 8.4+ with essential extensions (`ext-PDO`, `ext-redis`, `ext-gd`, etc.)\n- **Database**: Doctrine ORM 3.x with DBAL 4.x for modern PHP compatibility  \n- **HTTP**: Slim 4.x framework with PSR-compliant middleware\n- **Async Processing**: Symfony Messenger with Redis transport\n- **Audio Processing**: Custom integration with `php-ffmpeg` and `james-heinrich/getid3`\n\n**Sources:** [composer.json:12-97](), [composer.json:116-137]()\n\n## Plugin and Extension Architecture\n\nAzuraCast provides a plugin system through event-driven architecture:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Plugin Loading\"\n        PluginManager[\"App\\Plugins\u003cbr/\u003ePlugin Discovery\u003cbr/\u003eEvent Registration\u003cbr/\u003eService Registration\"]\n Events\u003cbr/\u003eBuildConsoleCommands\u003cbr/\u003eBuildDoctrineMappingPaths\u003cbr/\u003eBuildMigrationConfiguration\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Extension Points\"\n        Console[\"Console Commands\u003cbr/\u003eCustom CLI Tools\"]\n        Database[\"Database Entities\u003cbr/\u003eCustom Tables\u003cbr/\u003eMigration Scripts\"]\n        MessageQueue[\"Message Handlers\u003cbr/\u003eBackground Processing\"]\n    end\n    \n    PluginManager --\u003e ComposerMerge\n    PluginManager --\u003e EventDispatcher\n    EventDispatcher --\u003e CoreEvents\n    CoreEvents --\u003e Console\n    CoreEvents --\u003e Database\n    CoreEvents --\u003e MessageQueue\n```\n\n**Sources:** [composer.json:152-164](), [backend/config/services.php:299-319](), [backend/config/services.php:285-295]()\n\nAzuraCast represents a sophisticated integration of multiple open-source technologies unified under a modern PHP application architecture, containerized for provides a comprehensive overview of AzuraCast's system architecture, explaining how different components interact to form a complete web radio management platform. For deployment instructions, see [Installation and Deployment](#2), and for detailed API information, see [API and Integration](#4).\n\n## Overview\n\nAzuraCast is built as a modern web application with a layered architecture that prioritizes containerization, scalability, and flexibility. The system is primarily deployed using Docker, which encapsulates all necessary components in a unified environment while maintaining clear separation of concerns.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"User Interface Layer\"\n        WebUI[\"Web UI\"]\n        API[\"REST API\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Application Layer\"\n        PHP[\"PHP Backend\"]\n        MessageQueue[\"Message Queue\\nSymfony Messenger\"]\n        Cache[\"Redis Cache\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Data Layer\"\n        DB[\"MariaDB Database\"]\n        Storage[\"Storage Systems\\n(Local/S3/Dropbox/SFTP)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Streaming Layer\"\n        AutoDJ[\"AutoDJ System\\n(Liquidsoap)\"]\n        Broadcasters[\"Broadcasting Servers\\n(Icecast/SHOUTcast/RSAS)\"]\n    end\n\n    WebUI --\u003e PHP\n    API --\u003e PHP\n    PHP --\u003e DB\n    PHP --\u003e Cache\n    PHP --\u003e Storage\n    PHP --\u003e MessageQueue\n    MessageQueue --\u003e PHP\n    PHP --\u003e AutoDJ\n    AutoDJ --\u003e Broadcasters\n    Broadcasters --\u003e \"Listeners\"\n```\n\nSources:\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml]()\n- [Dockerfile]()\n- [composer.json]()\n- [docker.sh]()\n\n## Docker-Based Architecture\n\nAzuraCast uses Docker as its primary deployment method, encapsulating all required services in containerized environments for consistent operation across different hosting platforms.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    User[[\"User\"]] --\u003e DockerSh[\"docker.sh\\nUtility Script\"]\n    \n    DockerSh --\u003e DockerCompose[\"Docker Compose\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Docker Environment\"\n        Web[\"Web Container\\n(azuracast)\"]\n        Updater[\"Updater Container\\n(azuracast_updater)\"]\n    end\n    \n    DockerCompose --\u003e Web\n    DockerCompose --\u003e Updater\n    \n    subgraph \"Web Container Services\"\n        Nginx[\"Nginx Web Server\"]\n        PhpFpm[\"PHP-FPM\"]\n        MariaDB[\"MariaDB\"]\n        Redis[\"Redis\"]\n        Liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap/AutoDJ\"]\n        Icecast[\"Icecast\"]\n        SHOUTcast[\"SHOUTcast (Optional)\"]\n        RSAS[\"RSAS (Optional)\"]\n        Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo\\n(WebSocket/Real-time)\"]\n        SftpGo[\"SFTPGo\\n(SFTP Server)\"]\n    end\n    \n    Web --\u003e Nginx\n    Web --\u003e PhpFpm\n    Web --\u003e MariaDB\n    Web --\u003e Redis\n    Web --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    Web --\u003e Icecast\n    Web --\u003e SHOUTcast\n    Web --\u003e RSAS\n    Web --\u003e Centrifugo\n    Web --\u003e SftpGo\n    \n    subgraph \"Docker Volumes\"\n        StationData[\"stations\\n(Station Media/Config)\"]\n        Backups[\"backups\"]\n        DbData[\"db_data\\n(Database Files)\"]\n        Uploads[\"www_uploads\"]\n        AcmeData[\"acme\\n(SSL Certificates)\"]\n        GeoLite[\"geolite_install\"]\n        SftpData[\"sftpgo_data\"]\n    end\n    \n    Web --\u003e StationData\n    Web --\u003e Backups\n    Web --\u003e DbData\n    Web --\u003e Uploads\n    Web --\u003e AcmeData\n    Web --\u003e GeoLite\n    Web --\u003e SftpData\n```\n\nSources:\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml]()\n- [Dockerfile]()\n- [docker.sh]()\n- [docker-compose.dev.yml]()\n- [docker-compose.installer.yml]()\n\n### Container Management\n\nAzuraCast's architecture revolves around Docker containers orchestrated through Docker Compose:\n\n1. **Web Container (azuracast)**: The main container that hosts multiple services:\n   - Nginx web server for handling HTTP requests\n   - PHP-FPM for processing PHP application code\n   - MariaDB for database storage\n   - Redis for caching and message queue\n   - Liquidsoap for AutoDJ functionality\n   - Broadcasting servers (Icecast, SHOUTcast, RSAS)\n   - Centrifugo for WebSocket/real-time updates\n   - SFTPGo for SFTP file transfers\n\n2. **Updater Container**: Handles system updates using Watchtower\n\nThe `docker.sh` utility script provides a simplified interface for managing the Docker environment, handling installation, updates, backups, and other administrative tasks.\n\nSources:\n- [docker.sh:424-492]()\n- [docker.sh:547-637]()\n- [Dockerfile:95-101]()\n\n### Docker Volumes\n\nThe system uses several Docker volumes to maintain persistent data:\n\n- **station_data**: Contains station media files and configuration\n- **backups**: Stores system backups\n- **db_data**: Contains the MariaDB database files\n- **www_uploads**: User-uploaded media and files\n- **acme**: SSL certificates managed by ACME\n- **geolite_install**: GeoIP location database\n- **sftpgo_data**: SFTP server configuration and data\n\nSources:\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml:177-219]()\n- [Dockerfile:95-104]()\n\n## Application Architecture\n\nAzuraCast's application layer is built with PHP, following modern application architecture principles.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"HTTP Layer\"\n        Nginx[\"Nginx\"]\n        PhpFpm[\"PHP-FPM\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Application Core\"\n        SlimApp[\"Slim Application\"]\n        DI[\"PHP-DI\\nDependency Injection\"]\n        Controllers[\"API/Web Controllers\"]\n        Services[\"Service Classes\"]\n        Repositories[\"Entity Repositories\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Data Access\"\n        EntityManager[\"Doctrine ORM\\nEntity Manager\"]\n        Redis[\"Redis Cache/Queue\"]\n        Storage[\"Storage Adapters\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Domain Model\"\n        Entities[\"Entity Classes\"]\n    end\n    \n    Nginx --\u003e PhpFpm\n    PhpFpm --\u003e SlimApp\n    SlimApp --\u003e DI\n    DI --\u003e Controllers\n    DI --\u003e Services\n    DI --\u003e Repositories\n    \n    Controllers --\u003e Services\n    Services --\u003e Repositories\n    Repositories --\u003e EntityManager\n    EntityManager --\u003e Entities\n    \n    Services --\u003e Redis\n    Services --\u003e Storage\n```\n\nSources:\n- [composer.json:13-96]()\n- [backend/src/OpenApi.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasAutoIncrementId.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasUniqueId.php]()\n- [backend/src/Doctrine/Generator/UuidV6Generator.php]()\n\n### Core Components\n\n1. **Slim Framework**: Handles HTTP routing and request/response handling\n2. **PHP-DI**: Manages dependency injection throughout the application\n3. **Doctrine ORM**: Provides object-relational mapping for database access\n4. **Symfony Components**: Various Symfony components for:\n   - Console commands\n   - Cache management\n   - Event handling\n   - Message queue (Symfony Messenger)\n   - Validation\n   - Serialization\n\n### Entity System\n\nThe application uses Doctrine ORM entities to model its domain objects:\n\n- **Entity Base Classes**: Most entities use traits like `HasAutoIncrementId` or `HasUniqueId` for primary key management\n- **Entity Repositories**: Custom repository classes extend Doctrine's EntityRepository for specialized query methods\n- **Entity Serialization**: Entity normalization handled by custom normalizers for API responses\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasAutoIncrementId.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasUniqueId.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasSplitTokenFields.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Api/Traits/HasLinks.php]()\n\n## API Architecture\n\nAzuraCast exposes a comprehensive REST API documented using OpenAPI 3.0.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"API Endpoints\"\n        PublicAPI[\"Public API\\n(No Authentication)\"]\n        StationsAPI[\"Stations API\"]\n        AdminAPI[\"Admin API\"]\n        FrontendAPI[\"Frontend API\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"API Core\"\n        OpenApi[\"OpenAPI Definition\"]\n        Controllers[\"API Controllers\"]\n        Normalizers[\"Entity Normalizers\"]\n        DTOs[\"API-Specific DTOs\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Authentication\"\n        ApiKey[\"API Key Authentication\"]\n        Session[\"Session Authentication\"]\n    end\n    \n    PublicAPI --\u003e OpenApi\n    StationsAPI --\u003e OpenApi\n    AdminAPI --\u003e OpenApi\n    FrontendAPI --\u003e OpenApi\n    \n    OpenApi --\u003e Controllers\n    Controllers --\u003e Normalizers\n    Controllers --\u003e DTOs\n    \n    StationsAPI --\u003e ApiKey\n    AdminAPI --\u003e ApiKey\n    FrontendAPI --\u003e Session\n```\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/OpenApi.php]()\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/ApiKeysController.php]()\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Frontend/Account/ApiKeysController.php]()\n- [web/static/openapi.yml]()\n- [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts]()\n\n### API Organization\n\nThe API is organized into several logical sections:\n\n1. **Public Endpoints**: Access without authentication\n   - Now Playing data\n   - Public station information\n   - Miscellaneous endpoints\n\n2. **Station Management**: Station-specific operations\n   - Broadcasting control\n   - Media management\n   - Playlist management\n   - Podcast management\n   - Mount points and remote relays\n\n3. **Administration**: System-wide administration\n   - User and role management\n   - Settings\n   - Debugging tools\n   - Backup management\n   - Storage locations\n\n4. **Account Management**: User account operations\n   - Profile management\n   - API key management\n   - Two-factor authentication\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/OpenApi.php:32-67]()\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/LogsAction.php]()\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Stations/LogsAction.php]()\n\n### OpenAPI Documentation\n\nThe API is fully documented using OpenAPI 3.0, with documentation generated from PHP attributes. The OpenAPI specification provides:\n\n- Endpoint descriptions\n- Request/response schemas\n- Authentication requirements\n- Example values\n\nThe frontend TypeScript interfaces are auto-generated from the OpenAPI specification.\n\nSources:\n- [web/static/openapi.yml]()\n- [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts]()\n\n## Streaming Architecture\n\nThe streaming subsystem is a key component of AzuraCast, responsible for generating and broadcasting audio streams.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Content Sources\"\n        Media[\"Media Files\"]\n        Playlists[\"Playlists\"]\n        RemoteStreams[\"Remote Streams\"]\n        LiveBroadcasts[\"Live DJ Broadcasts\"]\n        WebDJ[\"Web DJ\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"AutoDJ System\"\n        Liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap\"]\n        AudioProcessing[\"Audio Processing\\n(Optional)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Broadcasting Servers\"\n        Icecast[\"Icecast\"]\n        SHOUTcast[\"SHOUTcast\\n(Optional)\"]\n        RSAS[\"RSAS\\n(Optional)\"]\n        HLS[\"HLS Streams\"]\n    end\n    \n    Media --\u003e Playlists\n    Playlists --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    RemoteStreams --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    LiveBroadcasts --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    WebDJ --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    \n    Liquidsoap --\u003e AudioProcessing\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e Icecast\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e SHOUTcast\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e RSAS\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e HLS\n    \n    Icecast --\u003e Listeners[\"Listeners\"]\n    SHOUTcast --\u003e Listeners\n    RSAS --\u003e Listeners\n    HLS --\u003e Listeners\n```\n\nSources:\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml:24-173]()\n- [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh]()\n- [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh]()\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl]()\n\n### AutoDJ System\n\nThe core of the streaming system is Liquidsoap, which:\n\n1. Manages media playback through playlists\n2. Handles scheduled events\n3. Processes song requests\n4. Accepts live DJ broadcasts\n5. Applies audio processing (if configured)\n6. Outputs to broadcasting servers\n\nLiquidsoap is configured dynamically by AzuraCast based on station settings, with configuration files generated for each station.\n\n### Broadcasting Servers\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple broadcasting server options:\n\n1. **Icecast**: Default broadcasting server\n2. **SHOUTcast**: Optional alternative broadcasting server\n3. **RSAS (Rocket Streaming Audio Server)**: Optional commercial alternative\n4. **HLS (HTTP Live Streaming)**: For improved compatibility with modern browsers and devices\n\nEach broadcasting server exposes one or more mount points that listeners can connect to, with support for different audio formats and bitrates.\n\n### Audio Processing\n\nOptional audio processing can be applied using:\n\n- **Stereo Tool**: Commercial audio processing software\n- **master_me**: Open-source audio mastering tool\n\nThese tools can enhance audio quality, normalize volume levels, and apply effects before broadcasting.\n\nSources:\n- [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh]()\n\n## Web Server Architecture\n\nNginx serves as the web server and reverse proxy for AzuraCast, handling HTTP requests and directing them to appropriate backend services.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    Client[[\"Client\"]] --\u003e Nginx[\"Nginx Web Server\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Static Content\"\n        StaticFiles[\"Static Files\\n(CSS/JS/Images)\"]\n        Docs[\"Built-in Documentation\"]\n        NowPlayingStatic[\"Static Now Playing Data\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Dynamic Content\"\n        PhpFpm[\"PHP-FPM\"]\n        Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo WebSocket\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Internal Routing\"\n        PublicAPI[\"/api\"]\n        WebInterface[\"/\"]\n        NowPlayingSSE[\"/api/live/nowplaying\"]\n        InternalBackups[\"/internal/backups\"]\n        InternalStorage[\"/internal/storage\"]\n        InternalStations[\"/internal/stations\"]\n    end\n    \n    Nginx --\u003e StaticFiles\n    Nginx --\u003e Docs\n    Nginx --\u003e NowPlayingStatic\n    \n    Nginx --\u003e PhpFpm\n    Nginx --\u003e Centrifugo\n    \n    PhpFpm --\u003e PublicAPI\n    PhpFpm --\u003e WebInterface\n    Centrifugo --\u003e NowPlayingSSE\n    \n    PhpFpm --\u003e InternalBackups\n    PhpFpm --\u003e InternalStorage\n    PhpFpm --\u003e InternalStations\n```\n\nSources:\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl]()\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/nginx.conf.tmpl]()\n- [util/docker/web/setup/nginx.sh]()\n\n### Configuration\n\nNginx is configured to:\n\n1. Serve static files directly\n2. Forward PHP requests to PHP-FPM\n3. Proxy WebSocket connections to Centrifugo\n4. Handle SSL/TLS termination\n5. Implement caching for improved performance\n6. Support internal X-Accel-Redirect functionality for protected file access\n\nThe configuration is templated and customized at container startup based on environment variables.\n\nSources:\n- [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh]()\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl]()\n\n### Request Flow\n\n1. Client requests arrive at Nginx\n2. Static content is served directly\n3. PHP requests are forwarded to PHP-FPM\n4. WebSocket connections are proxied to Centrifugo\n5. Station-specific routes are handled via included configuration files\n\nEach station has its own Nginx configuration that defines routes for its streams and mount points.\n\n## Database Architecture\n\nAzuraCast uses MariaDB as its primary database, with Redis for caching and message queueing.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Database Layer\"\n        MariaDB[\"MariaDB Database\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Cache/Queue Layer\"\n        Redis[\"Redis\"]\n        RedisCaching[\"Caching\"]\n        RedisQueue[\"Message Queue\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Application Layer\"\n        DoctrineORM[\"Doctrine ORM\"]\n        Entities[\"Entity Classes\"]\n        SymfonyCache[\"Symfony Cache\"]\n        SymfonyMessenger[\"Symfony Messenger\"]\n    end\n    \n    DoctrineORM --\u003e MariaDB\n    Entities --\u003e DoctrineORM\n    \n    SymfonyCache --\u003e RedisCaching\n    SymfonyMessenger --\u003e RedisQueue\n    \n    Redis --\u003e RedisCaching\n    Redis --\u003e RedisQueue\n```\n\nSources:\n- [composer.json:37-93]()\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml:177-219]()\n\n### MariaDB\n\nMariaDB stores all persistent application data, with tables corresponding to entity classes defined in the application. The database schema is managed through Doctrine migrations.\n\nKey components:\n- **Doctrine ORM**: Object-Relational Mapper for database access\n- **Doctrine Migrations**: Handles database schema versioning and updates\n- **Doctrine Extensions**: Provides additional functionality like custom SQL functions\n\n### Redis\n\nRedis serves multiple purposes:\n1. **Caching**: Application cache through Symfony Cache component\n2. **Message Queue**: Background processing via Symfony Messenger\n3. **Session Storage**: Web session storage for authenticated users\n4. **Real-time Data**: Now playing data for Centrifugo\n\n## Storage System\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple storage backends for media and other files.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Storage Locations\"\n        StationMedia[\"Station Media\"]\n        StationRecordings[\"Station Recordings\"]\n        Backups[\"Backup Storage\"]\n        Podcasts[\"Podcast Media\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Storage Adapters\"\n        Local[\"Local Storage\"]\n        S3[\"S3-Compatible\\n(AWS S3, DigitalOcean, etc)\"]\n        Dropbox[\"Dropbox\"]\n        SFTP[\"SFTP\"]\n    end\n    \n    StationMedia --\u003e StorageManager[\"Storage Manager\"]\n    StationRecordings --\u003e StorageManager\n    Backups --\u003e StorageManager\n    Podcasts --\u003e StorageManager\n    \n    StorageManager --\u003e Local\n    StorageManager --\u003e S3\n    StorageManager --\u003e Dropbox\n    StorageManager --\u003e SFTP\n```\n\nSources:\n- [composer.json:50-54]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/StorageLocation.php]()\n\n### Storage Locations\n\nEach type of storage has a dedicated location type:\n1. **Station Media**: Music files for station playlists\n2. **Station Recordings**: Recorded broadcasts\n3. **Backup Storage**: System backups\n4. **Podcast Media**: Podcast episode files\n\n### Storage Adapters\n\nMultiple storage adapters are supported:\n1. **Local Storage**: Default, files stored on the local filesystem\n2. **S3-Compatible**: Amazon S3 or compatible services (DigitalOcean Spaces, etc.)\n3. **Dropbox**: Dropbox cloud storage\n4. **SFTP**: Remote storage via SFTP protocol\n\nEach storage location can use a different adapter, allowing for flexible configuration based on needs and resources.\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/StorageLocation.php:282-391]()\n\n## Background Processing\n\nAzuraCast uses message queues for background processing tasks.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Message Sources\"\n        Stations[\"Station Operations\"]\n        Admin[\"Admin Operations\"]\n        Scheduled[\"Scheduled Tasks\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Message Queues\"\n        HighPriority[\"High Priority Queue\"]\n        NormalPriority[\"Normal Priority Queue\"]\n        LowPriority[\"Low Priority Queue\"]\n        \n        MediaProcessing[\"Media Processing Queue\"]\n        PodcastProcessing[\"Podcast Media Queue\"]\n        SearchIndex[\"Search Indexing Queue\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Message Consumers\"\n        QueueWorkers[\"Queue Workers\"]\n    end\n    \n    Stations --\u003e HighPriority\n    Stations --\u003e NormalPriority\n    Stations --\u003e LowPriority\n    Stations --\u003e MediaProcessing\n    \n    Admin --\u003e HighPriority\n    Admin --\u003e SearchIndex\n    \n    Scheduled --\u003e LowPriority\n    \n    HighPriority --\u003e QueueWorkers\n    NormalPriority --\u003e QueueWorkers\n    LowPriority --\u003e QueueWorkers\n    MediaProcessing --\u003e QueueWorkers\n    PodcastProcessing --\u003e QueueWorkers\n    SearchIndex --\u003e QueueWorkers\n```\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/Debug/ClearQueueAction.php]()\n\n### Queue Types\n\n1. **Priority Queues**:\n   - High Priority: Urgent tasks requiring immediate processing\n   - Normal Priority: Standard operations\n   - Low Priority: Background tasks that can be delayed\n\n2. **Specialized Queues**:\n   - Media Processing: Handling media file processing (metadata extraction, waveform generation)\n   - Podcast Media: Podcast episode processing\n   - Search Index: Updating search indices\n\n### Queue Processing\n\nThe system uses Symfony Messenger with Redis transport for message queuing. Queue workers run as separate processes, consuming messages as they become available.\n\n## Logging and Monitoring\n\nAzuraCast includes comprehensive logging and monitoring capabilities.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Log Sources\"\n        App[\"Application Logs\"]\n        Nginx[\"Nginx Logs\"]\n        PHP[\"PHP Logs\"]\n        Service[\"Service Logs\"]\n        Station[\"Station-specific Logs\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Log Storage\"\n        AppLog[\"App Log Files\"]\n        NowPlayingLog[\"Now Playing Logs\"]\n        SyncLog[\"Sync Task Logs\"]\n        WorkerLog[\"Queue Worker Logs\"]\n        \n        ServiceLogs[\"Service-specific Logs\"]\n        StationLogs[\"Station-specific Logs\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Log Viewing\"\n        AdminLogViewer[\"Admin Log Viewer\"]\n        StationLogViewer[\"Station Log Viewer\"]\n        \n        API[\"API Log Endpoints\"]\n    end\n    \n    App --\u003e AppLog\n    App --\u003e NowPlayingLog\n    App --\u003e SyncLog\n    App --\u003e WorkerLog\n    \n    Nginx --\u003e ServiceLogs\n    PHP --\u003e ServiceLogs\n    Service --\u003e ServiceLogs\n    \n    Station --\u003e StationLogs\n    \n    AppLog --\u003e AdminLogViewer\n    NowPlayingLog --\u003e AdminLogViewer\n    SyncLog --\u003e AdminLogViewer\n    WorkerLog --\u003e AdminLogViewer\n    ServiceLogs --\u003e AdminLogViewer\n    \n    StationLogs --\u003e StationLogViewer\n    \n    AdminLogViewer --\u003e API\n    StationLogViewer --\u003e API\n```\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/LogsAction.php]()\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Stations/LogsAction.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Api/LogType.php]()\n- [backend/src/Entity/Api/LogContents.php]()\n\n### Log Types\n\n1. **Application Logs**:\n   - Main application log\n   - Now playing update log\n   - Synchronization task log\n   - Queue worker log\n\n2. **Service Logs**:\n   - Nginx access and error logs\n   - PHP error logs\n   - Service-specific logs (Redis, MariaDB, etc.)\n\n3. **Station Logs**:\n   - Liquidsoap logs\n   - Icecast/SHOUTcast logs\n   - Station configuration logs\n\n### Log Access\n\nLogs can be accessed through:\n1. **Admin UI**: Administrators can view all system logs\n2. **Station UI**: Station managers can view station-specific logs\n3. **API**: Logs are accessible via API endpoints for integration with external tools\n\nSources:\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/LogsAction.php:120-209]()\n- [backend/src/Controller/Api/Stations/LogsAction.php:138-150]()\n\n## Real-time Updates\n\nAzuraCast provides real-time updates using Centrifugo for WebSocket communication.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Event Sources\"\n        NowPlaying[\"Now Playing Updates\"]\n        StationChanges[\"Station Configuration Changes\"]\n        SystemEvents[\"System Events\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Event Processing\"\n        EventHandler[\"Event Handler\"]\n        EventPublisher[\"Event Publisher\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"WebSocket Server\"\n        Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Clients\"\n        WebUI[\"Web Interface\"]\n        MobileApps[\"Mobile Apps\"]\n        ExternalIntegrations[\"External Integrations\"]\n    end\n    \n    NowPlaying --\u003e EventHandler\n    StationChanges --\u003e EventHandler\n    SystemEvents --\u003e EventHandler\n    \n    EventHandler --\u003e EventPublisher\n    EventPublisher --\u003e Centrifugo\n    \n    Centrifugo --\u003e WebUI\n    Centrifugo --\u003e MobileApps\n    Centrifugo --\u003e ExternalIntegrations\n```\n\nSources:\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl:101-104]()\n- [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:420-436]()\n\n### Event Flow\n\n1. **Event Generation**: System events occur (new song playing, configuration changes, etc.)\n2. **Event Processing**: Events are processed and formatted for publication\n3. **Event Publication**: Events are published to Centrifugo\n4. **Event Delivery**: Centrifugo delivers events to connected clients in real-time\n\nThis architecture enables real-time updates in the web interface without requiring page refreshes, providing a more responsive user experience.\n\n## Summary\n\nAzuraCast's architecture is designed for modularity, scalability, and ease of deployment. The Docker-based system encapsulates all necessary components while maintaining clear separation of concerns. The PHP backend, coupled with Vue.js frontend, provides a robust foundation for managing web radio stations, while the streaming subsystem handles the technical aspects of audio broadcasting.\n\nKey architectural features include:\n- Containerized deployment with Docker\n- Modular backend built with modern PHP frameworks\n- Comprehensive REST API\n- Flexible storage system supporting multiple backends\n- Real-time updates via WebSockets\n- Background processing with message queues\n- Detailed logging and monitoring\n\nThis architecture allows AzuraCast to function effectively across diverse hosting environments while providing the performance and features needed for managing radio stations."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"19:T3975,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Key Features and Components\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source features and architectural components. It covers the broadcasting engine, media management system, API infrastructure, webhook integrations, and administrative capabilities that make up the complete web radio management platform.\n\nFor detailed information about the underlying system architecture and containerization, see [System Architecture](#1.1). For deployment-specific configuration and setup procedures, see [Installation and Deployment](#2).\n\n## Broadcasting Engine\n\nAzuraCast's broadcasting capabilities are built around a sophisticated audio processing pipeline that handles automated playlist scheduling, live DJ support, and multi-format streaming output.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    AutoDJ[\"AutoDJ Scheduler\"] --\u003e Liquidsoap[\"liquidsoap Backend\"]\n    LiveDJ[\"Live DJ (Harbor)\"] --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e AudioProcessing[\"Audio Processing\"]\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e MasterMe[\"master_me\"]\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e StereoTool[\"StereoTool\"] \n    AudioProcessing --\u003e ReplayGain[\"ReplayGain\"]\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e IcecastFrontend[\"icecast Frontend\"]\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e ShoutcastFrontend[\"shoutcast2 Frontend\"] \n    Liquidsoap --\u003e RsasFrontend[\"rsas Frontend\"]\n    IcecastFrontend --\u003e Streams[\"Audio Streams\"]\n    ShoutcastFrontend --\u003e Streams\n    RsasFrontend --\u003e Streams\n    Streams --\u003e HLS[\"HLS Output\"]\n    Streams --\u003e DirectStreams[\"Direct Mount Points\"]\n```\n\n**Core Broadcasting Components:**\n\n| Component | Purpose | Configuration |\n|-----------|---------|---------------|\n| `liquidsoap` | Audio processing engine and AutoDJ | Dynamic configuration generation |\n| `icecast` | Open-source streaming server | Multiple mount points, metadata support |\n| `shoutcast2` | Proprietary streaming server | Commercial broadcasting features |\n| `rsas` | Rocket Streaming Audio Server | Drop-in Icecast replacement |\n\nThe broadcasting engine supports multiple audio processing methods defined in `AudioProcessingMethods` enum, including Liquidsoap's built-in processing, the open-source `master_me` tool, and commercial StereoTool integration.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:91-96](), [CHANGELOG.md:78-86](), [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh:1-27]()\n\n## Media Management System\n\nThe media management system handles file storage, metadata processing, playlist organization, and podcast distribution across multiple storage backends.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Storage Backends\"\n        LocalStorage[\"Local FileSystem\"]\n        S3Storage[\"S3 Compatible Storage\"] \n        DropboxStorage[\"Dropbox Storage\"]\n        SftpStorage[\"SFTP Storage\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Media Processing\"\n        ApiStationMedia[\"ApiStationMedia Entity\"]\n        FileUpload[\"File Upload Pipeline\"]\n        MetadataExtraction[\"Metadata Extraction\"]\n        AutoCue[\"AutoCue Analysis\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Organization\"\n        Playlists[\"Playlist Management\"]\n        PodcastSystem[\"Podcast Episodes\"]\n        CustomFields[\"Custom Metadata Fields\"]\n    end\n    \n    StorageLocationAdapters --\u003e LocalStorage\n    StorageLocationAdapters --\u003e S3Storage\n    StorageLocationAdapters --\u003e DropboxStorage\n    StorageLocationAdapters --\u003e SftpStorage\n    \n    FileUpload --\u003e MetadataExtraction\n    MetadataExtraction --\u003e AutoCue\n    AutoCue --\u003e ApiStationMedia\n    ApiStationMedia --\u003e Playlists\n    ApiStationMedia --\u003e PodcastSystem\n    CustomFields --\u003e ApiStationMedia\n```\n\n**Storage Location Types:**\n\nThe system supports four main storage adapter types defined in `StorageLocationAdapters` enum:\n- `local`: Direct filesystem storage\n- `s3`: S3-compatible object storage  \n- `dropbox`: Dropbox cloud storage integration\n- `sftp`: SFTP-based remote storage\n\n**Media Features:**\n- Automated metadata extraction and cue point detection\n- ReplayGain audio normalization\n- Visual waveform generation for cue editing\n- Batch media operations and bulk editing\n- Custom field support for extended metadata\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:174-179](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:1047-1055](), [CHANGELOG.md:235-240]()\n\n## API System Architecture\n\nAzuraCast exposes a comprehensive REST API with over 250 documented endpoints, real-time capabilities, and multiple authentication methods.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"API Endpoints\"\n        PublicAPI[\"/api/nowplaying/*\u003cbr/\u003ePublic Endpoints\"] \n        AdminAPI[\"/api/admin/*\u003cbr/\u003eAdministrative Endpoints\"]\n        StationAPI[\"/api/station/*\u003cbr/\u003eStation Management\"]\n        DebugAPI[\"/api/admin/debug/*\u003cbr/\u003eDebug Utilities\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Authentication\"\n        ApiKeys[\"API Key Authentication\"]\n        SessionAuth[\"Session Authentication\"] \n        Passkeys[\"WebAuthn Passkeys\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Real-time Updates\"\n        Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo WebSocket Server\"]\n      SwaggerUI[\"Interactive API Explorer\"]\n    end\n    \n    ApiKeys --\u003e PublicAPI\n    ApiKeys --\u003e AdminAPI\n    SessionAuth --\u003e StationAPI\n    Passkeys --\u003e SessionAuth\n DebugAPI\n```\n\n**API Categories:**\n\n| Endpoint Category | Purpose | Authentication |\n|------------------|---------|----------------|\n| `/api/nowplaying` | Public station data, requests | Optional API key |\n| `/api/admin/*` | System administration | Admin privileges required |\n| `/api/station/*` | Station management | Station permissions required |\n| `/api/admin/debug/*` | Debugging utilities | Super admin only |\n\n**Real-time Capabilities:**\n- WebSocket connections via Centrifugo for live updates\n- High-performance now playing data streams\n- Instant metadata updates across connected clients\n- Cross-tab synchronization for web players\n\n**Sources:** [web/static/openapi.yml:1-1030](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:394-400](), [CHANGELOG.md:407-412]()\n\n## Webhook and Integration System\n\nThe webhook system provides extensive third-party integration capabilities with rate limiting, custom payloads, and support for major platforms.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Webhook Types\"\n        Generic[\"generic\u003cbr/\u003eCustom HTTP Webhooks\"]\n        Discord[\"discord\u003cbr/\u003eDiscord Notifications\"] \n        Mastodon[\"mastodon\u003cbr/\u003eMastodon Posts\"]\n        Bluesky[\"bluesky\u003cbr/\u003eBluesky Posts\"]\n SongChanged[\"song_changed\"]\n        LiveEvents[\"live_connect\u003cbr/\u003elive_disconnect\"]\n        ListenerEvents[\"listener_gained\u003cbr/\u003elistener_lost\"] \n        StationEvents[\"station_online\u003cbr/\u003estation_offline\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Features\"\n        RateLimiting[\"Rate Limiting\"]\n        CustomPayloads[\"Custom Payloads\"]\n        DebugLogs[\"Debug Logging\"]\n    end\n    \n    SongChanged --\u003e Generic\n    LiveEvents --\u003e Discord\n    ListenerEvents --\u003e Analytics\n    StationEvents --\u003e RadioServices\n    \n    RateLimiting --\u003e Generic\n    CustomPayloads --\u003e Generic\n    DebugLogs --\u003e Generic\n```\n\n**Supported Webhook Types:**\n\nThe `WebhookTypes` enum defines supported integration platforms including social media (Discord, Mastodon, Bluesky), analytics services (Google Analytics V4, Matomo), and radio directory services (TuneIn, Radio.de).\n\n**Webhook Triggers:**\n\nEvents that can trigger webhook dispatches are defined in `WebhookTriggers` enum:\n- `song_changed`: New song starts playing\n- `live_connect/live_disconnect`: DJ connection events  \n- `listener_gained/listener_lost`: Audience changes\n- `station_online/station_offline`: Broadcast status changes\n\n**Advanced Features:**\n- Rate limiting to prevent spam to third-party services\n- Custom webhook payload templates\n- Debug logging for webhook delivery tracking\n- Retry mechanisms for failed deliveries\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:13-40](), [CHANGELOG.md:88-89](), [CHANGELOG.md:115-117]()\n\n## Administrative and Monitoring Tools\n\nAzuraCast includes comprehensive administrative tools for system monitoring, user management, audit tracking, and performance analysis.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"User Management\"\n        Users[\"User Accounts\"]\n        Roles[\"Role-based Access Control\"]\n Components:**\n\n| Component | Purpose | API Endpoint |\n|-----------|---------|--------------|\n| `AuditLog` | Track all system changes | `/api/admin/auditlog` |\n| `ServerStats` | Monitor system performance | `/api/admin/server/stats` |\n| `ApiKey` management | Control API access | `/api/admin/api-keys` |\n| Role management | Define user permissions | `/api/admin/roles` |\n\n**Monitoring Capabilities:**\n- Real-time CPU, memory, and network usage tracking\n- Service health monitoring for all system components\n- Comprehensive audit logging of all administrative actions\n- Prometheus metrics endpoint for external monitoring integration\n\n**Permission System:**\nThe system implements granular permissions through `GlobalPermissions` and `StationPermissions` enums, allowing fine-grained access control for both system-wide and station-specific operations.\n\n**Sources:** [backend/src/Entity/AuditLog.php:27-167](), [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/ServerStats/ServerStats.php:14-31](), [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/AuditLogAction.php:38-70](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:126-158]()\n\n## Frontend and For details about specific installation and deployment procedures, see [Installation and Deployment](#2).\n\n## Core Technology Overview\n\nAzuraCast is built as a modern web application using a ORM Configuration\n\nThe application uses Doctrine ORM 3 with MariaDB as the primary database:\n\n| Component | Implementation | Configuration |\n|-----------|---------------|---------------|\n| Database | MariaDB 11.8.2 | [util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh:6]() |\n| ORM | Doctrine ORM 3 | [backend/config/services.php:115-169]() |\n| Platform | `App\\Doctrine\\Platform\\MariaDbPlatform` | [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:9-216]()\n\n## Database and Storage Technologies\n\nAzuraCast uses a multi-tier storage approach combining relational database, caching, and file storage systems.\n\n### Database Configuration\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"MariaDB Configuration\"\n        MariaDB[\"mariadb-server 11.8.2\"] --\u003e Engine[\"InnoDB Storage Engine\"]\n Backend\"]\n        MetadataCache[\"ORM Metadata Cache\"] --\u003e RedisCache\n        SkipNameResolve[\"skip-name-resolve\"] --\u003e MariaDB\n    end\n```\n\nMariaDB is configured with performance Cache**: ORM result and metadata caching\n- **Serializer Cache**: API response serialization caching  \n- **Application Cache**: General application data caching\n- **Session Storage**: User session data persistence\n\nCache configuration automatically selects Redis when available, falling back to filesystem storage:\n\n```php\nif ($redisFactory-\u003eisSupported()) {\n    $cacheInterface = new Symfony\\Component\\Cache\\Adapter\\RedisAdapter(\n        $redisFactory-\u003egetInstance(),\n        marshaller: new Symfony\\Component\\Cache\\Marshaller\\DefaultMarshaller(\n            $environment-\u003eisProduction() ? null : false\n        )\n    );\n}\n```\n\nSources: [backend/config/services.php:174-199](), [backend/config/services.php:213-219]()\n\n### File Storage `league/flysystem-sftp-v3` | Remote filesystem storage |\n| **Dropbox** | `spatie/flysystem-dropbox` | Cloud backup and sync |\n\nThe file storage system uses League Flysystem for abstraction:\n\n- [.github/workflows/default.yml:189-190]() |\n| **PHPUnit** | Unit testing framework | [composer.json:131]() |\n| **Mockery** | PHP mocking library | [composer.json:125]() |\n| **PsySH** | Interactive PHP REPL | [composer.json:132]() |\n| **Whoops** | Development error handler | [composer.json:123]() |\n| **Symfony VarDumper** | Debug output formatting | [composer.json:137]() |\n\nDevelopment environment features:\n\n- **Interactive Console**: PsySH REPL for debugging and exploration\n- **Error Handling**: Whoops provides detailed error pages in development\n- **Database Profiling**: Optional SQL query logging via Doctrine\n- **Memory Profiling**: XHProf integration for performance analysis\n\nThe testing suite covers:\n\n- **API Endpoints**: REST API testing with Codeception\n- **Database Operations**: Doctrine integration testing\n- **CLI Commands**: Console command testing\n- **Web Interface**: Browser-based functional testing\n\nSources: [composer.json:117-137](), [backend/config/services.php:151]()\n\n## Containerization and Deployment\n\nAzuraCast uses Docker for unified deployment across development and production environments, with all services packaged in a single container.\n\n### Docker Container Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Base Images\"\n        PHPBase[\"php:8.4-fpm-bookworm\"] --\u003e BaseInstall[\"Base Package Installation\"]\n        MariaDBImage[\"mariadb:lts-noble\"] --\u003e MariaDBBin[\"MariaDB Binaries\"]\n        IcecastImage[\"ghcr.io/azuracast/icecast-kh-ac\"] --\u003e IcecastBin[\"Icecast-KH Binaries\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Service Installation\"\n        BaseInstall --\u003e SupervisorSetup[\"Supervisor Process Manager\"]\n        BaseInstall --\u003e WebSetup[\"Nginx + PHP-FPM\"]\n        BaseInstall --\u003e StationsSetup[\"Liquidsoap + Audio Tools\"]\n        BaseInstall --\u003e RedisSetup[\"Redis Server\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Application Layer\"\n        ComposerInstall[\"Composer Dependencies\"] --\u003e PHPApp[\"PHP Application\"]\n        NPMBuild[\"NPM Asset Build\"] --\u003e StaticAssets[\"Static Assets\"]\n        TranslationSync[\"Translation Files\"] --\u003e PHPApp\n        OpenAPIGen[\"API Documentation\"] --\u003e StaticAssets\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Runtime Environment\"\n        Supervisor[\"Supervisor init\"] --\u003e MultiService[\"Multi-service Container\"]\n        MultiService --\u003e NginxService[\"Nginx Web Server\"]\n        MultiService --\u003e PHPFPMService[\"PHP-FPM Worker Pool\"]\n        MultiService --\u003e MariaDBService[\"MariaDB Database\"]\n        MultiService --\u003e RedisService[\"Redis Cache\"]\n        MultiService --\u003e LiquidsoapService[\"Liquidsoap Audio Engine\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe Dockerfile implements a multi-stage build process:\n\n1. **Dependencies Stage**: Copies MariaDB, Icecast, and PHP extension installer\n2. **Pre-final Stage**: Installs all system dependencies and services\n3. **Development Stage**: Includes development tools and source mounting\n4. **Final Stage**: Production-optimized with compiled assets\n\nKey container features:\n\n- **Unified Container**: All services run in single container via Supervisor\n- **Multi-platform**: Supports AMD64 and ARM64 architectures\n- **Volume Management**: Persistent data stored in mounted volumes\n- **Port Mapping**: Exposes web (80/443) and streaming (8000-8999) ports\n\nSources: [Dockerfile:1-188](), [Dockerfile:25-84](), [Dockerfile:170-188]()\n\n### Service Configuration and Management\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Process Management\"\n        Supervisor[\"Supervisor\"] --\u003e NginxProc[\"nginx.conf\"]\n        Supervisor --\u003e PHPFPMProc[\"php-fpm.conf\"]\n        Supervisor --\u003e MariaDBProc[\"mysqld.conf\"]\n        Supervisor --\u003e RedisProc[\"redis.conf\"]\n        Supervisor --\u003e LiquidsoapProc[\"liquidsoap.liq\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Configuration Templates\"\n        Dockerize[\"Dockerize Templating\"] --\u003e EnvVars[\"Environment Variables\"]\n        Dockerize --\u003e NginxTemplate[\"nginx.conf.tmpl\"]\n        Dockerize --\u003e PHPTemplate[\"php.ini.tmpl\"]\n        Dockerize --\u003e MariaDBTemplate[\"db.cnf.tmpl\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Volume Mounts\"\n        StationsVolume[\"stations/\"] --\u003e MediaFiles[\"Media Files\"]\n        BackupsVolume[\"backups/\"] --\u003e DatabaseBackups[\"Database Backups\"]\n        StorageVolume[\"storage/\"] --\u003e AppData[\"Application Data\"]\n        MySQLVolume[\"mysql/\"] --\u003e DatabaseFiles[\"Database Files\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Network Configuration\"\n        WebPorts[\"80, 443\"] --\u003e Nginx\n        StreamingPorts[\"8000-8999\"] --\u003e Liquidsoap\n        SFTPPort[\"2022\"] --\u003e SFTPGo\n    end\n```\n\nThe container uses Dockerize for configuration templating:\n\n- **Environment-based Config**: Templates populated from environment variables\n- **Service Discovery**: Automatic configuration based on container environment\n- **Runtime Configuration**: Dynamic generation of service configurations\n\nVolume persistence ensures data safety:\n\n```dockerfile\nVOLUME \"/var/azuracast/stations\"\nVOLUME \"/var/azuracast/backups\"\nVOLUME \"/var/lib/mysql\"\nVOLUME \"/var/azuracast/storage/acme\"\nVOLUME \"/var/azuracast/storage/uploads\"\n```\n\nDefault environment variables provide sensible defaults:\n\n```dockerfile\nENV APPLICATION_ENV=\"production\" \\\n    MYSQL_HOST=\"localhost\" \\\n    MYSQL_PORT=3306 \\\n    ENABLE_REDIS=\"true\" \\\n    REDIS_HOST=\"localhost\" \\\n    NGINX_RADIO_PORTS=\"default\"\n```\n\nSources: [Dockerfile:95-131](), [util/docker/web/setup/php.sh:1-18](), [util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh:1-38]()\n\n### Development vs Production Configuration\n\nThe Docker setup supports both development and production environments with different optimizations:\n\n| Aspect | Development | Production |\n|--------|-------------|------------|\n| **PHP Extensions** | Xdebug enabled | Production optimizations |\n| **Asset Building** | Vite dev server | Pre-compiled assets |\n| **Error Handling** | Whoops error pages | Monolog file logging |\n| **Caching** | Array cache adapter | Redis-backed cache |\n| **Asset Serving** | Vite HMR | Nginx static serving |\n| **Database** | SQLite for testing | MariaDB with persistence |\n\nDevelopment environment features:\n\n```dockerfile\nFROM pre-final AS development\nENV APPLICATION_ENV=\"development\" \\\n    PROFILING_EXTENSION_ENABLED=1 \\\n    ENABLE_WEB_UPDATER=\"false\"\n```\n\nProduction optimizations:\n\n```dockerfile\nRUN composer install --no-dev --no-ansi --no-autoloader --no-interaction \\\n    \u0026\u0026 composer dump-autoload --optimize --classmap-authoritative \\\n    \u0026\u0026 composer clear-cache\n```\n\nThe development setup includes:\n\n- **Source Mounting**: Live code editing without rebuilds\n- **Hot Module Replacement**: Instant frontend updates\n- **Debug Tools**: Whoops, VarDumper, profiling extensions\n- **Testing Framework**: Codeception, PHPUnit, database fixtures\n\nSources: [Dockerfile:135-167](), [Dockerfile:171-188](), The primary focus is on the Docker installation method using the `docker.sh` utility script, which is the recommended and fully supported deployment approach.\n\nFor information about the streaming engine deployment and configuration, see [Streaming Engine](#3). For details about the development environment setup, see [Development Environment Setup](#6.2).\n\n## Deployment Architecture Overview\n\nAzuraCast employs a containerized deployment strategy centered is orchestrated by the `docker.sh` utility script, which provides a comprehensive installation workflow including dependency checking, Docker installation, and AzuraCast setup.\n\n### Installation Requirements and Validation\n\nThe installation begins with system requirement validation through the `check-install-requirements()` function:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    StartInstall[\"install() function\u003cbr/\u003edocker.sh:445\"]\n    CheckReqs[\"check-install-requirements()\u003cbr/\u003edocker.sh:266\"]\n    OSCheck[\"Operating System Check\u003cbr/\u003eLinux/Darwin supported\"]\n    ArchCheck[\"Architecture Check\u003cbr/\u003ex86_64/aarch64 supported\"]\n    CmdCheck[\"Required Commands\u003cbr/\u003ecurl, awk\"]\n    PermCheck[\"Permission Check\u003cbr/\u003eroot or sudo required\"]\n    DirCheck[\"Installation Directory\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast recommended\"]\n    \n    StartInstall --\u003e CheckReqs\n    CheckReqs --\u003e OSCheck\n    OSCheck --\u003e ArchCheck\n    ArchCheck --\u003e CmdCheck\n    CmdCheck --\u003e PermCheck\n    PermCheck --\u003e DirCheck\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:266-346](), [docker.sh:445-509]()\n\n### Docker Engine Installation\n\nThe script handles Docker installation automatically through the `install-docker()` and `install-docker-compose()` functions:\n\n| Component | Installation Method | Function | Location |\n|-----------|-------------------|----------|----------|\n| Docker Engine | get.docker.com script | `install-docker()` | [docker.sh:348-371]() |\n| Docker Compose | GitHub releases | `install-docker-compose()` | [docker.sh:373-408]() |\n| Podman Support | Detection-based | `PODMAN_MODE` variable | [docker.sh:4-25]() |\n\n### AzuraCast Installation Workflow\n\nThe core installation process uses the installer container approach:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    setupRelease[\"setup-release()\u003cbr/\u003eConfigure release channel\"]\n    runInstaller[\"run-installer()\u003cbr/\u003edocker.sh:410\"]\n    downloadConfigs[\"Download Configuration Files\u003cbr/\u003e.env, azuracast.env\u003cbr/\u003edocker-compose.yml\"]\n    installerContainer[\"azuracast_installer container\u003cbr/\u003edocker-compose.installer.yml\"]\n    finalizeInstall[\"Finalize Installation\u003cbr/\u003eSet PUID/PGID\u003cbr/\u003ePull images, start services\"]\n    \n    setupRelease --\u003e runInstaller\n    runInstaller --\u003e downloadConfigs\n    downloadConfigs --\u003e installerContainer\n    installerContainer --\u003e finalizeInstall\n```\n\nThe installer downloads configuration templates from the appropriate release branch:\n\n- **Environment files**: `.env` and `azuracast.env` from sample templates\n- **Docker Compose**: `docker-compose.sample.yml` as the service definition\n- **Installer container**: `docker-compose.installer.yml` for setup orchestration\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:410-439](), [docker.sh:481-508](), [docker-compose.installer.yml:1-11]()\n\n## Update Process\n\nAzuraCast updates are handled through the `update()` function, which provides a comprehensive update workflow including utility script updates, Docker image updates, and database migrations.\n\n### Update Workflow Components\n\nThe update process follows a systematic approach to ensure consistency and safety:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    updateStart[\"update() function\u003cbr/\u003edocker.sh:568\"]\n    backupWarning[\"Backup Warning\u003cbr/\u003eUser confirmation required\"]\n    utilityCheck[\"Docker Utility Script Check\u003cbr/\u003eCompare with remote version\"]\n    dockerCheck[\"Docker Version Check\u003cbr/\u003eMinimum version 20+\"]\n    runUpdater[\"run-installer --update\u003cbr/\u003eConfiguration updates\"]\n    composeCheck[\"Docker Compose Check\u003cbr/\u003eCompare configurations\"]\n    pullImages[\"Pull Updated Images\u003cbr/\u003edc pull command\"]\n    runMigration[\"azuracast_update\u003cbr/\u003eDatabase migrations\"]\n    cleanup[\"Optional Cleanup\u003cbr/\u003edocker system prune\"]\n    \n    updateStart --\u003e backupWarning\n    backupWarning --\u003e utilityCheck\n    utilityCheck --\u003e dockerCheck\n    dockerCheck --\u003e runUpdater\n    runUpdater --\u003e composeCheck\n    composeCheck --\u003e pullImages\n    pullImages --\u003e runMigration\n    runMigration --\u003e cleanup\n```\n\n### Update Safety Mechanisms\n\nThe update process includes several safety mechanisms:\n\n| Safety Feature | Implementation | Purpose |\n|----------------|----------------|---------|\n| Backup Warning | User confirmation prompt | Ensure user has backups |\n| Utility Script Versioning | `cmp --silent docker.sh docker.new.sh` | Keep script current |\n| Docker Version Check | `docker version -f \"{{.Server.Version}}\"` | Ensure compatibility |\n| Configuration Backup | `cp docker-compose.yml docker-compose.backup.yml` | Preserve current config |\n| Graceful Shutdown | `dc down --timeout 60` | Clean service shutdown |\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:568-654](), [docker.sh:576-600](), [docker.sh:602-615]()\n\n### Release Channel Management\n\nRelease channel configuration is managed through the `setup-release()` function:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    getReleaseChannel[\"get-release-channel()\u003cbr/\u003eRead AZURACAST_VERSION\"]\n    channelCheck[\"Channel Type Check\u003cbr/\u003elatest, stable, or specific\"]\n    userPrompt[\"User Prompt\u003cbr/\u003eSwitch channels?\"]\n    updateEnv[\"Update .env file\u003cbr/\u003eAZURACAST_VERSION\"]\n    updateUtility[\"update-self()\u003cbr/\u003eDownload latest docker.sh\"]\n    \n    getReleaseChannel --\u003e channelCheck\n    channelCheck --\u003e userPrompt\n    userPrompt --\u003e updateEnv\n    updateEnv --\u003e updateUtility\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:142-264](), [docker.sh:660-672]()\n\n## Environment Configuration\n\nAzuraCast's configuration system uses environment files and templated configurations to manage deployment settings across different environments.\n\n### Configuration File Structure\n\n| File | Purpose | Template Source | Content |\n|------|---------|----------------|---------|\n| `.env` | Docker Compose variables | `sample.env` | Container settings, ports, versions |\n| `azuracast.env` | Application configuration | `azuracast.sample.env` | Database, Redis, app settings |\n| `docker-compose.yml` | Service definitions | `docker-compose.sample.yml` | Container orchestration |\n\n### Environment Variable Management\n\nThe `docker.sh` script includes a comprehensive `.env` file management system:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    envFile[\".env file management\u003cbr/\u003eFunctions: .env.get, .env.set\"]\n    envGet[\".env.get()\u003cbr/\u003eExtract variable values\u003cbr/\u003eBASH_REMATCH regex\"]\n    envSet[\".env.set()\u003cbr/\u003eUpdate variable values\u003cbr/\u003ePreserve file structure\"]\n    envParse[\".env.parse()\u003cbr/\u003eProcess environment entries\u003cbr/\u003eFilter by keys\"]\n    envExport[\".env.export()\u003cbr/\u003eExport to shell environment\"]\n    \n    envFile --\u003e envGet\n    envFile --\u003e envSet\n    envFile --\u003e envParse\n    envFile --\u003e envExport\n```\n\n### Port Configuration\n\nPort configuration is handled through the `setup-ports()` function:\n\n| Variable | Default | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT` | 80 | HTTP web interface |\n| `AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT` | 443 | HTTPS web interface |\n| `AZURACAST_SFTP_PORT` | 2022 | SFTP file management |\n| Broadcasting ports | 8000-8496 | Station streaming endpoints |\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:27-135](), [docker.sh:220-224](), [docker-compose.sample.yml:22-173]()\n\n### User Permission Configuration\n\nThe installation automatically configures user permissions for non-root installations:\n\n```bash\n# From docker.sh install() function\nif [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then\n  .env --file .env set AZURACAST_PUID=\"$(id -u)\"\n  .env --file .env set AZURACAST_PGID=\"$(id -g)\"\nfi\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:494-498](), [docker.sh:548-552]()\n\n## Build and CI/CD Pipeline\n\nAzuraCast employs automated build and deployment processes through GitHub Actions and container registries.\n\n### Container Build and Registry Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Source Control\"\n        mainBranch[\"main branch\u003cbr/\u003eRolling releases\"]\n        stableBranch[\"stable branch\u003cbr/\u003eStable releases\"]\n        devBranch[\"development branch\u003cbr/\u003eDevelopment builds\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"GitHub Actions\"\n        buildAction[\"Build, Test and Publish\u003cbr/\u003eMulti-platform builds\"]\n        testSuite[\"PHP Testing\u003cbr/\u003eNode.js Building\"]\n        depotBuild[\"Depot Integration\u003cbr/\u003edepot.json\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Container Registry\"\n        latestTag[\"ghcr.io/azuracast/azuracast:latest\u003cbr/\u003eRolling updaterImage\n    \n    latestTag --\u003e productionInstall\n    stableTag --\u003e productionInstall\n    devTag --\u003e devInstall\n    devTag --\u003e gitpodInstall\n```\n\n### Development Development | `docker-compose.dev.yml` | `docker.sh install-dev` |\n| Gitpod | `gitpod.yml` | Automated setup script |\n| Standard Build | `Makefile` | Make-based commands |\n\n### Update
