[util/setup_gitpod.sh](util/setup_gitpod.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides detailed instructions for installing AzuraCast using Docker, which is the recommended and officially supported installation method. Docker allows AzuraCast to run in isolated containers with all dependencies pre-configured, making the installation process simpler and more consistent across different environments.\n\nFor standalone installation details, see [Installation and Deployment](#2).\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nBefore installing AzuraCast, ensure your system meets the following requirements:\n\n| Requirement | Specification |\n|-------------|---------------|\n| Operating System | Linux (Debian, Ubuntu, CentOS, etc.) |\n| Architecture | x86_64 (64-bit) or aarch64 (ARM64) |\n| Memory | Minimum 2GB RAM |\n| Storage | Minimum 10GB free space (more for media storage) |\n| Required Software | `curl`, `awk` |\n\n### System Validation\n\nThe installation script automatically checks your system for compatibility. These checks include:\n\n- Operating system (Linux required)\n- Architecture (x86_64 or aarch64 supported)\n- Required commands (curl, awk)\n- User permissions\n\n```bash\n# Check requirements before installation\n./docker.sh check-install-requirements\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:266-344]()\n\n## Installation Process\n\n### Getting Started\n\n1. Download the Docker installation script:\n\n```bash\nmkdir -p /var/azuracast\ncd /var/azuracast\ncurl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AzuraCast/AzuraCast/main/docker.sh \u003e docker.sh\nchmod a+x docker.sh\n```\n\n2. Run the installation command:\n\n```bash\n./docker.sh install\n```\n\nThis script will:\n- Check system requirements\n- Install Docker and Docker Compose if necessary\n- Configure ports and release channels\n- Launch the AzuraCast installer\n- Create and start the Docker containers\n\nSources: [docker.sh:429-493]()\n\n### Installation Flow\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"Start Installation\"] --\u003e CheckReq[\"Check Requirements\"]\n    CheckReq --\u003e InstallDocker[\"Install Docker/Docker Compose\\n(if needed)\"]\n    InstallDocker --\u003e SetupRelease[\"Configure Release Channel\\n(latest or stable)\"]\n    SetupRelease --\u003e RunInstaller[\"Run AzuraCast Installer Container\"]\n    RunInstaller --\u003e MoveConfig[\"Generate Docker Compose File\"]\n    MoveConfig --\u003e SetPermissions[\"Set User Permissions\"]\n    SetPermissions --\u003e PullImages[\"Pull Docker Images\"]\n    PullImages --\u003e RunInstall[\"Run Installation Process\"]\n    RunInstall --\u003e StartServices[\"Start AzuraCast Services\"]\n    StartServices --\u003e Finish[\"Installation Complete\"]\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:429-493](), [docker-compose.installer.yml:1-11]()\n\n## Docker Architecture\n\nAzuraCast runs using multiple interconnected services within Docker containers. The main container architecture includes:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Docker Containers\"\n        Web[\"Web Container\\n(azuracast)\"]\n        Updater[\"Updater Container\\n(azuracast_updater)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Web Container Services\"\n        Nginx[\"Nginx Web Server\"]\n        PhpFpm[\"PHP-FPM\"]\n        Liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap/AutoDJ\"]\n        Icecast[\"Icecast Streaming Server\"]\n        MariaDB[\"MariaDB Database\"]\n        Redis[\"Redis Cache/Queue\"]\n        Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo Websocket\"]\n        SftpGo[\"SFTPGo Server\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persistent Volumes\"\n        StationData[\"station_data\\n(Stations media and configs)\"]\n        Database[\"db_data\\n(Database files)\"]\n        Uploads[\"www_uploads\\n(User uploads)\"]\n        Backups[\"backups\\n(System backups)\"]\n        GeoLite[\"geolite_install\\n(GeoIP database)\"]\n        Acme[\"acme\\n(SSL certificates)\"]\n    end\n    \n    Web --\u003e Nginx\n    Web --\u003e PhpFpm\n    Web --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    Web --\u003e Icecast\n    Web --\u003e MariaDB\n    Web --\u003e Redis\n    Web --\u003e Centrifugo\n    Web --\u003e SftpGo\n    \n    Web --\u003e StationData\n    Web --\u003e Database\n    Web --\u003e Uploads\n    Web --\u003e Backups\n    Web --\u003e GeoLite\n    Web --\u003e Acme\n    \n    Updater --\u003e Web\n```\n\nSources: [docker-compose.sample.yml:15-196](), [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl:1-206](), [util/docker/stations/setup/liquidsoap.sh:1-46](), [util/docker/stations/setup/icecast.sh:1-8](), [util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh:1-38](), [util/docker/redis/setup/redis.sh:1-12]()\n\n### All-in-One Container\n\nAzuraCast uses an all-in-one container architecture where multiple services run inside the main `azuracast` container, managed by Supervisor. This approach simplifies deployment while maintaining proper service isolation.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Docker Container (azuracast)\"\n        Supervisor[\"Supervisor\\n(Process Manager)\"]\n        \n        subgraph \"Web Services\"\n            Nginx[\"Nginx\\n(Web Server)\"]\n            PhpFpm[\"PHP-FPM\\n(Web Application)\"]\n            PhpWorker[\"PHP Worker\\n(Background Tasks)\"]\n            PhpNowplaying[\"PHP Nowplaying\\n(Real-time Updates)\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Database \u0026 Cache\"\n            MariaDB[\"MariaDB\\n(Database)\"]\n            Redis[\"Redis\\n(Cache \u0026 Queue)\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Streaming Services\"\n            Icecast[\"Icecast\\n(Streaming Server)\"]\n            Liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap\\n(Audio Processing)\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Supporting Services\"\n            Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo\\n(WebSockets)\"]\n            SftpGo[\"SFTPGo\\n(SFTP Server)\"]\n            Cron[\"Cron\\n(Scheduled Tasks)\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    Supervisor --\u003e Web Services\n    Supervisor --\u003e \"Database \u0026 Cache\"\n    Supervisor --\u003e \"Streaming Services\"\n    Supervisor --\u003e \"Supporting Services\"\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/web/service.full/nginx.conf:1-18](), [util/docker/web/service.full/php-fpm.conf:1-15](), [util/docker/web/service.full/php-worker.conf:1-16](), [util/docker/web/service.full/php-nowplaying.conf:1-16](), [util/docker/mariadb/service.minimal/mariadb.conf:1-16](), [util/docker/redis/service.minimal/redis.conf:1-16](), [util/docker/web/service.full/sftpgo.conf:1-20](), [util/docker/web/service.full/cron.conf:1-15]()\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Port Configuration\n\nAzuraCast uses several ports for different services. The main ports can be configured during installation:\n\n```bash\n# Configure ports after installation\n./docker.sh change-ports\n```\n\n| Service | Default Port | Environment Variable |\n|---------|--------------|----------------------|\n| HTTP    | 80           | AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT  |\n| HTTPS   | 443          | AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT |\n| SFTP    | 2022         | AZURACAST_SFTP_PORT  |\n| Stations | 8000-8499   | (Pre-configured range) |\n\nStation broadcasting ports are pre-configured in the range of 8000-8499 for Icecast and SHOUTcast streams.\n\nSources: [docker.sh:218-224](), [docker-compose.sample.yml:23-193](), [sample.env:1-11]()\n\n### Release Channels\n\nAzuraCast offers different release channels that determine which version of the software you'll receive:\n\n```bash\n# Configure release channel\n./docker.sh setup-release\n```\n\n| Channel | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| latest  | Rolling releases with latest features (default) |\n| stable  | Stable releases with thorough testing |\n\nSources: [docker.sh:229-264]()\n\n### Environment Files\n\nTwo main environment files control the Docker configuration:\n\n1. `.env` - Contains Docker-specific settings (ports, user permissions)\n2. `azuracast.env` - Contains AzuraCast application settings\n\nThe installation process will create these files automatically, but they can be modified as needed.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    DockerScript[\"docker.sh\\n(Installation Script)\"] --\u003e EnvFiles[\"Environment Files\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"EnvFiles\"\n        MainEnv[\".env\\n(Docker Settings)\"]\n        AzuraEnv[\"azuracast.env\\n(AzuraCast Settings)\"]\n    end\n    \n    MainEnv --\u003e DockerCompose[\"docker-compose.yml\\n(Service Configuration)\"]\n    AzuraEnv --\u003e WebContainer[\"Web Container\\n(Application Configuration)\"]\n    \n    DockerCompose --\u003e Containers[\"Docker Containers\"]\n    Containers --\u003e WebContainer\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:27-135](), [sample.env:1-11]()\n\n## Managing Your AzuraCast Installation\n\nThe `docker.sh` script provides various commands for managing your AzuraCast installation:\n\n### Common Commands\n\n| Command | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| `./docker.sh install` | Install AzuraCast |\n| `./docker.sh update` | Update AzuraCast to the latest version |\n| `./docker.sh restart` | Restart all AzuraCast containers |\n| `./docker.sh backup [path]` | Back up AzuraCast data to a file |\n| `./docker.sh restore [path]` | Restore AzuraCast from a backup |\n| `./docker.sh cli [command]` | Run an AzuraCast CLI command |\n| `./docker.sh uninstall` | Remove AzuraCast (including data) |\n\nSources: [docker.sh:552-638](), [docker.sh:685-712](), [docker.sh:714-779](), [docker.sh:822-836]()\n\n## Audio Processing Architecture\n\nAzuraCast uses Liquidsoap for audio processing and stream output. This architecture handles media playback, live DJ connections, and streaming to listeners:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Media Sources\"\n        Media[\"Media Files\"]\n        Playlists[\"Playlists\"]\n        LiveDJ[\"Live DJ Connections\"]\n        Requests[\"Song Requests\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Audio Processing\"\n        AutoDJ[\"Liquidsoap AutoDJ\"]\n        CrossfadeEngine[\"Crossfade Engine\"]\n        AudioEffects[\"Audio Effects\\n(Normalization, Compression)\"]\n        StereoTool[\"Stereo Tool\\n(Optional)\"]\n        MasterMe[\"Master Me\\n(Optional)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Stream Output\"\n        Icecast[\"Icecast\"]\n        SHOUTcast[\"SHOUTcast\"]\n        HLS[\"HLS Streaming\"]\n    end\n    \n    Media --\u003e Playlists\n    Playlists --\u003e AutoDJ\n    LiveDJ --\u003e AutoDJ\n    Requests --\u003e AutoDJ\n    \n    AutoDJ --\u003e CrossfadeEngine\n    CrossfadeEngine --\u003e AudioEffects\n    AudioEffects --\u003e StereoTool\n    AudioEffects --\u003e MasterMe\n    StereoTool --\u003e Icecast\n    MasterMe --\u003e Icecast\n    AudioEffects --\u003e Icecast\n    \n    Icecast --\u003e Listeners[\"Listeners\"]\n    SHOUTcast --\u003e Listeners\n    HLS --\u003e Listeners\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:1-883](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:1-709](), [backend/src/Entity/StationMediaMetadata.php:1-107](), [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:1-240]()\n\n## Advanced Configuration\n\n### Custom Docker Compose Overrides\n\nFor advanced users, you can create a custom `docker-compose.override.yml` file to modify the Docker setup:\n\n```bash\n# Example: Create override file for development\ncp docker-compose.dev.yml docker-compose.override.yml\n```\n\nAny settings in this file will override the defaults in `docker-compose.yml`.\n\nSources: [docker-compose.sample.yml:8-11](), [docker-compose.dev.yml:1-25]()\n\n### Custom Liquidsoap Configuration\n\nAzuraCast allows advanced audio configuration through Liquidsoap:\n\n1. Audio processing options:\n   - Built-in normalization and compression\n   - MasterMe audio processor integration\n   - StereoTool integration\n\n2. Crossfade settings:\n   - Smart crossfade based on track analysis\n   - Standard crossfade with customizable duration\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:98-206](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:538-602]()\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Installation Issues\n\nIf you encounter issues during installation:\n\n1. Check system requirements\n2. Ensure Docker and Docker Compose are properly installed\n3. Check file permissions for the AzuraCast directory\n4. Review installation logs for specific errors\n\n### Common Docker Commands\n\n```bash\n# View running containers\ndocker ps\n\n# View container logs\ndocker logs azuracast\n\n# Restart all services\n./docker.sh restart\n\n# Update Docker utility script\n./docker.sh update-self\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:639-656]()\n\n### Docker Update Process\n\nThe update process involves:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"Start Update\"] --\u003e CheckScript[\"Check for Docker Script Updates\"]\n    CheckScript --\u003e|\"No Updates\"| CheckDocker[\"Check Docker Version\"]\n    CheckScript --\u003e|\"Updates Available\"| UpdateScript[\"Update Docker Script\"]\n    UpdateScript --\u003e Restart[\"Restart Update Process\"]\n    \n    CheckDocker --\u003e RunInstaller[\"Run Update Installer\"]\n    RunInstaller --\u003e CheckCompose[\"Check for Docker Compose Updates\"]\n    CheckCompose --\u003e|\"No Updates\"| PullImages[\"Pull Latest Images\"]\n    CheckCompose --\u003e|\"Updates Available\"| UpdateCompose[\"Update Compose File\"]\n    \n    UpdateCompose --\u003e DownContainers[\"Shut Down Containers\"]\n    PullImages --\u003e DownContainers\n    \n    DownContainers --\u003e RunUpdate[\"Run Update Process\"]\n    RunUpdate --\u003e StartServices[\"Start AzuraCast Services\"]\n    StartServices --\u003e CleanupImages[\"Clean Up Old Images (Optional)\"]\n    CleanupImages --\u003e Finish[\"Update Complete\"]\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:552-638]()\n\n## Conclusion\n\nDocker installation is the recommended method for deploying AzuraCast. It simplifies the installation process, provides consistency across different environments, and makes updates more reliable.\n\nFor additional configuration options and customization, see [Environment Configuration](#2.3)."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1d:T2b2e,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Update Process\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.gitpod.yml](.gitpod.yml)\n- [Makefile](Makefile)\n- [depot.json](depot.json)\n- [docker-compose.dev.yml](docker-compose.dev.yml)\n- [docker-compose.installer.yml](docker-compose.installer.yml)\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml](docker-compose.sample.yml)\n- [docker.sh](docker.sh)\n- [update.sh](update.sh)\n- [util/setup_gitpod.sh](util/setup_gitpod.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document explains how to update an existing AzuraCast installation. It covers both manual Docker-based updates and using the web updater interface. For initial installation instructions, see [Docker Installation](#2.1).\n\n## Overview of the Update Process\n\nThe AzuraCast update process handles several components:\n- Docker script updates\n- Docker image updates\n- Application code updates\n- Database schema migrations\n- Configuration file updates\n- Service restarts\n\n### Update Process Diagram\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"Start Update Process\"] --\u003e CheckBackup[\"Check/Create Backup\"]\n    CheckBackup --\u003e CheckDockerScript[\"Check for Docker Script Updates\"]\n    CheckDockerScript --\u003e|\"Update available\"| UpdateScript[\"Update Docker Script\"]\n    UpdateScript --\u003e RestartProcess[\"Restart Update Process\"]\n    \n    CheckDockerScript --\u003e|\"No update needed\"| CheckDockerVersion[\"Check Docker Version\"]\n    CheckDockerVersion --\u003e|\"Update needed\"| UpdateDocker[\"Update Docker\"]\n    CheckDockerVersion --\u003e|\"Version OK\"| PullUpdates[\"Pull Updates via Installer\"]\n    UpdateDocker --\u003e PullUpdates\n    \n    PullUpdates --\u003e CheckComposeConfig[\"Check Docker Compose Config\"]\n    CheckComposeConfig --\u003e|\"Config changed\"| UpdateComposeConfig[\"Update docker-compose.yml\"]\n    CheckComposeConfig --\u003e|\"Config unchanged\"| PullImages[\"Pull Latest Docker Images\"]\n    UpdateComposeConfig --\u003e StopContainers[\"Stop Containers\"]\n    \n    PullImages --\u003e StopContainers\n    StopContainers --\u003e RunUpdate[\"Run azuracast_update Inside Container\"]\n    RunUpdate --\u003e StartContainers[\"Start Containers\"]\n    StartContainers --\u003e Cleanup[\"Clean up Docker Resources (Optional)\"]\n    Cleanup --\u003e End[\"Update Complete\"]\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:564-654]()\n\n## Update Methods\n\nAzuraCast provides two primary methods for updating your installation:\n\n### 1. Docker Utility Script Method\n\nThe most versatile method is using the Docker Utility Script (`docker.sh`):\n\n```bash\ncd /var/azuracast\n./docker.sh update\n```\n\nThe script handles the entire update process, including checking for updates to itself, the Docker version, and Docker Compose configuration.\n\nSources: [docker.sh:564-654]()\n\n### 2. Web Updater\n\nSince version 0.17.7, AzuraCast includes a web updater component, based on Watchtower. The web updater runs as a separate container (`azuracast_updater`) and allows you to update your installation directly from the web interface.\n\nThe web updater is configured in the Docker Compose file and has access to the Docker socket to manage containers:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    WebUI[\"AzuraCast Web Interface\"] --\u003e WebUpdater[\"Web Updater\\n(Watchtower)\"]\n    WebUpdater --\u003e|\"Accesses\"| DockerSocket[\"Docker Socket\\n/var/run/docker.sock\"]\n    DockerSocket --\u003e|\"Controls\"| AzuraCastContainer[\"AzuraCast Container\"]\n```\n\nSources: [docker-compose.sample.yml:198-208]()\n\n## Release Channels\n\nAzuraCast offers different release channels to suit various needs:\n\n### Release Channel Structure\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Development[\"Development\"] --\u003e GitHub[\"GitHub Repository\"]\n    GitHub --\u003e|\"Main branch\"| Latest[\"Latest (Rolling Release)\"]\n    GitHub --\u003e|\"Stable branch\"| Stable[\"Stable Release\"]\n    \n    Latest --\u003e|\"Regular updates\"| RollingUsers[\"Rolling Release Users\"]\n    Stable --\u003e|\"Less frequent, more tested updates\"| StableUsers[\"Stable Release Users\"]\n    \n    SpecificVersion[\"Specific Version\\n(e.g., 0.20.0)\"] --\u003e LockUsers[\"Version-Locked Users\"]\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:142-159](), [CHANGELOG.md:1-9]()\n\n### Switching Release Channels\n\nTo switch between release channels:\n\n```bash\ncd /var/azuracast\n./docker.sh setup-release\n```\n\nThis interactive command allows you to select between:\n- `latest` (Rolling Release): Most recent updates and features\n- `stable`: Less frequent but more stable updates\n- Specific version (e.g., `0.19.0`): Locks to a particular version\n\nSources: [docker.sh:229-264]()\n\n## Update Process Details\n\nLet's examine what happens during the update process in detail:\n\n### 1. Initial Checks\n\nThe update begins with safety checks and backups:\n- Prompts user to confirm they have a recent backup\n- Checks for Docker utility script updates\n- Verifies Docker version (minimum version 20)\n\n### 2. Component Updates\n\nAfter initial checks, the update proceeds through several phases:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Phase 1: Environment Setup\"\n        UtilityScript[\"Update Docker Utility Script\"]\n        DockerVersion[\"Check Docker Version\"]\n        RunInstaller[\"Run Installer with --update Flag\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Phase 2: Container Management\"\n        ComposeUpdate[\"Update docker-compose.yml\"]\n        PullImages[\"Pull Latest Docker Images\"]\n        StopContainers[\"Stop Running Containers\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Phase 3: Application Update\"\n        RunUpdateCommand[\"Run azuracast_update Command\"]\n        UpdateDB[\"Update Database Schema\"]\n        UpdateFiles[\"Update Application Files\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Phase 4: Completion\"\n        StartContainers[\"Start Updated Containers\"]\n        CleanupDocker[\"Clean Up Docker Resources\"]\n    end\n    \n    Phase1[\"Phase 1\"] --\u003e Phase2[\"Phase 2\"]\n    Phase2 --\u003e Phase3[\"Phase 3\"]\n    Phase3 --\u003e Phase4[\"Phase 4\"]\n```\n\nSources: [docker.sh:564-654]()\n\n### Key Update Steps\n\n1. **Check for Docker Script Updates**: \n   The script downloads the latest version of itself from GitHub and compares it to the current version.\n\n2. **Docker Compose Configuration Update**:\n   The script compares your `docker-compose.yml` with the latest version and updates if necessary.\n\n3. **Container Updates**:\n   The Docker images are pulled, containers are stopped, and the update command is run.\n\n4. **Application Update**:\n   Inside the container, the `azuracast_update` command handles database migrations and file updates.\n\n5. **Restarting Services**:\n   After updates are applied, all services are restarted with the new configuration.\n\nSources: [docker.sh:564-654](), [docker-compose.sample.yml:1-220]()\n\n## Rolling Back Updates\n\nIf you encounter issues after updating, you can roll back to a previous stable version:\n\n```bash\ncd /var/azuracast\n./docker.sh rollback VERSION\n```\n\nReplace `VERSION` with the specific version (e.g., `0.19.0`).\n\nThe rollback process:\n1. Sets the specified version in environment variables\n2. Stops current containers\n3. Pulls Docker images for the target version\n4. Runs the update command for the older version\n5. Starts containers with the rolled-back version\n\nSources: [docker.sh:856-889]()\n\n## Technical Implementation\n\nLet's examine how the update process is implemented in the codebase:\n\n### Update Process Code Map\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    UpdateFunction[\"update() function\\ndocker.sh:564-654\"] --\u003e CheckScript[\"Check Utility Script\\ndocker.sh:573-600\"]\n    UpdateFunction --\u003e DockerVersionCheck[\"Docker Version Check\\ndocker.sh:603-615\"]\n    UpdateFunction --\u003e RunInstaller[\"run-installer() function\\ndocker.sh:617\"]\n    \n    RunInstaller --\u003e InstallerYml[\"docker-compose.installer.yml\\nCreates azuracast_installer container\"]\n    \n    UpdateFunction --\u003e ComposeFilesCheck[\"Check Compose Files\\ndocker.sh:621-642\"]\n    UpdateFunction --\u003e DockerPull[\"dc pull\\nPull Docker images\\ndocker.sh:640\"]\n    UpdateFunction --\u003e DockerDown[\"dc down\\nStop containers\\ndocker.sh:641\"]\n    \n    UpdateFunction --\u003e RunAzuracastUpdate[\"dc run web azuracast_update\\ndocker.sh:644\"]\n    UpdateFunction --\u003e DockerUp[\"dc up -d\\nStart containers\\ndocker.sh:645\"]\n    UpdateFunction --\u003e DockerCleanup[\"d system prune -f\\nCleanup\\ndocker.sh:648\"]\n```\n\nThe update process interacts with several components:\n\n1. **Docker Scripting**:\n   - The `dc()` function is a wrapper for Docker Compose\n   - The `d()` function is a wrapper for Docker commands\n   - These functions handle Podman compatibility automatically\n\n2. **Environment File Management**:\n   - The `.env` function provides utilities for managing environment variables\n   - The `envfile-set()` function handles setting values in the `.env` file\n\n3. **Release Channel Detection**:\n   - The `get-release-channel()` function checks the current release channel\n   - The `get-release-branch-name()` function maps channels to GitHub branches\n\nSources: [docker.sh:7-25](), [docker.sh:28-135](), [docker.sh:142-159]()\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### Before Updating\n\n1. **Always backup your installation**:\n   ```bash\n   cd /var/azuracast\n   ./docker.sh backup /path/to/backup/location/backup_name.zip\n   ```\n\n2. **Check the changelog** for breaking changes before updating:\n   The changelog is available in the [CHANGELOG.md]() file and on the AzuraCast website.\n\n3. **Plan updates during low-traffic periods** to minimize disruption.\n\n### During Update\n\n1. **Monitor the update process** for any errors.\n2. **Do not interrupt** the update process once it's started.\n\n### After Update\n\n1. **Verify functionality** of your stations and critical features.\n2. **Check logs** for any errors or warnings.\n3. **Clean up Docker resources** to free up disk space, especially if you update frequently.\n\nSources: [CHANGELOG.md:1-873]()\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Common Update Issues\n\n| Issue | Possible Cause | Solution |\n|-------|---------------|----------|\n| Docker script fails to update | Network connectivity or permission issues | Manually download the script or check your internet connection |\n| Docker images fail to pull | Network issues or Docker Hub rate limiting | Try again later or authenticate with Docker Hub |\n| Database migration errors | Schema changes that conflict with customizations | Restore from backup and check logs for specific errors |\n| Containers fail to start | Port conflicts or configuration issues | Check logs with `./docker.sh logs` and resolve conflicts |\n| Web updater not appearing | Missing updater container | Ensure the updater container is in your docker-compose.yml |\n\n### Port Configuration After Updates\n\nIf you need to change ports after an update, use:\n\n```bash\ncd /var/azuracast\n./docker.sh change-ports\n```\n\nThis command provides an interactive way to update the HTTP, HTTPS, and SFTP ports.\n\nSources: [docker.sh:909-914]()\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe AzuraCast update process is designed to be straightforward while ensuring that your installation remains stable and secure. By following the recommended practices and understanding the update process, you can keep your AzuraCast installation up-to-date with the latest features and fixes.\n\nRemember to always back up your installation before updating, and choose the appropriate release channel based on your stability requirements."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1e:T3084,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Environment Configuration\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [azuracast.dev.env](azuracast.dev.env)\n- [azuracast.sample.env](azuracast.sample.env)\n- [backend/src/Installer/EnvFiles/EnvFile.php](backend/src/Installer/EnvFiles/EnvFile.php)\n- [dev.env](dev.env)\n- [sample.env](sample.env)\n- [util/docker/common/chown_dirs.sh](util/docker/common/chown_dirs.sh)\n- [util/docker/mariadb/mariadb/db.cnf.tmpl](util/docker/mariadb/mariadb/db.cnf.tmpl)\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl](util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl)\n- [util/docker/web/nginx/nginx.conf.tmpl](util/docker/web/nginx/nginx.conf.tmpl)\n- [util/docker/web/php/php.ini.tmpl](util/docker/web/php/php.ini.tmpl)\n- [util/docker/web/php/www.conf.tmpl](util/docker/web/php/www.conf.tmpl)\n- [util/docker/web/setup/nginx.sh](util/docker/web/setup/nginx.sh)\n- [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/00_setup_user.sh](util/docker/web/startup_scripts/00_setup_user.sh)\n- [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh](util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh)\n- [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/07_sftpgo_conf.sh](util/docker/web/startup_scripts/07_sftpgo_conf.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers AzuraCast's comprehensive environment configuration system, including environment variable management, template processing, and service-specific configuration generation. The system uses a combination of environment files, dockerize templating, and startup scripts to dynamically configure all services within the Docker container.\n\nFor installation procedures, see [Docker Installation](#2.1). For update-related configuration changes, see [Update Process](#2.2).\n\n## Configuration Management Overview\n\nAzuraCast employs a multi-layered configuration approach that transforms environment variables into service-specific configuration files at container startup. The system supports both development and production environments with appropriate defaults and overrides.\n\n### Environment Variable Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    EnvFiles[\"Environment Files\u003cbr/\u003e.env, azuracast.env\"]\n    DockerCompose[\"docker-compose.yml\u003cbr/\u003eEnvironment Injection\"]\n    Container[\"Docker Container\u003cbr/\u003eRuntime Environment\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Template Processing\"\n        Dockerize[\"dockerize tool\u003cbr/\u003eTemplate Engine\"]\n        Templates[\"Configuration Templates\u003cbr/\u003e.tmpl files\"]\n        Generated[\"Generated Config Files\u003cbr/\u003eService-specific\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Services\"\n        Nginx[\"nginx\u003cbr/\u003e/etc/nginx/sites-available/\"]\n        PHP[\"php-fpm\u003cbr/\u003e/etc/php/\"]\n        MariaDB[\"mariadb\u003cbr/\u003e/etc/mysql/\"]\n        SFTPGo[\"sftpgo\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast/sftpgo/\"]\n    end\n    \n    EnvFiles --\u003e DockerCompose\n    DockerCompose --\u003e Container\n    Container --\u003e Dockerize\n    Templates --\u003e Dockerize\n    Dockerize --\u003e Generated\n    Generated --\u003e Nginx\n    Generated --\u003e PHP\n    Generated --\u003e MariaDB\n    Generated --\u003e SFTPGo\n```\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh:27-31](), [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/07_sftpgo_conf.sh:15]()\n\n## Environment Files Structure\n\nThe configuration system uses multiple environment files with a clear hierarchy and inheritance model.\n\n### Core Environment Files\n\n| File | Purpose | Scope |\n|------|---------|--------|\n| `.env` | Docker Compose configuration | Container orchestration |\n| `azuracast.env` | AzuraCast application settings | Application behavior |\n| `azuracast.dev.env` | Development overrides | Development environment |\n| `sample.env` | Production Docker template | Production baseline |\n| `dev.env` | Development Docker template | Development baseline |\n\n### Environment Variable Categories\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Application Configuration\"\n        APPLICATION_ENV[\"APPLICATION_ENV\u003cbr/\u003eproduction/development/testing\"]\n        LOG_LEVEL[\"LOG_LEVEL\u003cbr/\u003edebug/info/notice/warning/error\"]\n        COMPOSER_PLUGIN_MODE[\"COMPOSER_PLUGIN_MODE\u003cbr/\u003etrue/false\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Database Configuration\"\n        MYSQL_HOST[\"MYSQL_HOST\u003cbr/\u003eDatabase host\"]\n        MYSQL_PASSWORD[\"MYSQL_PASSWORD\u003cbr/\u003eDatabase password\"]\n        MYSQL_SLOW_QUERY_LOG[\"MYSQL_SLOW_QUERY_LOG\u003cbr/\u003eQuery logging\"]\n        MYSQL_MAX_CONNECTIONS[\"MYSQL_MAX_CONNECTIONS\u003cbr/\u003eConnection limit\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Web Server Configuration\"\n        NGINX_TIMEOUT[\"NGINX_TIMEOUT\u003cbr/\u003eRequest timeout\"]\n        NGINX_CLIENT_MAX_BODY_SIZE[\"NGINX_CLIENT_MAX_BODY_SIZE\u003cbr/\u003eUpload limit\"]\n        PHP_MAX_FILE_SIZE[\"PHP_MAX_FILE_SIZE\u003cbr/\u003eFile upload size\"]\n        PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT[\"PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT\u003cbr/\u003eMemory allocation\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Network Configuration\"\n        AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT[\"AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT\u003cbr/\u003eHTTP port (80)\"]\n        AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT[\"AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT\u003cbr/\u003eHTTPS port (443)\"]\n        AZURACAST_SFTP_PORT[\"AZURACAST_SFTP_PORT\u003cbr/\u003eSFTP port (2022)\"]\n        AUTO_ASSIGN_PORT_MIN[\"AUTO_ASSIGN_PORT_MIN\u003cbr/\u003eStation port range start\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [azuracast.sample.env:6-7](), [azuracast.sample.env:49-86](), [azuracast.sample.env:125-153](), [sample.env:3-11]()\n\n### Environment Variable Validation\n\nThe `EnvFile` class defines configuration schemas with validation rules and default values:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    EnvFileClass[\"App\\Installer\\EnvFiles\\EnvFile\"]\n    ConfigArray[\"getConfiguration()\u003cbr/\u003eValidation Schema\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Configuration Properties\"\n        Required[\"required: true/false\"]\n        Default[\"default: value\"]\n        Options[\"options: [array]\"]\n        Description[\"description: text\"]\n    end\n    \n    EnvFileClass --\u003e ConfigArray\n    ConfigArray --\u003e Required\n    ConfigArray --\u003e Default\n    ConfigArray --\u003e Options\n    ConfigArray --\u003e Description\n```\n\n**Sources:** [backend/src/Installer/EnvFiles/EnvFile.php:15-95]()\n\n## Template Processing System\n\nAzuraCast uses the `dockerize` tool to process Go templates and generate service-specific configuration files from environment variables.\n\n### Template Processing Workflow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Startup as \"Container Startup\"\n    participant Script as \"Startup Scripts\"\n    participant Dockerize as \"dockerize Tool\"\n    participant Template as \"Template Files\"\n    participant Config as \"Generated Configs\"\n    participant Service as \"Service Process\"\n    \n    Startup-\u003e\u003eScript: Execute startup scripts\n    Script-\u003e\u003eDockerize: Process templates\n    Dockerize-\u003e\u003eTemplate: Read .tmpl files\n    Template-\u003e\u003eDockerize: Template content\n    Dockerize-\u003e\u003eConfig: Generate config files\n    Config-\u003e\u003eService: Service reads config\n    Service-\u003e\u003eService: Start with configuration\n```\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh:28-29]()\n\n### Template Syntax and Functions\n\nThe templates use Go template syntax with dockerize-specific functions:\n\n| Function | Purpose | Example |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `default .Env.VAR \"value\"` | Provide default value | `{{ default .Env.NGINX_TIMEOUT \"1800\" }}` |\n| `eq .Env.VAR \"value\"` | Equality comparison | `{{if eq .Env.APPLICATION_ENV \"development\"}}` |\n| `isTrue .Env.VAR` | Boolean evaluation | `{{if isTrue .Env.PROFILING_EXTENSION_ENABLED}}` |\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl:13](), [util/docker/web/php/php.ini.tmpl:25]()\n\n## Service-Specific Configuration\n\nEach service has dedicated configuration templates that are processed during container startup.\n\n### Nginx Configuration\n\nThe Nginx configuration template handles multiple concerns including SSL, caching, and proxy configurations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    NginxTemplate[\"azuracast.conf.tmpl\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Generated Configuration Blocks\"\n        InternalServer[\"Internal Server\u003cbr/\u003e:6010 - API calls\"]\n        MainServer[\"Main Server\u003cbr/\u003eHTTP/HTTPS\"]\n        StaticAssets[\"Static Asset Serving\u003cbr/\u003eCaching rules\"]\n        Proxying[\"Reverse Proxy\u003cbr/\u003ePHP-FPM, Centrifugo\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Environment Dependencies\"\n        AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT[\"AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT\"]\n        AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT[\"AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT\"]\n        NGINX_TIMEOUT[\"NGINX_TIMEOUT\"]\n        APPLICATION_ENV[\"APPLICATION_ENV\"]\n        UPLOADS_DIR[\"UPLOADS_DIR\"]\n        ACME_DIR[\"ACME_DIR\"]\n    end\n    \n    NginxTemplate --\u003e InternalServer\n    NginxTemplate --\u003e MainServer\n    NginxTemplate --\u003e StaticAssets\n    NginxTemplate --\u003e Proxying\n    \n    AZURACAST_HTTP_PORT --\u003e MainServer\n    AZURACAST_HTTPS_PORT --\u003e MainServer\n    NGINX_TIMEOUT --\u003e MainServer\n    APPLICATION_ENV --\u003e StaticAssets\n    UPLOADS_DIR --\u003e StaticAssets\n    ACME_DIR --\u003e MainServer\n```\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl:55-56](), [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl:123](), [util/docker/web/nginx/azuracast.conf.tmpl:127-136]()\n\n### PHP Configuration\n\nPHP configuration includes both `php.ini` and PHP-FPM pool configurations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"PHP Configuration Templates\"\n        PHPIni[\"php.ini.tmpl\u003cbr/\u003eCore PHP settings\"]\n        PHPFPMConf[\"www.conf.tmpl\u003cbr/\u003eProcess management\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"PHP Settings\"\n        FileSize[\"post_max_size\u003cbr/\u003eupload_max_filesize\"]\n        Memory[\"memory_limit\"]\n        Execution[\"max_execution_time\"]\n        OPCache[\"opcache settings\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Development Features\"\n        XDebug[\"Xdebug configuration\"]\n        SPXProfiler[\"SPX profiling extension\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Process Management\"\n        WWWPool[\"www pool\u003cbr/\u003ePublic requests\"]\n        InternalPool[\"internal pool\u003cbr/\u003eAPI calls\"]\n        ProcessLimits[\"pm.max_children\u003cbr/\u003eWorker limits\"]\n    end\n    \n    PHPIni --\u003e FileSize\n    PHPIni --\u003e Memory\n    PHPIni --\u003e Execution\n    PHPIni --\u003e OPCache\n    PHPIni --\u003e XDebug\n    PHPIni --\u003e SPXProfiler\n    \n    PHPFPMConf --\u003e WWWPool\n    PHPFPMConf --\u003e InternalPool\n    PHPFPMConf --\u003e ProcessLimits\n```\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/web/php/php.ini.tmpl:4-7](), [util/docker/web/php/php.ini.tmpl:17-23](), [util/docker/web/php/www.conf.tmpl:22](), [util/docker/web/php/www.conf.tmpl:48-49]()\n\n### Database Configuration\n\nMariaDB configuration is managed through the `db.cnf.tmpl` template:\n\n| Configuration Area | Environment Variables | Purpose |\n|-------------------|---------------------|---------|\n| Connection Management | `MYSQL_MAX_CONNECTIONS` | Concurrent connection limit |\n| Performance Tuning | `MYSQL_INNODB_BUFFER_POOL_SIZE`, `MYSQL_INNODB_LOG_FILE_SIZE` | Memory and disk optimization |\n| Query Logging | `MYSQL_SLOW_QUERY_LOG` | Performance monitoring |\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/mariadb/mariadb/db.cnf.tmpl:4](), [util/docker/mariadb/mariadb/db.cnf.tmpl:8](), [util/docker/mariadb/mariadb/db.cnf.tmpl:10-11]()\n\n## Runtime Configuration Generation\n\nConfiguration generation occurs during container startup through a series of initialization scripts.\n\n### Startup Script Execution Order\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    UserSetup[\"00_setup_user.sh\u003cbr/\u003eConfigure azuracast user\"]\n    NginxConf[\"05_nginx_conf.sh\u003cbr/\u003eGenerate nginx config\"]\n    SFTPConf[\"07_sftpgo_conf.sh\u003cbr/\u003eConfigure SFTP keys\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"User Configuration\"\n        AZURACAST_PUID[\"AZURACAST_PUID\u003cbr/\u003eUser ID mapping\"]\n        AZURACAST_PGID[\"AZURACAST_PGID\u003cbr/\u003eGroup ID mapping\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Directory Detection\"\n        UploadsDir[\"UPLOADS_DIR detection\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast/uploads vs storage\"]\n        ACMEDir[\"ACME_DIR detection\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast/acme vs storage\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Key Generation\"\n        RSAKey[\"RSA host key\"]\n        ECDSAKey[\"ECDSA host key\"]\n        ED25519Key[\"ED25519 host key\"]\n    end\n    \n    UserSetup --\u003e AZURACAST_PUID\n    UserSetup --\u003e AZURACAST_PGID\n    NginxConf --\u003e UploadsDir\n    NginxConf --\u003e ACMEDir\n    SFTPConf --\u003e RSAKey\n    SFTPConf --\u003e ECDSAKey\n    SFTPConf --\u003e ED25519Key\n```\n\n**Sources:** [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/00_setup_user.sh:3-10](), [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/05_nginx_conf.sh:11-25](), [util/docker/web/startup_scripts/07_sftpgo_conf.sh:3-13]()\n\n### Environment Variable Inheritance\n\nThe configuration system supports multiple levels of environment variable inheritance:\n\n1. **Base defaults** - Defined in template files using `default` function\n2. **Sample environment** - Provided in `azuracast.sample.env`\n3. **User environment** - User-defined values in `azuracast.env`\n4. **Development overrides** - Development-specific values in `azuracast.dev.env`\n\n**Sources:** [azuracast.sample.env:5-38](), [azuracast.dev.env:5-36]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1f:T49d4,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Streaming [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh](util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe Streaming Engine is AzuraCast's core audio broadcasting system, responsible for managing automated playlists, live DJ streams, audio processing, and output to various broadcasting formats. This system is built around Liquidsoap as the primary audio engine and provides comprehensive automation and live broadcasting capabilities.\n\nFor information about the specific broadcasting methods and output configuration, see [Broadcasting Methods](#3.2). For details about Liquidsoap configuration generation, see [Liquidsoap Configuration](#3.1). For audio enhancement and processing features, see [Audio Processing](#3.3).\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nThe streaming engine operates as a multi-layered system with Liquidsoap at its core, managed through PHP backend services that generate configurations and handle real-time communication.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"AzuraCast Backend\"\n        ConfigWriter[\"ConfigWriter\"]\n        Annotations[\"Annotations\"]\n        Commands[\"Liquidsoap Commands\"]\n        StationConfig[\"StationBackendConfiguration\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Liquidsoap Engine\"\n        AzuracstLiq[\"azuracast.liq\"]\n        CrossfadeLiq[\"crossfade.liq\"] \n        UtilitiesLiq[\"utilities.liq\"]\n        GeneratedConfig[\"station.liq\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Audio Sources\"\n        Playlists[\"Station Playlists\"]\n        LiveDJ[\"Harbor Live Input\"]\n        RequestQueue[\"Request Queue\"]\n        RemoteStreams[\"Remote Streams\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Audio Processing\"\n        AutoCue[\"AutoCue Engine\"]\n        Crossfade[\"Smart Crossfade\"]\n        AudioFX[\"Audio Processing\u003cbr/\u003e(MasterMe/StereoTool)\"]\n        ReplayGain[\"ReplayGain\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Broadcasting Outputs\"\n        Icecast[\"Icecast Server\"]\n        Shoutcast[\"Shoutcast Server\"] \n        HLS[\"HLS Streaming\"]\n        Recording[\"Stream Recording\"]\n    end\n    \n    ConfigWriter --\u003e GeneratedConfig\n    StationConfig --\u003e ConfigWriter\n    Annotations --\u003e ConfigWriter\n    \n    GeneratedConfig --\u003e AzuracstLiq\n    AzuracstLiq --\u003e CrossfadeLiq\n    AzuracstLiq --\u003e UtilitiesLiq\n    \n    Playlists --\u003e AzuracstLiq\n    LiveDJ --\u003e AzuracstLiq\n    RequestQueue --\u003e AzuracstLiq\n    RemoteStreams --\u003e AzuracstLiq\n    \n    AzuracstLiq --\u003e AutoCue\n    AutoCue --\u003e Crossfade\n    Crossfade --\u003e AudioFX\n    AudioFX --\u003e ReplayGain\n    \n    ReplayGain --\u003e Icecast\n    ReplayGain --\u003e Shoutcast\n    ReplayGain --\u003e HLS\n    ReplayGain --\u003e Recording\n    \n    Commands -.-\u003e|HTTP API| AzuracstLiq\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq](), [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php]()\n\n## Core Components\n\n### Liquidsoap Integration\n\nAzuraCast integrates with Liquidsoap through dynamically generated configuration files and real-time HTTP API communication. The `ConfigWriter` class generates comprehensive Liquidsoap scripts based on station configuration.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Station[\"Station Entity\"] --\u003e BackendConfig[\"StationBackendConfiguration\"]\n    BackendConfig --\u003e ConfigWriter[\"ConfigWriter\"]\n    ConfigWriter --\u003e LiqConfig[\"station.liq\"]\n    \n    ConfigWriter --\u003e HeaderFunctions[\"writeHeaderFunctions\"]\n    ConfigWriter --\u003e PlaylistConfig[\"writePlaylistConfiguration\"]\n    ConfigWriter --\u003e CrossfadeConfig[\"writeCrossfadeConfiguration\"]\n    ConfigWriter --\u003e HarborConfig[\"writeHarborConfiguration\"]\n    ConfigWriter --\u003e BroadcastConfig[\"writeBroadcastConfiguration\"]\n    \n    LiqConfig --\u003e Liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap Process\"]\n    Liquidsoap --\u003e HttpApi[\"HTTP API\"]\n    HttpApi --\u003e Commands[\"Command Classes\"]\n```\n\nThe configuration generation process follows a structured approach with multiple configuration sections written by dedicated methods in `ConfigWriter`:\n\n- `writeHeaderFunctions()`: Sets up global settings, API endpoints, and common variables\n- `writePlaylistConfiguration()`: Configures playlists, scheduling, and AutoDJ behavior  \n- `writeCrossfadeConfiguration()`: Handles crossfade logic and audio processing\n- `writeHarborConfiguration()`: Sets up live DJ input through Harbor\n- `writeBroadcastConfiguration()`: Configures output streams and recording\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:38-67](), [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php]()\n\n### AutoDJ System\n\nThe AutoDJ system automatically manages playlist rotation, song scheduling, and queue management through the `Annotations` class and Liquidsoap's `request.dynamic` functionality.\n\n| Component | Purpose | Implementation |\n|-----------|---------|----------------|\n| `Annotations` | Processes song metadata and generates Liquidsoap annotations | [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:23]() |\n| `NextSongCommand` | Handles requests for the next song from Liquidsoap | [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/Command/NextSongCommand.php]() |\n| `FeedbackCommand` | Processes song playback feedback and updates history | [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/Command/FeedbackCommand.php]() |\n| `azuracast.autodj_next_song()` | Liquidsoap function that requests next song from API | [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:362-391]() |\n\nThe AutoDJ workflow operates through API communication between Liquidsoap and AzuraCast:\n\n1. Liquidsoap calls `azuracast.autodj_next_song()` when it needs a new track\n2. This function makes an HTTP request to the `/nextsong` endpoint\n3. `NextSongCommand` processes the request and calls `Annotations::annotateNextSong()`\n4. `Annotations` builds the song URI with metadata annotations\n5. Liquidsoap receives the annotated URI and begins playback\n6. When the song starts playing, Liquidsoap sends feedback via `/feedback` endpoint\n7. `FeedbackCommand` updates the song history and now-playing data\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:55-69](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:362-391](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/Command/NextSongCommand.php]()\n\n### Live Broadcasting\n\nLive DJ support is implemented through Liquidsoap's Harbor input source, which provides authentication and stream management capabilities.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    LiveDJ[\"Live DJ Connection\"] --\u003e Harbor[\"input.harbor\"]\n    Harbor --\u003e DjAuth[\"azuracast.dj_auth\"]\n    DjAuth --\u003e AuthCommand[\"DjAuthCommand\"]\n    \n    AuthCommand --\u003e StreamerRepo[\"StationStreamerRepository\"]\n    StreamerRepo --\u003e Scheduler[\"AutoDJ Scheduler\"]\n    \n    Harbor --\u003e LiveConnected[\"azuracast.live_connected\"]\n    Harbor --\u003e LiveDisconnected[\"azuracast.live_disconnected\"]\n    \n    LiveConnected --\u003e DjOnCommand[\"DjOnCommand\"]\n    LiveDisconnected --\u003e DjOffCommand[\"DjOffCommand\"]\n    \n    Harbor --\u003e FallbackMix[\"fallback() with radio\"]\n    FallbackMix --\u003e AudioOutput[\"Audio Processing Pipeline\"]\n```\n\nThe live broadcasting system includes:\n\n- **Authentication**: `azuracast.dj_auth()` function validates streamer credentials via `DjAuthCommand`\n- **Connection Management**: `azuracast.live_connected()` and `azuracast.live_disconnected()` handle DJ session lifecycle\n- **Stream Recording**: Optional recording of live broadcasts to files when enabled\n- **Fallback Logic**: Automatic switching between live input and AutoDJ using `fallback()`\n\nThe Harbor configuration includes buffering, metadata handling, and connection callbacks:\n\n```\nlive = input.harbor(\n    \"/\",\n    id = \"input_streamer\",\n    port = [DJ_PORT],\n    auth = azuracast.dj_auth,\n    icy = true,\n    on_connect = azuracast.live_connected,\n    on_disconnect = azuracast.live_disconnected\n)\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:796-929](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:452-543](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/Command/DjAuthCommand.php]()\n\n## Audio Processing Pipeline\n\nThe audio processing pipeline applies various enhancements and effects to the audio stream before broadcasting, with configurable processing methods and crossfade logic.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Input[\"Audio Input\"] --\u003e Amplify[\"azuracast.apply_amplify\"]\n    Amplify --\u003e ReplayGain[\"ReplayGain Metadata\"]\n    ReplayGain --\u003e Crossfade[\"azuracast.apply_crossfade\"]\n    Crossfade --\u003e Processing[\"Audio Processing Method\"]\n    \n    Processing --\u003e Liquidsoap[\"Built-in Processing\"]\n    Processing --\u003e MasterMe[\"MasterMe LADSPA\"]\n    Processing --\u003e StereoTool[\"Stereo Tool\"]\n    Processing --\u003e None[\"No Processing\"]\n    \n    Liquidsoap --\u003e Normalize[\"normalize + compress\"]\n    MasterMe --\u003e LADSPAPlugin[\"ladspa.master_me\"]\n    StereoTool --\u003e STProcess[\"stereotool() or pipe()\"]\n    \n    Normalize --\u003e Output[\"Final Output\"]\n    LADSPAPlugin --\u003e Output\n    STProcess --\u003e Output\n    None --\u003e Output\n```\n\n### Audio Processing Methods\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple audio processing methods configured via `StationBackendConfiguration::$audio_processing_method`:\n\n| Method | Implementation | Configuration |\n|--------|---------------|---------------|\n| **Liquidsoap** | Built-in normalization and compression | `normalize()` + `compress.exponential()` |\n| **MasterMe** | LADSPA plugin for mastering | Configurable presets and loudness targets |\n| **Stereo Tool** | Professional audio processor | External binary or shared library |\n| **None** | No additional processing | Bypass all enhancement |\n\nThe processing method selection is handled in `ConfigWriter::writePostProcessingSection()`:\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:96-209](), [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php:93-119]()\n\n### Crossfade System\n\nThe crossfade system provides intelligent transitions between tracks using the `cross.smart()` function and live-aware logic through `azuracast.live_aware_crossfade_impl()`.\n\n**Smart Crossfade Logic**:\n- Analyzes dB levels of outgoing and incoming tracks\n- Applies different transition types based on volume characteristics\n- Handles fade-in, fade-out, and full crossfade scenarios\n- Special handling for live DJ transitions\n\n**AutoCue Integration**:\nThe system integrates with AutoCue metadata to provide precise cueing:\n\n- `cue_in`: Start playback at specific time offset\n- `cue_out`: End playback at specific time  \n- `fade_in`/`fade_out`: Fade duration values\n- `cross_start_next`: When to begin next track transition\n- `amplify`: Volume adjustment in dB\n\nAutoCue data is processed by `Annotations::processAutocueAnnotations()` and can be computed dynamically by Liquidsoap or retrieved from AzuraCast's cache.\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/crossfade.liq](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:549-611](), [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:144-248]()\n\n## Configuration Management\n\nThe streaming engine configuration is managed through a combination of PHP entities and Liquidsoap script generation, with support for custom configuration sections and dynamic updates.\n\n### Configuration Entities\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Station[\"Station Entity\"] --\u003e BackendConfig[\"StationBackendConfiguration\"]\n    Station --\u003e MediaMeta[\"StationMediaMetadata\"]\n    \n    BackendConfig --\u003e ProcessingMethod[\"audio_processing_method\"]\n    BackendConfig --\u003e CrossfadeSettings[\"crossfade + crossfade_type\"]\n    BackendConfig --\u003e DJSettings[\"dj_port + dj_buffer + dj_mount_point\"]\n    BackendConfig --\u003e RecordingSettings[\"record_streams + record_streams_format\"]\n    BackendConfig --\u003e CustomSections[\"Custom Configuration Sections\"]\n    \n    MediaMeta --\u003e AutoCueData[\"cue_in + cue_out + fade_in + fade_out\"]\n    MediaMeta --\u003e AmplifyData[\"amplify\"]\n    MediaMeta --\u003e CrossData[\"cross_start_next\"]\n    \n    CustomSections --\u003e TopSection[\"CUSTOM_TOP\"]\n    CustomSections --\u003e PrePlaylist[\"CUSTOM_PRE_PLAYLISTS\"]\n    CustomSections --\u003e PreLive[\"CUSTOM_PRE_LIVE\"]\n    CustomSections --\u003e PreFade[\"CUSTOM_PRE_FADE\"]\n    CustomSections --\u003e PreBroadcast[\"CUSTOM_PRE_BROADCAST\"]\n    CustomSections --\u003e Bottom[\"CUSTOM_BOTTOM\"]\n```\n\n### Custom Configuration Sections\n\nThe system supports six custom configuration sections that allow advanced users to inject Liquidsoap code at specific points:\n\n| Section | Constant | Purpose |\n|---------|----------|---------|\n| Top | `CUSTOM_TOP` | Global settings and variable definitions |\n| Pre-Playlists | `CUSTOM_PRE_PLAYLISTS` | Code before playlist configuration |\n| Pre-Live | `CUSTOM_PRE_LIVE` | Code before Harbor/live setup |\n| Pre-Fade | `CUSTOM_PRE_FADE` | Code before crossfade configuration |\n| Pre-Broadcast | `CUSTOM_PRE_BROADCAST` | Code before output configuration |\n| Bottom | `CUSTOM_BOTTOM` | Final configuration additions |\n\nThese sections are processed by `ConfigWriter::writeCustomConfigurationSection()` and allow for advanced customization while maintaining the core configuration structure.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php:282-355](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:69-94]()\n\n## API Integration\n\nThe streaming engine integrates with AzuraCast's backend through a comprehensive HTTP API that handles real-time communication between Liquidsoap and the web application.\n\n### Command System\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    LiquidsoapProcess[\"Liquidsoap Process\"] --\u003e HttpApi[\"HTTP API Calls\"]\n    HttpApi --\u003e LiquidsoapAction[\"LiquidsoapAction Controller\"]\n    LiquidsoapAction --\u003e CommandEnum[\"LiquidsoapCommands Enum\"]\n    \n    CommandEnum --\u003e NextSong[\"NextSongCommand\"]\n    CommandEnum --\u003e Feedback[\"FeedbackCommand\"]  \n    CommandEnum --\u003e DjAuth[\"DjAuthCommand\"]\n    CommandEnum --\u003e DjOn[\"DjOnCommand\"]\n    CommandEnum --\u003e DjOff[\"DjOffCommand\"]\n    CommandEnum --\u003e Copy[\"CopyCommand\"]\n    CommandEnum --\u003e SaveCache[\"SaveCacheCommand\"]\n    \n    NextSong --\u003e Annotations[\"Annotations Service\"]\n    Feedback --\u003e SongHistory[\"Song History Updates\"]\n    DjAuth --\u003e StreamerAuth[\"Streamer Authentication\"]\n    Copy --\u003e MediaAccess[\"Media File Access\"]\n    SaveCache --\u003e AutoCueCache[\"AutoCue Cache Storage\"]\n```\n\n### Command Endpoints\n\n| Command | Endpoint | Purpose | Handler Class |\n|---------|----------|---------|---------------|\n| `nextsong` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/nextsong` | Request next song for AutoDJ | `NextSongCommand` |\n| `feedback` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/feedback` | Report current song metadata | `FeedbackCommand` |\n| `auth` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/auth` | Authenticate live DJ connection | `DjAuthCommand` |\n| `djon` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/djon` | Handle DJ connection start | `DjOnCommand` |\n| `djoff` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/djoff` | Handle DJ disconnection | `DjOffCommand` |\n| `cp` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/cp` | Copy/access media files | `CopyCommand` |\n| `savecache` | `/api/internal/{station}/liquidsoap/savecache` | Save AutoCue cache data | `SaveCacheCommand` |\n\n### Authentication and Security\n\nAPI communication is secured through station-specific API keys:\n\n- Each station has a unique `adapter_api_key` generated by `Station::generateAdapterApiKey()`\n- Liquidsoap includes this key in the `X-Liquidsoap-Api-Key` header\n- `LiquidsoapAction` validates the key via `Station::validateAdapterApiKey()`\n- Commands are only executed for authenticated requests\n\nThe API key is configured in the generated Liquidsoap configuration:\n\n```\nsettings.azuracast.api_key := \"{STATION_API_KEY}\"\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Controller/Api/Internal/LiquidsoapAction.php](), [backend/src/Radio/Enums/LiquidsoapCommands.php](), [backend/src/Entity/Station.php:190-206]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"20:T4e3f,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh](util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers how AzuraCast dynamically generates and manages Liquidsoap configuration files for radio stations. Liquidsoap serves as AzuraCast's primary audio streaming backend, handling playlist management, live DJ support, audio processing, and broadcasting. For information about broadcasting frontends and stream output configuration, see [Broadcasting Methods](#3.2). For details about audio enhancement and processing tools, see [Audio Processing](#3.3).\n\n## Configuration Generation Architecture\n\nAzuraCast generates Liquidsoap configuration files dynamically based on station settings, playlists, and streaming requirements. The configuration process uses an event-driven architecture to build complex configuration files from modular components.\n\n**Configuration Generation Flow**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    StationSettings[\"Station Backend Configuration\\n(StationBackendConfiguration)\"] --\u003e ConfigWriter[\"ConfigWriter::writeConfiguration\"]\n    ConfigWriter --\u003e Event[\"WriteLiquidsoapConfiguration Event\"]\n    \n    Event --\u003e HeaderFunctions[\"writeHeaderFunctions()\\nSettings and API setup\"]\n    Event --\u003e PlaylistConfig[\"writePlaylistConfiguration()\\nPlaylist and AutoDJ logic\"]\n    Event --\u003e CrossfadeConfig[\"writeCrossfadeConfiguration()\\nAudio transitions\"]\n    Event --\u003e HarborConfig[\"writeHarborConfiguration()\\nLive DJ support\"]\n    Event --\u003e PreBroadcast[\"writePreBroadcastConfiguration()\\nPre-processing\"]\n    Event --\u003e LocalBroadcast[\"writeLocalBroadcastConfiguration()\\nLocal outputs\"]\n    Event --\u003e RemoteBroadcast[\"writeRemoteBroadcastConfiguration()\\nRemote relays\"]\n    Event --\u003e PostBroadcast[\"writePostBroadcastConfiguration()\\nPost-processing\"]\n    \n    HeaderFunctions --\u003e LiquidConfig[\"Generated .liq Configuration File\"]\n    PlaylistConfig --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    CrossfadeConfig --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    HarborConfig --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    PreBroadcast --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    LocalBroadcast --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    RemoteBroadcast --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    PostBroadcast --\u003e LiquidConfig\n    \n    LiquidConfig --\u003e LiquidsoapProcess[\"Liquidsoap Process\"]\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:38-67](), [backend/src/Event/Radio/WriteLiquidsoapConfiguration.php]()\n\nThe `ConfigWriter` class coordinates configuration generation through event subscribers that handle different aspects of the configuration. Each subscriber writes specific sections of the Liquidsoap configuration file.\n\n**Core Configuration Components**\n\n| Component | Class Method | Purpose |\n|-----------|--------------|---------|\n| Header Functions | `writeHeaderFunctions` | API settings, timezone, performance tuning |\n| Playlist Configuration | `writePlaylistConfiguration` | AutoDJ, playlists, scheduling logic |\n| Crossfade Configuration | `writeCrossfadeConfiguration` | Audio transitions and metadata handling |\n| Harbor Configuration | `writeHarborConfiguration` | Live DJ input streams |\n| Broadcast Configuration | `writeLocalBroadcastConfiguration` | Output streams and encoding |\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:49-67]()\n\n## Common Liquidsoap Functions\n\nAzuraCast provides a comprehensive set of common functions that are included in every Liquidsoap configuration. These functions handle core radio automation tasks and provide integration points with the AzuraCast API.\n\n**AzuraCast Common Runtime Functions**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    AzuracastLib[\"azuracast.liq\\nCommon Runtime Functions\"] --\u003e Settings[\"Settings Management\\nsettings.azuracast.*\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e Variables[\"Global Variables\\nazuracast.*\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e API[\"API Communication\\nazuracast.api_call()\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e AutoDJ[\"AutoDJ Functions\\nazuracast.enable_autodj()\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e LiveDJ[\"Live DJ Functions\\nazuracast.dj_auth()\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e Crossfade[\"Crossfade Logic\\nazuracast.apply_crossfade()\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e Feedback[\"Metadata Feedback\\nazuracast.send_feedback()\"]\n    AzuracastLib --\u003e Fallback[\"Fallback Handling\\nazuracast.add_fallback()\"]\n    \n    Settings --\u003e LiquidsoapSettings[\"settings.azuracast.api_url\\nsettings.azuracast.api_key\\nsettings.azuracast.media_path\"]\n    Variables --\u003e RuntimeVars[\"azuracast.live_enabled\\nazuracast.autodj_is_loading\\nazuracast.last_authenticated_dj\"]\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:20-96](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:100-122]()\n\nThe common runtime provides essential settings that control how Liquidsoap integrates with AzuraCast:\n\n- **API Communication**: HTTP API calls to AzuraCast for metadata, authentication, and control\n- **Media Protocol**: Custom `media:` protocol for accessing station media files\n- **AutoCue Support**: Metadata-driven audio cue point handling\n- **Performance Tuning**: Garbage collection and memory optimization settings\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:170-213](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:308-326]()\n\n## Runtime Communication Architecture\n\nLiquidsoap communicates with AzuraCast through HTTP API calls and real-time command processing. This bidirectional communication enables dynamic control and metadata exchange.\n\n**AzuraCast-Liquidsoap Communication Flow**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"AzuraCast Backend\"\n        LiquidsoapAction[\"LiquidsoapAction\\nAPI Controller\"]\n        CommandHandlers[\"Command Handlers\"]\n        \n        NextSongCommand[\"NextSongCommand\\nAnnotations::annotateNextSong()\"]\n        FeedbackCommand[\"FeedbackCommand\\nSong History Updates\"]\n        DjAuthCommand[\"DjAuthCommand\\nStreamer Authentication\"]\n        CopyCommand[\"CopyCommand\\nMedia File Access\"]\n        SaveCacheCommand[\"SaveCacheCommand\\nAutoCue Cache Storage\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Liquidsoap Process\"\n        HTTPRequests[\"azuracast.api_call()\\nHTTP API Requests\"]\n        AutoDJRequests[\"azuracast.autodj_next_song()\\nNext Song Requests\"]\n        AuthRequests[\"azuracast.dj_auth()\\nDJ Authentication\"]\n        FeedbackRequests[\"azuracast.send_feedback()\\nMetadata Updates\"]\n        MediaProtocol[\"media: protocol\\nFile Access Requests\"]\n    end\n    \n    AutoDJRequests --\u003e NextSongCommand\n    AuthRequests --\u003e DjAuthCommand\n    FeedbackRequests --\u003e FeedbackCommand\n    MediaProtocol --\u003e CopyCommand\n    \n    NextSongCommand --\u003e HTTPRequests\n    DjAuthCommand --\u003e HTTPRequests\n    FeedbackCommand --\u003e HTTPRequests\n    CopyCommand --\u003e HTTPRequests\n    SaveCacheCommand --\u003e HTTPRequests\n    \n    LiquidsoapAction --\u003e CommandHandlers\n    CommandHandlers --\u003e NextSongCommand\n    CommandHandlers --\u003e FeedbackCommand\n    CommandHandlers --\u003e DjAuthCommand\n    CommandHandlers --\u003e CopyCommand\n    CommandHandlers --\u003e SaveCacheCommand\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Controller/Api/Internal/LiquidsoapAction.php:26-79](), [backend/src/Radio/Enums/LiquidsoapCommands.php:9-32]()\n\n### Command Processing\n\nAzuraCast handles Liquidsoap commands through dedicated command classes that implement specific functionality:\n\n| Command | Liquidsoap Function | Purpose |\n|---------|-------------------|---------|\n| `nextsong` | `azuracast.autodj_next_song()` | Retrieve next song for AutoDJ queue |\n| `feedback` | `azuracast.send_feedback()` | Update now playing metadata and history |\n| `auth` | `azuracast.dj_auth()` | Authenticate live DJ connections |\n| `cp` | `media:` protocol | Copy media files for playback |\n| `savecache` | AutoCue cache storage | Store computed autocue metadata |\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:362-391](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:453-497](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/Command/]()\n\n## AutoDJ and Playlist Configuration\n\nThe AutoDJ system manages automated playlist playback with support for scheduled playlists, jingles, and various playlist types. Configuration generation handles complex scheduling logic and playlist hierarchies.\n\n**Playlist Configuration Generation**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    StationPlaylists[\"Station Playlists\\n(StationPlaylist entities)\"] --\u003e PlaylistTypes[\"Playlist Type Processing\"]\n    \n    PlaylistTypes --\u003e Standard[\"Standard Playlists\\nrandom() weighted selection\"]\n    PlaylistTypes --\u003e Scheduled[\"Scheduled Playlists\\nswitch() time conditions\"]\n    PlaylistTypes --\u003e OncePerX[\"Once Per X Playlists\\nrotate() or delay()\"]\n    PlaylistTypes --\u003e OncePerHour[\"Once Per Hour\\ntime-based triggers\"]\n    PlaylistTypes --\u003e RemoteURL[\"Remote URL Playlists\\ninput.http() streams\"]\n    \n    Standard --\u003e GeneralRotation[\"radio = random(weights=[...], [...])\"]\n    Scheduled --\u003e ScheduleSwitches[\"switch() with time predicates\"]\n    OncePerX --\u003e SpecialHandling[\"rotate() or delay() wrapping\"]\n    OncePerHour --\u003e HourlyTriggers[\"Minute-based scheduling\"]\n    RemoteURL --\u003e RemoteStreams[\"Remote stream fallbacks\"]\n    \n    GeneralRotation --\u003e FinalRadio[\"Final radio source\"]\n    ScheduleSwitches --\u003e FinalRadio\n    SpecialHandling --\u003e FinalRadio\n    HourlyTriggers --\u003e FinalRadio\n    RemoteStreams --\u003e FinalRadio\n    \n    FinalRadio --\u003e QueueSystem[\"Request Queues\\nrequests, interrupting_queue\"]\n    QueueSystem --\u003e AutoDJEnabled[\"azuracast.enable_autodj()\"]\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:329-638]()\n\n### Playlist Processing Logic\n\nThe configuration writer processes different playlist types with specific Liquidsoap implementations:\n\n- **Standard Playlists**: Use `random()` with weighted selection for general rotation\n- **Scheduled Playlists**: Implement time-based `switch()` statements with complex time predicates\n- **Jingle Playlists**: Special handling with metadata stripping using `azuracast.utilities.drop_metadata()`\n- **Remote Streams**: Integration of external audio sources via `input.http()` or `input.external.ffmpeg()`\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:375-436](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:449-536]()\n\n## Live Streaming and Harbor Configuration\n\nHarbor input enables live DJ broadcasting with authentication, metadata handling, and seamless transitions between live and automated content.\n\n**Harbor Live Streaming Configuration**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    LiveStreamers[\"Station Streamers\\n(enable_streamers = true)\"] --\u003e HarborInput[\"input.harbor() configuration\"]\n    \n    HarborInput --\u003e AuthSystem[\"DJ Authentication\\nazuracast.dj_auth()\"]\n    HarborInput --\u003e ConnectionHandlers[\"Connection Event Handlers\"]\n    HarborInput --\u003e BufferSettings[\"Buffer Configuration\\ndj_buffer settings\"]\n    \n    AuthSystem --\u003e DjAuthCommand[\"DjAuthCommand\\nStreamer validation\"]\n    ConnectionHandlers --\u003e LiveConnected[\"azuracast.live_connected()\"]\n    ConnectionHandlers --\u003e LiveDisconnected[\"azuracast.live_disconnected()\"]\n    \n    LiveConnected --\u003e MetadataInsertion[\"Live metadata
