      OutputFile --\u003e TempFiles[\"Temporary .tmp files\"]\n        TempFiles --\u003e FinalRecordings[\"Final recording files\"]\n    end\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:796-928](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:499-543]()\n\n### Harbor Parameters\n\nHarbor configuration includes several key parameters that control live streaming behavior:\n\n| Parameter | Configuration Source | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------------------|---------|\n| `port` | `liquidsoap-\u003egetStreamPort()` | Streaming port for DJ connections |\n| `auth` | `azuracast.dj_auth` | Authentication function |\n| `on_connect` | `azuracast.live_connected` | Connection event handler |\n| `on_disconnect` | `azuracast.live_disconnected` | Disconnection event handler |\n| `buffer` | `dj_buffer` setting | Buffer size for live streams |\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:811-829]()\n\n## Audio Processing and Crossfade\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple audio processing methods and sophisticated crossfade logic that can adapt to different audio characteristics and live streaming scenarios.\n\n**Audio Processing Pipeline**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    RadioSource[\"radio source\"] --\u003e AudioProcessing[\"Audio Processing Method\"]\n    \n    AudioProcessing --\u003e LiquidsoapMethod[\"Liquidsoap Processing\\nnormalize() + compress()\"]\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e MasterMe[\"MasterMe LADSPA\\nladspa.master_me()\"]\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e StereoTool[\"Stereo Tool\\nstereotool() or pipe()\"]\n    AudioProcessing --\u003e NoProcessing[\"No Processing\\npass-through\"]\n    \n    LiquidsoapMethod --\u003e CrossfadeLogic[\"Crossfade Application\"]\n    MasterMe --\u003e CrossfadeLogic\n    StereoTool --\u003e CrossfadeLogic\n    NoProcessing --\u003e CrossfadeLogic\n    \n    CrossfadeLogic --\u003e SmartCrossfade[\"Smart Crossfade\\ncross.smart()\"]\n    CrossfadeLogic --\u003e SimpleCrossfade[\"Simple Crossfade\\ncross.simple()\"]\n    CrossfadeLogic --\u003e AddCrossfade[\"Add Crossfade\\nadd() with fades\"]\n    CrossfadeLogic --\u003e LiveAwareFade[\"Live-Aware Crossfade\\nazuracast.live_aware_crossfade_impl()\"]\n    \n    SmartCrossfade --\u003e OutputSource[\"Processed audio output\"]\n    SimpleCrossfade --\u003e OutputSource\n    AddCrossfade --\u003e OutputSource\n    LiveAwareFade --\u003e OutputSource\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:96-208](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:749-794](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:549-610]()\n\n### Crossfade Implementation\n\nThe crossfade system adapts to different scenarios:\n\n- **Live Transitions**: Special handling when transitioning to/from live DJs with minimal fade-in\n- **Smart Crossfade**: Audio level analysis determines optimal transition type\n- **AutoCue Integration**: Metadata-driven cue points override default crossfade settings\n- **Track-Sensitive Switching**: Respects song boundaries for playlist switches\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/crossfade.liq:11-119](), [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:604-610]()\n\n## Metadata and Annotation System\n\nAzuraCast provides rich metadata annotation for tracks, including AutoCue data, playlist information, and custom fields. The annotation system integrates with Liquidsoap's metadata handling.\n\n**Annotation Processing Flow**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    StationQueue[\"StationQueue\\nNext song request\"] --\u003e AnnotateEvent[\"AnnotateNextSong Event\"]\n    \n    AnnotateEvent --\u003e SongPath[\"annotateSongPath()\\nmedia: protocol URI\"]\n    AnnotateEvent --\u003e LiquidsoapAnnotate[\"annotateForLiquidsoap()\\nBasic metadata\"]\n    AnnotateEvent --\u003e AutoCueData[\"addCachedAutocueData()\\nCached cue points\"]\n    AnnotateEvent --\u003e PlaylistAnnotate[\"annotatePlaylist()\\nPlaylist metadata\"]\n    AnnotateEvent --\u003e RequestAnnotate[\"annotateRequest()\\nRequest metadata\"]\n    \n    LiquidsoapAnnotate --\u003e BasicMeta[\"title, artist, duration\\nsong_id, media_id\"]\n    AutoCueData --\u003e CuePoints[\"autocue_cue_in, autocue_cue_out\\nautocue_fade_in, autocue_fade_out\"]\n    PlaylistAnnotate --\u003e PlaylistMeta[\"playlist_id, jingle_mode\"]\n    \n    BasicMeta --\u003e FinalAnnotation[\"Final Annotated URI\"]\n    CuePoints --\u003e FinalAnnotation\n    PlaylistMeta --\u003e FinalAnnotation\n    \n    FinalAnnotation --\u003e LiquidsoapQueue[\"Liquidsoap request queue\"]\n    LiquidsoapQueue --\u003e MetadataProcessing[\"Liquidsoap metadata processing\"]\n    MetadataProcessing --\u003e FeedbackAPI[\"azuracast.send_feedback()\"]\n    FeedbackAPI --\u003e SongHistory[\"Song History Updates\"]\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:51-291](), [backend/src/Event/Radio/AnnotateNextSong.php:19-152]()\n\n### AutoCue Metadata Integration\n\nThe AutoCue system provides precise control over audio playback timing:\n\n| Metadata Field | Purpose | Default Behavior |\n|----------------|---------|------------------|\n| `autocue_cue_in` | Start playback position | 0.0 seconds |\n| `autocue_cue_out` | End playback position | Track duration |\n| `autocue_fade_in` | Fade-in duration | Station crossfade setting |\n| `autocue_fade_out` | Fade-out duration | Station crossfade setting |\n| `autocue_start_next` | Next track start timing | Computed from cue points |\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:144-248](), [backend/src/Entity/StationMediaMetadata.php:15-98]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"21:T25ee,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Broadcasting Methods\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)\n- [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh](util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh)\n- [util/write_git_info.sh](util/write_git_info.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document details the various broadcasting methods supported by AzuraCast, including Icecast, SHOUTcast, Rocket Streaming Audio Server (RSAS), and HLS. It explains how these broadcasting methods are configured and integrated within the system architecture. For information about audio processing before broadcasting, see [Audio Processing](#3.3).\n\n## Overview of Broadcasting Methods\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple broadcasting methods that enable stations to deliver audio streams to listeners. These broadcasting methods form the Streaming Layer in the overall system architecture and work in conjunction with the AutoDJ system powered by Liquidsoap.\n\n### Broadcasting Method Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"AutoDJ System\"\n        liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap Process\"]\n    end\n    \n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| icecast[\"Icecast Server\"]\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| shoutcast[\"SHOUTcast Server\"]\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| rsas[\"RSAS (Rocket Streaming Audio Server)\"]\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| hls[\"HLS Stream\"]\n    \n    icecast --\u003e|\"serves via mount points\"| listeners([\"Listeners\"])\n    shoutcast --\u003e|\"serves via stream IDs\"| listeners\n    rsas --\u003e|\"serves via mount points\"| listeners\n    hls --\u003e|\"serves via m3u8 playlists\"| listeners\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/setup/icecast.sh:1-7]()\n\n## Supported Broadcasting Methods\n\nAzuraCast supports the following broadcasting methods:\n\n| Method | Protocol | Formats Supported | Mount Point Style | Container Integration |\n|--------|----------|-------------------|-------------------|----------------------|\n| Icecast | HTTP | MP3, AAC, Ogg Vorbis, Opus | Path-based (`/station-name`) | Dedicated container |\n| SHOUTcast | HTTP | MP3, AAC | Numbered streams (`/stream/1`) | Integrated in station container |\n| RSAS | HTTP | MP3, AAC, Ogg Vorbis | Path-based or numbered | Integrated in station container |\n| HLS | HTTP | Various (in segments) | Playlist files (`/station-name/playlist.m3u8`) | Served via Nginx |\n\n### Icecast\n\nIcecast is a free, open-source streaming media server that AzuraCast uses as its primary broadcasting method. It's deployed in its own Docker container and supports multiple audio formats.\n\n#### Icecast Implementation\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Docker Setup\"\n        icecast_sh[\"util/docker/stations/setup/icecast.sh\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Runtime Container\"\n        supervisord[\"Supervisord Process\"]\n        icecast[\"Icecast Server\"]\n    end\n    \n    icecast_sh --\u003e|\"installs\"| dependencies[\"libxml2, libxslt1.1, openssl\"]\n    dependencies --\u003e|\"required by\"| icecast\n    supervisord --\u003e|\"manages\"| icecast\n    \n    icecast --\u003e|\"creates\"| mountpoints[\"Mount Points (/station-name)\"]\n    mountpoints --\u003e|\"accessed by\"| listeners([\"Listeners\"])\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/setup/icecast.sh:1-7]()\n\nThe Icecast setup script installs the necessary dependencies for the Icecast server:\n\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\nset -e\nset -x\n\n# Icecast is built and imported in its own Docker container.\n\napt-get install -q -y --no-install-recommends libxml2 libxslt1.1 openssl\n```\n\n### SHOUTcast\n\nSHOUTcast is another popular streaming server primarily used for MP3 and AAC audio streams. Unlike Icecast, SHOUTcast uses numbered stream IDs instead of named mount points.\n\n### RSAS (Rocket Streaming Audio Server)\n\nRSAS is a modern streaming server designed to handle a large number of concurrent listeners with improved performance. It supports multiple audio formats and can use either path-based mount points or numbered streams.\n\n### HLS (HTTP Live Streaming)\n\nHLS is a streaming protocol that works by breaking the overall stream into a sequence of small HTTP-based file downloads. This provides better compatibility with various network conditions and is especially useful for mobile devices.\n\n## Broadcasting Flow\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how audio flows from the AutoDJ system to listeners through the various broadcasting methods:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Content Sources\"\n        playlists[\"Playlists\"]\n        schedules[\"Scheduled Events\"]\n        requests[\"Song Requests\"]\n        live[\"Live DJ/Streamer\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"AutoDJ System\"\n        liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap Process\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Broadcasting Servers\"\n        icecast[\"Icecast\"]\n        shoutcast[\"SHOUTcast\"]\n        rsas[\"RSAS\"]\n        hls[\"HLS\"]\n    end\n    \n    playlists --\u003e|\"provides content to\"| liquidsoap\n    schedules --\u003e|\"provides content to\"| liquidsoap\n    requests --\u003e|\"provides content to\"| liquidsoap\n    live --\u003e|\"provides content to\"| liquidsoap\n    \n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| icecast\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| shoutcast\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| rsas\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| hls\n    \n    icecast --\u003e|\"serves streams to\"| listeners([\"Listeners\"])\n    shoutcast --\u003e|\"serves streams to\"| listeners\n    rsas --\u003e|\"serves streams to\"| listeners\n    hls --\u003e|\"serves streams to\"| listeners\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/setup/icecast.sh:1-7]()\n\n## Docker Container Integration\n\nBroadcasting methods are integrated into AzuraCast's Docker infrastructure:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Docker Infrastructure\"\n        docker_compose[\"docker-compose.yml\"]\n    end\n    \n    docker_compose --\u003e|\"defines\"| web_container[\"Web Container\"]\n    docker_compose --\u003e|\"defines\"| station_container[\"Station Container\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Web Container Services\"\n        nginx[\"Nginx Server\"]\n        php_fpm[\"PHP-FPM\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Station Container Services\"\n        icecast[\"Icecast\"]\n        liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap\"]\n    end\n    \n    web_container --\u003e|\"runs\"| nginx\n    web_container --\u003e|\"runs\"| php_fpm\n    station_container --\u003e|\"runs\"| icecast\n    station_container --\u003e|\"runs\"| liquidsoap\n    \n    nginx --\u003e|\"proxies requests to\"| icecast\n    php_fpm --\u003e|\"configures\"| liquidsoap\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"streams to\"| icecast\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/web/service.full/nginx.conf:1-18](), [util/docker/common/scripts/my_init:1-31]()\n\n## Service Management\n\nBroadcasting services are managed by Supervisord with specific configuration files:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Supervisor Configuration\"\n        supervisord_conf[\"/etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf\"]\n        nginx_conf[\"service.full/nginx.conf\"]\n    end\n    \n    my_init[\"util/docker/common/scripts/my_init\"]\n    supervisord[\"Supervisord Process\"]\n    \n    my_init --\u003e|\"starts\"| supervisord\n    supervisord_conf --\u003e|\"includes\"| nginx_conf\n    supervisord --\u003e|\"reads\"| supervisord_conf\n    supervisord --\u003e|\"manages\"| nginx[\"Nginx Server\"]\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/web/service.full/nginx.conf:1-18](), [util/docker/supervisor/setup/supervisor.sh:1-16](), [util/docker/common/scripts/my_init:1-31]()\n\n## Listener Connection Flow\n\nThe following sequence diagram illustrates how a listener connects to a stream:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Listener\n    participant Nginx as \"Nginx Server\"\n    participant Icecast as \"Icecast Server\"\n    participant Liquidsoap as \"Liquidsoap Process\"\n    \n    Listener-\u003e\u003eNginx: Request stream URL\n    Nginx-\u003e\u003eIcecast: Forward request\n    Icecast-\u003e\u003eLiquidsoap: Request stream if not cached\n    Liquidsoap-\u003e\u003eIcecast: Provide audio stream\n    Icecast-\u003e\u003eNginx: Deliver stream\n    Nginx-\u003e\u003eListener: Stream audio content\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/web/service.full/nginx.conf:1-18]()\n\n## Audio Processing Integration\n\nBefore broadcasting, audio can be processed using tools like master_me:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Audio Processing\"\n        master_me_sh[\"util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh\"]\n        liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap Process\"]\n        master_me[\"master_me Audio Processor\"]\n    end\n    \n    master_me_sh --\u003e|\"installs\"| master_me\n    liquidsoap --\u003e|\"processes audio through\"| master_me\n    master_me --\u003e|\"sends processed audio to\"| broadcasting[\"Broadcasting Servers\"]\n    broadcasting --\u003e|\"streams to\"| listeners([\"Listeners\"])\n```\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh:1-27]()\n\n## Mount Points and Stream Access\n\nEach broadcasting method uses a different approach to make streams available to listeners:\n\n| Method | Access Pattern | Example URL | Configuration |\n|--------|----------------|-------------|--------------|\n| Icecast | Named mount points | `http://station.com:8000/station-name` | Set in station configuration |\n| SHOUTcast | Numbered streams | `http://station.com:8000/stream/1` | Automatically numbered |\n| RSAS | Named/numbered | `http://station.com:8000/station-name` | Set in station configuration |\n| HLS | Playlist files | `http://station.com/station-name/playlist.m3u8` | Generated by Liquidsoap |\n\n## Conclusion\n\nAzuraCast provides multiple broadcasting methods to accommodate different streaming needs. Each method has its own advantages and use cases:\n\n- **Icecast**: Versatile, open-source option with good format support\n- **SHOUTcast**: Widely compatible with many players and services\n- **RSAS**: Modern server with high performance for many listeners\n- **HLS**: Adaptive streaming for varying network conditions\n\nThese broadcasting methods are tightly integrated with AzuraCast's Docker infrastructure and the Liquidsoap AutoDJ system, providing a complete end-to-end solution for internet radio broadcasting.\n\nFor more information about audio processing before broadcasting, see [Audio Processing](#3.3)."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"22:T537d,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Audio Processing\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/utilities.liq](util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/utilities.liq)\n- [util/docker/stations/setup/liquidsoap.sh](util/docker/stations/setup/liquidsoap.sh)\n- [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh](util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the audio processing capabilities of AzuraCast, covering how audio is processed both at the per-track level and globally for station streams. For information about general broadcasting methods and outputs, see [Broadcasting Methods](#3.2). For information about Liquidsoap configuration, see [Liquidsoap Configuration](#3.1).\n\n## Overview\n\nAzuraCast provides several methods for audio processing to optimize sound quality, ensure consistent volume levels, and create professional transitions between songs. These capabilities are implemented through the Liquidsoap audio streaming software, which serves as the AutoDJ engine that powers stations.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Audio Processing Flow\"\n        input[\"Audio Input Sources\"]\n        metadata[\"Track Metadata\"]\n        processing[\"Audio Processing\"]\n        output[\"Output Stream\"]\n        \n        input --\u003e|\"Files, Live DJs\"| processing\n        metadata --\u003e|\"Cue points, fade settings,\\namplify values\"| processing\n        processing --\u003e|\"Processed audio\"| output\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Processing Methods\"\n        liq[\"Liquidsoap\\nNormalization\"]\n        masterme[\"master_me\\nMastering Plugin\"]\n        stereotool[\"Stereo Tool\\nAudio Processor\"]\n        none[\"No Processing\"]\n    end\n    \n    processing --- liq\n    processing --- masterme\n    processing --- stereotool\n    processing --- none\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:96-209](), [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php:93-114](), [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh]()\n\n## Audio Processing Methods\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple audio processing methods defined in `AudioProcessingMethods` enum and configured at the station level through `StationBackendConfiguration`:\n\n### Liquidsoap Built-in Processing\n\nThe default processing method uses Liquidsoap's built-in normalization and compression functions. This method provides basic volume normalization and dynamic range compression to create a more consistent sound.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    radio[\"Audio Stream\"] --\u003e normalize[\"normalize()\\nTarget: 0dB\\nWindow: 0.03s\\nGain range: -16 to 0dB\"]\n    normalize --\u003e compress[\"compress.exponential()\\nMu: 1.0\"]\n    compress --\u003e output[\"Processed Stream\"]\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:102-111]()\n\n### master_me\n\n[master_me](https://github.com/trummerschlunk/master_me) is an open-source loudness normalization and mastering plugin integrated into AzuraCast via LADSPA. It offers several presets defined in `MasterMePresets` enum with configurable loudness targets.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph \"master_me Configuration\"\n        input[\"Audio Stream\"] --\u003e masterme[\"LADSPA master_me plugin\"]\n        masterme --\u003e output[\"Processed Stream\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Available Presets\"\n        standard[\"Standard\"]\n        aggressive[\"Aggressive\"]\n        light[\"Light\"]\n        classical[\"Classical\"]\n        voice[\"Voice/Speech\"]\n    end\n    \n    masterme -.- standard\n    masterme -.- aggressive\n    masterme -.- light\n    masterme -.- classical\n    masterme -.- voice\n```\n\nThe `ConfigWriter` generates dynamic Liquidsoap configuration that calls `ladspa.master_me()` with preset-specific parameters. The `MasterMePresets::getOptions()` method provides the preset configuration, which can be overridden by the `master_me_loudness_target` setting.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:113-142](), [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh]()\n\n### Stereo Tool\n\nStereo Tool is a professional audio processing solution that provides advanced processing capabilities including multi-band compression, limiting, stereo enhancement, and more. AzuraCast supports Stereo Tool through either the command-line binary or the library API.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph \"Stereo Tool Integration\"\n        input[\"Audio Stream\"] --\u003e binary[\"Stereo Tool Binary Process\\n(--silent - - -s configuration)\"]\n        input --\u003e library[\"Stereo Tool Library\\n(libStereoTool.so)\"]\n        binary --\u003e output[\"Processed Stream\"]\n        library --\u003e output\n    end\n```\n\nConfiguration is stored in `StationBackendConfiguration` fields `stereo_tool_configuration_path` and `stereo_tool_license_key`. The `ConfigWriter` automatically detects the server architecture and selects the appropriate library file from `StereoTool::getLibraryPath()`.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:144-203]()\n\n## Per-Track Audio Processing\n\nAzuraCast allows for customizing audio processing parameters for individual tracks through metadata:\n\n### Metadata Storage and Processing\n\n**Metadata Flow from Files to Liquidsoap**\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    stationmedia[\"StationMedia Entity\"] --\u003e extrametadata[\"extra_metadata field\\n(StationMediaMetadata)\"]\n    extrametadata --\u003e annotate[\"AnnotateNextSong Event\"]\n    annotate --\u003e annotations[\"Annotations::processAutocueAnnotations()\"]\n    annotations --\u003e liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap azuracast.autocue()\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Metadata Fields\"\n        amplify[\"AMPLIFY constant\"]\n        fadein[\"FADE_IN constant\"] \n        fadeout[\"FADE_OUT constant\"]\n        cuein[\"CUE_IN constant\"]\n        cueout[\"CUE_OUT constant\"]\n        crossstart[\"CROSS_START_NEXT constant\"]\n    end\n    \n    extrametadata --- amplify\n    extrametadata --- fadein\n    extrametadata --- fadeout\n    extrametadata --- cuein\n    extrametadata --- cueout\n    extrametadata --- crossstart\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Entity/StationMediaMetadata.php](), [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:117-139]()\n\n### Supported Audio Parameters\n\nAzuraCast supports the following per-track audio processing parameters:\n\n| Parameter | Description | Example Value |\n|-----------|-------------|--------------|\n| Amplify | Volume adjustment in dB | -3.0 dB |\n| Cue In | Start point of track playback | 0:15.5 |\n| Cue Out | End point of track playback | 3:45.0 |\n| Fade In | Fade-in duration | 2.0 (seconds) |\n| Fade Out | Fade-out duration | 3.0 (seconds) |\n| Cross Start Next | When to start the next track | -10.0 (seconds before end) |\n\nThese parameters are stored in the `extra_metadata` field of the `StationMedia` entity and are converted to appropriate annotations for Liquidsoap during playback.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Entity/StationMediaMetadata.php:15-74](), [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:144-248]()\n\n## Crossfade Configuration\n\nAzuraCast provides flexible crossfade controls at both the station level and per-track level:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Crossfade System\"\n        mode[\"Crossfade Mode\\n(Smart or Normal)\"]\n        duration[\"Default Crossfade Duration\"]\n        enabled[\"Crossfade Enabled/Disabled\"]\n        \n        enabled --\u003e crossfade[\"azuracast.apply_crossfade()\"]\n        mode --\u003e crossfade\n        duration --\u003e crossfade\n        \n        pertrack[\"Per-track Settings\\n(fade_in, fade_out, cross_start_next)\"] --\u003e crossfade\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Live Broadcasts\"\n        live[\"Live DJ Connection\"]\n        live --\u003e liveTransition[\"Special Live Transitions\"]\n        liveTransition --\u003e crossfade\n    end\n```\n\nTwo crossfade modes are available:\n- **Normal**: Simple crossfades with configurable duration\n- **Smart**: Intelligent crossfades that analyze the audio for optimal transition points\n\nWhen a live DJ connects, a special transition handling is applied to fade out the AutoDJ and fade in the live stream.\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:549-611](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:749-794]()\n\n## Audio Processing Implementation\n\nThe audio processing system is implemented through several key components:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Audio Processing Components\"\n        config[\"StationBackendConfiguration\\nstores processing settings\"]\n        writer[\"ConfigWriter\\nGenerates Liquidsoap code\"]\n        annotations[\"Annotations\\nPrepares metadata for tracks\"]\n        liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap\\nExecutes audio processing\"]\n    end\n    \n    config --\u003e|\"Audio processing method\\nand settings\"| writer\n    annotations --\u003e|\"Per-track metadata\"| liquidsoap\n    writer --\u003e|\"Generates configuration\"| liquidsoap\n    \n    subgraph \"Processing Pipeline\"\n        input[\"Input Sources\\n(Files, DJs)\"]\n        fade[\"Crossfade Logic\"]\n        process[\"Audio Processing\\n(Selected method)\"]\n        output[\"Output Stream\"]\n        \n        input --\u003e fade\n        fade --\u003e process\n        process --\u003e output\n    end\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php](), [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php]()\n\n### ConfigWriter Event Priority System\n\nThe `ConfigWriter` implements `EventSubscriberInterface` and processes `WriteLiquidsoapConfiguration` events in priority order:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    event[\"WriteLiquidsoapConfiguration Event\"] --\u003e subscriber[\"ConfigWriter EventSubscriber\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Event Handler Priority Order\"\n        header[\"writeHeaderFunctions (35)\"]\n        playlists[\"writePlaylistConfiguration (30)\"]\n        crossfade[\"writeCrossfadeConfiguration (25)\"]\n        harbor[\"writeHarborConfiguration (20)\"]\n        prebroadcast[\"writePreBroadcastConfiguration (10)\"]\n        localbroadcast[\"writeLocalBroadcastConfiguration (5)\"]\n        hlsbroadcast[\"writeHlsBroadcastConfiguration (2)\"]\n        remotebroadcast[\"writeRemoteBroadcastConfiguration (0)\"]\n        postbroadcast[\"writePostBroadcastConfiguration (-5)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subscriber --\u003e header\n    header --\u003e playlists\n    playlists --\u003e crossfade\n    crossfade --\u003e harbor\n    harbor --\u003e prebroadcast\n    prebroadcast --\u003e localbroadcast\n    localbroadcast --\u003e hlsbroadcast\n    hlsbroadcast --\u003e remotebroadcast\n    remotebroadcast --\u003e postbroadcast\n    \n    crossfade -.-\u003e|\"if !post_processing_include_live\"| processing[\"writePostProcessingSection()\"]\n    prebroadcast -.-\u003e|\"if post_processing_include_live\"| processing\n```\n\nThe `writePostProcessingSection()` method is called from either `writeCrossfadeConfiguration` or `writePreBroadcastConfiguration` depending on the `post_processing_include_live` setting.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:52-66]()\n\n## Metadata Extraction and Management\n\nAzuraCast extracts metadata from media files to enable accurate audio processing:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph \"Metadata Extraction\"\n        audiofile[\"Audio File\"]\n        ffprobe[\"FFprobe Reader\"]\n        phpid3[\"PHP GetID3 Reader\"]\n        metadata[\"Metadata Object\"]\n        \n        audiofile --\u003e ffprobe\n        audiofile --\u003e phpid3\n        ffprobe --\u003e metadata\n        phpid3 --\u003e metadata\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Metadata Storage\"\n        metadata --\u003e stationmedia[\"StationMedia Entity\"]\n        stationmedia --\u003e extrametadata[\"StationMediaMetadata\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe metadata extraction process uses multiple readers (FFprobe and GetID3) to extract audio information, including:\n- Basic metadata (artist, title, album)\n- Audio characteristics (duration, format)\n- ReplayGain information\n- Custom processing metadata (cue points, fades)\n\nSources: [backend/src/Entity/StationMediaMetadata.php:75-97]()\n\n## ReplayGain Support\n\nAzuraCast supports ReplayGain, a standard for normalizing the perceived loudness of audio files. When enabled, ReplayGain metadata from files is used to adjust playback volume:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph \"ReplayGain Processing\"\n        metadata[\"File Metadata\"] --\u003e extractRG[\"Extract ReplayGain Data\"]\n        extractRG --\u003e enableRG[\"enable_replaygain_metadata()\"]\n        enableRG --\u003e applyRG[\"replaygain() Function\\nApplies volume adjustment\"]\n    end\n```\n\nReplayGain can be enabled or disabled in the station settings. When enabled, it uses the ReplayGain metadata in the audio files to normalize volume across the library.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:770-778](), [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php:167-170]()\n\n## Live Broadcasting and Audio Processing\n\nWhen a live DJ connects to the station, the audio processing behavior changes:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Live Broadcasting\"\n        dj[\"Live DJ Connection\"]\n        auth[\"DJ Authentication\"]\n        harbor[\"Liquidsoap Harbor Input\"]\n        \n        dj --\u003e auth\n        auth --\u003e harbor\n        \n        harbor --\u003e metadata[\"Live Metadata Handling\"]\n        harbor --\u003e transition[\"Live Transition Logic\"]\n        \n        transition --\u003e fallback[\"Fallback to AutoDJ\\nwhen DJ disconnects\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Processing Options\"\n        processlive[\"Process Live Broadcasts?\"]\n        processlive --\u003e|\"Yes\"| applyprocessing[\"Apply same processing\\nas AutoDJ\"]\n        processlive --\u003e|\"No\"| skipprocessing[\"Skip processing\\nfor live audio\"]\n    end\n```\n\nAzuraCast provides an option to either apply the same audio processing to live broadcasts or to bypass processing for live streams. This can be configured in the station settings.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:796-929](), [backend/src/Entity/StationBackendConfiguration.php:122-124]()\n\n## Post-Processing Inclusion Options\n\nStation administrators can choose whether to apply audio processing to:\n1. AutoDJ content only\n2. Both AutoDJ and live broadcasts\n\nThis is controlled by the `postProcessingIncludeLive` setting in the station configuration and determines where in the Liquidsoap chain the processing is applied.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:96-209](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:791-794](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:942-945]()\n\n## Setting Up Audio Processing\n\nTo configure audio processing for a station:\n\n1. Navigate to the station's profile settings\n2. Go to the \"Backend Settings\" section\n3. Choose an audio processing method:\n   - None (disabled)\n   - Liquidsoap (basic normalization)\n   - master_me (with preset selection)\n   - Stereo Tool (requires configuration file)\n4. Configure additional options:\n   - For master_me: Select preset and optional loudness target\n   - For Stereo Tool: Upload configuration file and optionally provide license key\n5. Choose whether to apply processing to live broadcasts\n6. Save changes\n\nChanges to audio processing settings require a restart of the station to take effect.\n\n## Technical Implementation Details\n\n### Installation of Audio Processing Components\n\nAudio processing components are installed during the Docker build process:\n\n- **LADSPA SDK**: Base framework for audio plugins\n- **master_me**: Installed from GitHub releases\n- **Stereo Tool**: Provided by the user\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/setup/liquidsoap.sh:13](), [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh]()\n\n### Dependencies Installation\n\nAudio processing dependencies are installed during the Docker container build process:\n\n- **LADSPA SDK**: Provides the plugin framework for master_me\n- **Liquidsoap dependencies**: Audio libraries and codecs for processing\n- **master_me plugin**: Downloaded and installed from GitHub releases\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/setup/liquidsoap.sh:6-10](), [util/docker/stations/setup/liquidsoap.sh:13](), [util/docker/stations/setup/master_me.sh:14-26]()\n\n### Liquidsoap Configuration Generation\n\nThe Liquidsoap configuration is generated dynamically by the `ConfigWriter` class, which is registered as an event subscriber to the `WriteLiquidsoapConfiguration` event:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Liquidsoap Configuration Generation\"\n        event[\"WriteLiquidsoapConfiguration Event\"]\n        writer[\"ConfigWriter Event Subscriber\"]\n        \n        event --\u003e writer\n        \n        writer --\u003e|\"35\"| header[\"writeHeaderFunctions()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"30\"| playlists[\"writePlaylistConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"25\"| crossfade[\"writeCrossfadeConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"20\"| harbor[\"writeHarborConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"10\"| pre[\"writePreBroadcastConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"5\"| local[\"writeLocalBroadcastConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"2\"| hls[\"writeHlsBroadcastConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"0\"| remote[\"writeRemoteBroadcastConfiguration()\"]\n        writer --\u003e|\"-5\"| post[\"writePostBroadcastConfiguration()\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe audio processing configuration is written in the `writePostProcessingSection` method of the `ConfigWriter` class, which is called from either `writeCrossfadeConfiguration` or `writePreBroadcastConfiguration` depending on whether processing should be applied to live broadcasts.\n\nSources: [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:52-66](), [backend/src/Radio/Backend/Liquidsoap/ConfigWriter.php:96-209]()\n\n## AutoCue System\n\nAzuraCast implements an \"AutoCue\" system that handles the precise playback timing of tracks based on their metadata:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"AutoCue System\"\n        media[\"StationMedia\"] --\u003e metadata[\"StationMediaMetadata\"]\n        metadata --\u003e annotations[\"Annotations Component\"]\n        annotations --\u003e|\"Process cue points,\\nfades, amplify\"| autocue[\"AutoCue Annotations\"]\n        autocue --\u003e liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap AutoCue Function\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"AutoCue Cache\"\n        compute[\"Compute AutoCue Values\"] --\u003e cache[\"Cache Storage\"]\n        cache --\u003e|\"Reuse for\\nsame file\"| liquidsoap\n    end\n```\n\nThe AutoCue system uses the metadata from media files to control:\n- When a track starts playing (cue_in)\n- When a track stops playing (cue_out)\n- How the track fades in (fade_in)\n- How the track fades out (fade_out)\n- When to start the next track (cross_start_next)\n- Volume adjustment (amplify)\n\nThese values are passed to Liquidsoap as annotations that control the playback behavior.\n\nSources: [util/docker/stations/liquidsoap/azuracast.liq:238-348](), [backend/src/Radio/AutoDJ/Annotations.php:111-196]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"23:T3a84,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# API and [web/static/openapi.yml](web/static/openapi.yml)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nAzuraCast provides a comprehensive RESTful API system that enables programmatic access to all major platform functionality. This includes public endpoints for now playing data and song requests, administrative endpoints for system management, and a robust webhook system for real-time integrations with external services.\n\nThis page covers the API architecture, authentication mechanisms, endpoint organization, and integration capabilities. For information about the streaming engine's internal APIs, see [Streaming Engine](#3). For frontend application architecture, see [Frontend](#5).\n\n## API Architecture Overview\n\nAzuraCast's API system is organized into distinct layers serving different audiences and security contexts:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"API Layer Architecture\"\n        PublicAPI[\"Public API\u003cbr/\u003e(/api/nowplaying, /api/station/{id})\"]\n        StationAPI[\"Station Management API\u003cbr/\u003e(/api/station/{id}/**)\"]\n        AdminAPI[\"Administrative API\u003cbr/\u003e(/api/admin/**)\"]\n        InternalAPI[\"Internal APIs\u003cbr/\u003e(Backend-Frontend Communication)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Authentication Methods\"\n        NoAuth[\"No Authentication\u003cbr/\u003e(Public endpoints)\"]\n        APIKey[\"API Key Authentication\u003cbr/\u003e(X-API-Key header)\"]\n        SessionAuth[\"Session Authentication\u003cbr/\u003e(Cookie-based)\"]\n        PasskeyAuth[\"Passkey/WebAuthn\u003cbr/\u003e(Passwordless)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Documentation System\"\n        OpenAPISpec[\"OpenAPI 3.0 Specification\u003cbr/\u003e(/api endpoint)\"]\n        SwaggerUI[\"Interactive Documentation\u003cbr/\u003e(Built-in Swagger UI)\"]\n        TypeScriptTypes[\"Generated TypeScript Types\u003cbr/\u003e(ApiInterfaces.ts)\"]\n    end\n    \n    PublicAPI --\u003e NoAuth\n    StationAPI --\u003e APIKey\n    StationAPI --\u003e SessionAuth\n    AdminAPI --\u003e APIKey\n    AdminAPI --\u003e SessionAuth\n    AdminAPI --\u003e PasskeyAuth\n    InternalAPI --\u003e SessionAuth\n    \n    OpenAPISpec --\u003e SwaggerUI\n    OpenAPISpec --\u003e TypeScriptTypes\n```\n\n**Sources:** [web/static/openapi.yml:1-10](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:1-50]()\n\n## OpenAPI Specification and Documentation\n\nAzuraCast maintains a comprehensive OpenAPI 3.0 specification that documents over 250 API endpoints with detailed request/response schemas:\n\n| Component | Location | Purpose |\n|-----------|----------|---------|\n| OpenAPI Specification | [web/static/openapi.yml]() | Complete API documentation in YAML format |\n| Interactive Documentation | `/api` endpoint | Swagger UI for testing endpoints |\n| TypeScript Interfaces | [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts]() | Generated type definitions for frontend |\n\nThe specification includes detailed schemas for all request bodies, response formats, and authentication requirements. The frontend TypeScript interfaces are automatically generated from this specification to ensure type safety between backend and frontend.\n\n**Sources:** [web/static/openapi.yml:1-8](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:1-11]()\n\n## Authentication and Authorization\n\nThe API supports multiple authentication mechanisms depending on the endpoint type and security requirements:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Request[\"Incoming API Request\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Authentication Methods\"\n        APIKeyAuth[\"API Key Authentication\u003cbr/\u003e(X-API-Key header)\"]\n        SessionAuth[\"Session Cookie Authentication\u003cbr/\u003e(azuracast_session)\"]\n        PasskeyAuth[\"Passkey/WebAuthn\u003cbr/\u003e(Passwordless login)\"]\n        NoAuth[\"No Authentication Required\u003cbr/\u003e(Public endpoints)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Authorization Checks\"\n        GlobalPerms[\"Global Permissions\u003cbr/\u003e(administer all, view administration)\"]\n        StationPerms[\"Station Permissions\u003cbr/\u003e(manage station media, broadcasting)\"]\n        PublicAccess[\"Public Access\u003cbr/\u003e(No permissions required)\"]\n    end\n    \n    Request --\u003e APIKeyAuth\n    Request --\u003e SessionAuth  \n    Request --\u003e PasskeyAuth\n    Request --\u003e NoAuth\n    \n    APIKeyAuth --\u003e GlobalPerms\n    APIKeyAuth --\u003e StationPerms\n    SessionAuth --\u003e GlobalPerms\n    SessionAuth --\u003e StationPerms\n    PasskeyAuth --\u003e SessionAuth\n    NoAuth --\u003e PublicAccess\n```\n\n### Permission System\n\nThe authorization system uses enumerated permission sets defined in TypeScript:\n\n- **Global Permissions**: System-wide administrative access defined in `GlobalPermissions` enum\n- **Station Permissions**: Per-station access controls defined in `StationPermissions` enum\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:148-158](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:126-141]()\n\n## Administrative APIs\n\nAdministrative endpoints provide comprehensive system management capabilities organized into functional groups:\n\n### Endpoint Organization\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Admin API Groups (/api/admin/**)\"\n        General[\"General\u003cbr/\u003e(/acme, /api-keys, /logs)\"]\n        Backups[\"Backups\u003cbr/\u003e(/backups/*)\"]\n        CustomFields[\"Custom Fields\u003cbr/\u003e(/custom_fields)\"]\n        Debugging[\"Debugging\u003cbr/\u003e(/debug/*)\"]\n        Roles[\"Roles \u0026 Permissions\u003cbr/\u003e(/roles, /permissions)\"]\n        Settings[\"System Settings\u003cbr/\u003e(/settings)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Key Controllers\"\n        AuditLogAction[\"AuditLogAction\u003cbr/\u003e(GET /admin/auditlog)\"]\n        BackupActions[\"Backup Management\u003cbr/\u003e(GET|POST|DELETE /admin/backups/*)\"]\n        DebugActions[\"Debug Tools\u003cbr/\u003e(PUT /admin/debug/*)\"]\n        RoleActions[\"Role Management\u003cbr/\u003e(GET|POST|PUT|DELETE /admin/role/*)\"]\n    end\n    \n    General --\u003e AuditLogAction\n    Backups --\u003e BackupActions\n    Debugging --\u003e DebugActions\n    Roles --\u003e RoleActions\n```\n\n### Audit Logging System\n\nThe audit logging system tracks all administrative actions with detailed change tracking:\n\n| Component | Purpose | Implementation |\n|-----------|---------|----------------|\n| `AuditLog` Entity | Core audit log data model | [backend/src/Entity/AuditLog.php]() |\n| `AuditLogAction` Controller | API endpoint for retrieving audit logs | [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/AuditLogAction.php]() |\n| `AuditLogChangeset` | Change detail schema | [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/AuditLogChangeset.php]() |\n| Frontend Components | Vue.js audit log viewer | [frontend/components/Admin/AuditLog.vue]() |\n\nThe audit log tracks:\n- **Operations**: Insert, update, delete operations using `AuditLogOperations` enum\n- **Entity Changes**: Field-level before/after values in JSON format\n- **User Attribution**: Links actions to specific user accounts\n- **Temporal Data**: High-precision timestamps for all actions\n\n**Sources:** [backend/src/Entity/AuditLog.php:27-167](), [backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/AuditLogAction.php:18-70]()\n\n## Public APIs\n\nPublic endpoints provide unauthenticated access to essential station data:\n\n### Core Public Endpoints\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    PublicRequests[\"Public API Requests\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Now Playing System\"\n        NowPlayingEndpoint[\"/api/nowplaying\u003cbr/\u003e(All stations)\"]\n        StationNowPlaying[\"/api/nowplaying/{station_id}\u003cbr/\u003e(Single station)\"]\n        NowPlayingData[\"ApiNowPlaying Schema\u003cbr/\u003e(Station, listeners, current song)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Station Information\"\n        StationList[\"/api/stations\u003cbr/\u003e(Public station list)\"]\n        StationDetails[\"/api/station/{id}\u003cbr/\u003e(Station details)\"]\n        StationSchema[\"ApiNowPlayingStation Schema\u003cbr/\u003e(Metadata, mount points, URLs)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Interactive Features\"\n        SongRequests[\"/api/station/{id}/requests\u003cbr/\u003e(Submit song requests)\"]\n        RequestList[\"/api/station/{id}/requests\u003cbr/\u003e(View available songs)\"]\n    end\n    \n    PublicRequests --\u003e NowPlayingEndpoint\n    PublicRequests --\u003e StationNowPlaying\n    PublicRequests --\u003e StationList\n    PublicRequests --\u003e StationDetails\n    PublicRequests --\u003e SongRequests\n    \n    NowPlayingEndpoint --\u003e NowPlayingData\n    StationNowPlaying --\u003e NowPlayingData\n    StationList --\u003e StationSchema\n    StationDetails --\u003e StationSchema\n```\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:756-770](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:800-888]()\n\n## Real-time Integration\n\nAzuraCast provides real-time data updates through WebSocket connections and server-sent events:\n\n### Centrifugo Integration\n\nThe platform uses Centrifugo for high-performance real-time updates:\n\n- **Now Playing Updates**: Real-time song change notifications\n- **Listener Statistics**: Live listener count updates  \n- **System Status**: Station online/offline state changes\n\n### High-Performance Updates\n\nThe system supports optimized update mechanisms that immediately send current data upon connection, eliminating the need for separate initial API calls.\n\n**Sources:** [CHANGELOG.md:407-411]()\n\n## Webhook System\n\nAzuraCast includes a comprehensive webhook system for integrating with external services:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Webhook Triggers (WebhookTriggers enum)\"\n        SongChanged[\"song_changed\"]\n        SongChangedLive[\"song_changed_live\"] \n        ListenerGained[\"listener_gained\"]\n        ListenerLost[\"listener_lost\"]\n        LiveConnect[\"live_connect\"]\n        LiveDisconnect[\"live_disconnect\"]\n        StationOffline[\"station_offline\"]\n        StationOnline[\"station_online\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Webhook Types (WebhookTypes enum)\"\n        Generic[\"generic\"]\n        Discord[\"discord\"]\n        Email[\"email\"]\n        Mastodon[\"mastodon\"]\n        Bluesky[\"bluesky\"]\n        Analytics[\"google_analytics_v4, matomo_analytics\"]\n        RadioServices[\"tunein, radiode, radioreg, getmeradio\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"External Services\"\n        DiscordService[\"Discord Webhooks\u003cbr/\u003e(Message color support)\"]\n        SocialMedia[\"Social Media APIs\u003cbr/\u003e(Bluesky, Mastodon)\"]\n        RadioAggregators[\"Radio Aggregators\u003cbr/\u003e(TuneIn, Radio.de)\"]\n        AnalyticsServices[\"Analytics Platforms\u003cbr/\u003e(Google Analytics, Matomo)\"]\n    end\n    \n    SongChanged --\u003e Discord\n    LiveConnect --\u003e Discord\n    StationOnline --\u003e RadioServices\n    SongChanged --\u003e SocialMedia\n    ListenerGained --\u003e Analytics\n    \n    Discord --\u003e DiscordService\n    Mastodon --\u003e SocialMedia\n    Bluesky --\u003e SocialMedia\n    RadioServices --\u003e RadioAggregators\n    Analytics --\u003e AnalyticsServices\n```\n\n### Webhook Features\n\n- **Rate Limiting**: Configurable rate limits to prevent flooding external services\n- **Custom Payloads**: Flexible payload customization for different service requirements\n- **Retry Logic**: Automatic retry mechanisms for failed webhook deliveries\n- **Debug Logging**: Comprehensive logging for troubleshooting webhook issues\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:13-29](), [frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:31-40](), [CHANGELOG.md:115-116]()\n\n## System Monitoring APIs\n\nAdministrative endpoints provide detailed system performance metrics:\n\n### Server Statistics\n\nThe admin API exposes comprehensive server monitoring data through structured schemas:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"ServerStats API (/api/admin/server/stats)\"\n        StatsEndpoint[\"GET /admin/server/stats\"]\n        ServerStatsSchema[\"Api_Admin_ServerStats\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Statistics Components\"\n        CpuStats[\"CpuStats\u003cbr/\u003e(Usage, cores, load average)\"]\n        MemoryStats[\"MemoryStats\u003cbr/\u003e(Total, free, cached, buffers)\"] \n        StorageStats[\"StorageStats\u003cbr/\u003e(Disk usage, swap)\"]\n        NetworkStats[\"NetworkInterfaceStats\u003cbr/\u003e(Received/transmitted data)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Detailed Metrics\"\n        CpuStatsSection[\"CpuStatsSection\u003cbr/\u003e(Per-core usage, I/O wait)\"]\n        NetworkReceived[\"NetworkInterfaceReceived\u003cbr/\u003e(Bytes, packets, errors)\"]\n        NetworkTransmitted[\"NetworkInterfaceTransmitted\u003cbr/\u003e(Bytes, packets, errors)\"]\n    end\n    \n    StatsEndpoint --\u003e ServerStatsSchema\n    ServerStatsSchema --\u003e CpuStats\n    ServerStatsSchema --\u003e MemoryStats\n    ServerStatsSchema --\u003e StorageStats\n    ServerStatsSchema --\u003e NetworkStats\n    \n    CpuStats --\u003e CpuStatsSection\n    NetworkStats --\u003e NetworkReceived\n    NetworkStats --\u003e NetworkTransmitted\n```\n\n**Sources:** [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/ServerStats/ServerStats.php:9-31](), [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/ServerStats/CpuStats.php:9-25](), [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/ServerStats/MemoryStats.php:11-71]()\n\n## External Service Integrations\n\nAzuraCast supports integration with numerous external platforms and services:\n\n### Radio Directory Services\n\n- **TuneIn**: Automatic metadata updates to TuneIn directory\n- **Radio.de**: Integration with German radio aggregator\n- **RadioReg**: Webhook support for RadioReg service  \n- **GetMeRadio**: API integration for station listings\n\n### Social Media Platforms\n\n- **Discord**: Rich webhook messages with customizable colors\n- **Mastodon**: ActivityPub-compatible social posting\n- **Bluesky**: AT Protocol social media integration\n\n### Analytics Platforms\n\n- **Google Analytics v4**: Advanced web analytics integration\n- **Matomo**: Privacy-focused analytics platform\n- **Prometheus**: Time-series metrics endpoint at `/api/prometheus`\n\n### Cloud Storage Services\n\n- **Amazon S3**: S3-compatible storage for media and backups\n- **Dropbox**: OAuth-based file storage integration\n- **SFTP**: Secure file transfer protocol support\n\n**Sources:** [web/static/openapi.yml](web/static/openapi.yml)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides a comprehensive overview of the AzuraCast REST API, its structure, authentication methods, and usage patterns. The AzuraCast API allows external applications to interact with AzuraCast radio stations, retrieve now playing data, manage media, and perform administrative functions.\n\nFor specific information about authentication and authorization, see [Authentication and Authorization](#4.2). For a detailed list of available endpoints, see [Available Endpoints](#4.3).\n\n## API Architecture\n\nThe AzuraCast API is a RESTful API that follows OpenAPI 3.0 specifications. It provides access to AzuraCast's functionality through HTTP endpoints organized by functionality.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Client[\"Client Application\"] --\u003e|HTTP Requests| API[\"AzuraCast API\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"AzuraCast API Gateway\"\n        API --\u003e|Routes to| Controllers[\"API Controllers\"]\n        Controllers --\u003e|Uses| Entities[\"API Entities\"]\n        Controllers --\u003e|Interacts with| Backend[\"Backend Services\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Response Processing\"\n        Backend --\u003e ResponseFormat[\"Response Formatting\"]\n        ResponseFormat --\u003e Client\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Authentication\"\n        API --\u003e|Validates| Auth[\"API Keys/Authentication\"]\n        Auth --\u003e|Enforces| Permissions[\"Permission Checks\"]\n    end\n```\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:1-167]()\n[web/static/openapi.yml:1-100]()\n\n## API Structure\n\nThe AzuraCast API is organized into logical groups of endpoints based on their functionality:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    API[\"AzuraCast API\"] --\u003e Public[\"Public Endpoints\"]\n    API --\u003e Station[\"Station Management\"]\n    API --\u003e Admin[\"Administration\"]\n    API --\u003e Account[\"Account Management\"]\n    \n    Public --\u003e NowPlaying[\"Now Playing\"]\n    Public --\u003e PublicStations[\"Stations\"]\n    Public --\u003e Misc[\"Miscellaneous\"]\n    \n    Station --\u003e StationGeneral[\"General\"]\n    Station --\u003e Broadcasting[\"Broadcasting\"]\n    Station --\u003e Media[\"Media\"]\n    Station --\u003e Playlists[\"Playlists\"]\n    Station --\u003e Podcasts[\"Podcasts\"]\n    Station --\u003e Mounts[\"Mount Points\"]\n    Station --\u003e Relays[\"Remote Relays\"]\n    Station --\u003e Streamers[\"Streamers/DJs\"]\n    \n    Admin --\u003e AdminGeneral[\"General\"]\n    Admin --\u003e Debug[\"Debugging\"]\n    Admin --\u003e Backups[\"Backups\"]\n    Admin --\u003e Roles[\"Roles\"]\n    Admin --\u003e Settings[\"Settings\"]\n```\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:30-67]()\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:170-240]()\n\n### Endpoint Groups\n\nThe API endpoints are organized into the following main groups:\n\n| Group | Description | Tag Prefix |\n|-------|-------------|------------|\n| Public | Publicly accessible endpoints that don't require authentication | `Public:` |\n| Station Management | Endpoints for managing station-specific functions | `Stations:` |\n| Administration | Endpoints for system-wide administrative functions | `Administration:` |\n| Account | Endpoints for managing user accounts and API keys | `My Account` |\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:170-236]()\n\n## API Base URL\n\nThe API is accessible through the `/api` path on your AzuraCast installation. For example, if your AzuraCast instance is at `https://your-azuracast.example.com`, the API base URL would be `https://your-azuracast.example.com/api`.\n\nThe example server in the OpenAPI specification is:\n\n```\nhttps://demo.azuracast.com/api\n```\n\nSources:\n[web/static/openapi.yml:9-12]()\n\n## Authentication\n\nThe AzuraCast API supports authentication via API keys. API keys are passed in the `X-API-Key` HTTP header.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API as AzuraCast API\n    participant Auth as Authentication Handler\n    participant Backend as AzuraCast Backend\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: Request with X-API-Key header\n    API-\u003e\u003eAuth: Validate API key\n    Auth--\u003e\u003eAPI: Valid/Invalid + Permission level\n    \n    alt API Key Valid\n        API-\u003e\u003eBackend: Process request\n        Backend--\u003e\u003eAPI: Response data\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: 200 OK with response\n    else API Key Invalid or Missing\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: 403 Forbidden\n    else Insufficient Permissions\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: 403 Forbidden\n    end\n```\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:161-166]()\n[backend/src/Controller/Api/Frontend/Account/ApiKeysController.php:17-201]()\n[backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/ApiKeysController.php:1-64]()\n\n### Creating API Keys\n\nAPI keys can be created by authenticated users through the `/frontend/account/api-keys` endpoint. Each API key can have specific permissions and can be revoked at any time.\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/Controller/Api/Frontend/Account/ApiKeysController.php:46-67]()\n[backend/src/Entity/Api/Account/NewApiKey.php:1-24]()\n\n## Response Format\n\nThe API returns data in JSON format. A standard response structure is used across the API:\n\n### Success Responses\n\nSuccessful responses typically return HTTP status code 200 and a JSON object containing the requested data.\n\n### Error Responses\n\nError responses follow a standard format:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"code\": 500,\n  \"type\": \"NotLoggedInException\",\n  \"message\": \"Error description.\",\n  \"formatted_message\": \"\u003cb\u003eError description.\u003c/b\u003e\u003cbr\u003eDetailed error text.\",\n  \"extra_data\": [],\n  \"success\": false\n}\n```\n\nCommon error status codes:\n- 403: Access Denied\n- 404: Record Not Found\n- 500: Generic Error\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:140-160]()\n[frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:442-472]()\n\n## API Entities\n\nAzuraCast uses standardized entity types throughout the API. These entities form the basis for request and response data formats.\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class HasLinks {\n        +array links\n    }\n    \n    class ApiStatus {\n        +boolean success\n        +string message\n    }\n    \n    class ApiError {\n        +number code\n        +string type\n        +string message\n        +string formatted_message\n        +array extra_data\n        +boolean success\n    }\n    \n    class ApiSong {\n        +string id\n        +string text\n        +string artist\n        +string title\n        +string album\n        +string genre\n        +number lyrics\n        +string art\n        +array custom_fields\n    }\n    \n    class ApiNowPlaying {\n        +ApiNowPlayingStation station\n        +ApiNowPlayingListeners listeners\n        +ApiNowPlayingLive live\n        +ApiNowPlayingCurrentSong now_playing\n        +ApiNowPlayingStationQueue playing_next\n        +ApiNowPlayingSongHistory[] song_history\n        +boolean is_online\n        +string cache\n    }\n    \n    HasLinks \u003c|-- ApiNowPlaying\n    HasLinks \u003c|-- ApiSong\n```\n\nSources:\n[frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:420-720]()\n[backend/src/Entity/Api/Traits/HasLinks.php:1-18]()\n\n## Common API Patterns\n\n### Entity Identifiers\n\nAzuraCast uses several types of identifiers throughout the API:\n\n1. **Auto-incrementing IDs**: Integer IDs that increment automatically\n2. **UUID/GUID IDs**: String-based unique identifiers\n3. **SplitToken IDs**: Used for API keys and other secure tokens\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasAutoIncrementId.php:1-48]()\n[backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasUniqueId.php:1-50]()\n[backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasSplitTokenFields.php:1-57]()\n\n### CRUD Operations\n\nMany API endpoints follow standard CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) patterns:\n\n- `GET /resource` - List all resources\n- `POST /resource` - Create a new resource\n- `GET /resource/{id}` - Get a specific resource\n- `PUT /resource/{id}` - Update a specific resource\n- `DELETE /resource/{id}` - Delete a specific resource\n\n### API Documentation\n\nThe API is documented using OpenAPI 3.0 specifications. The documentation is available at `/static/openapi.yml` on your AzuraCast installation.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    OpenAPISpec[\"OpenAPI Specification\\n/static/openapi.yml\"] --\u003e PHPAttributes[\"PHP Attributes\\n#OA\\\\* Annotations\"]\n    OpenAPISpec --\u003e TSTypes[\"TypeScript Interfaces\\nGenerated Types\"]\n    \n    PHPAttributes --\u003e Controllers[\"API Controllers\"]\n    PHPAttributes --\u003e Entities[\"API Entities\"]\n    \n    Controllers --\u003e Endpoints[\"API Endpoints\"]\n    Entities --\u003e RequestBodies[\"Request Bodies\"]\n    Entities --\u003e Responses[\"Response Objects\"]\n    \n    TSTypes --\u003e FrontendCode[\"Frontend API Client\"]\n```\n\nSources:\n[web/static/openapi.yml:1-39]()\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:1-252]()\n[frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts:1-10]()\n\n## API Tags and Categories\n\nThe API endpoints are categorized using tags that group related functionality:\n\n### Public Endpoints\n- **Public: Now Playing** - Endpoints for retrieving current playback information\n- **Public: Stations** - Public station information\n- **Public: Miscellaneous** - Other public endpoints\n\n### Station Management\n- **Stations: General** - General station management\n- **Stations: Broadcasting** - Manage station broadcasting\n- **Stations: Media** - Manage station media\n- **Stations: Playlists** - Manage station playlists\n- **Stations: Podcasts** - Manage station podcasts\n- And many more station-specific categories\n\n### Administration\n- **Administration: General** - General admin functions\n- **Administration: Debugging** - Debug tools\n- **Administration: Settings** - System settings\n- **Administration: Roles** - User role management\n- And other admin categories\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:30-67]()\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:170-240]()\n\n## Typical Use Cases\n\nThe API can be used for various purposes, including:\n\n1. **Now Playing Data**: Retrieve current song information for display on external websites\n2. **Station Management**: Automate station operations\n3. **Media Management**: Upload, organize, and manage media files\n4. **Playlist Management**: Create and manage playlists\n5. **Administrative Functions**: Perform system administration tasks\n\n## Example: Fetching Now Playing Data\n\nOne of the most common API uses is fetching \"Now Playing\" data for a station. This doesn't require authentication and returns detailed information about what's currently playing on a station.\n\nThe endpoint for this is:\n```\nGET /api/nowplaying/{station_id}\n```\n\nResponse includes:\n- Current song information\n- Listener statistics\n- Stream status\n- Song history\n- Upcoming songs\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/OpenApi.php:170-172]()\n\n## Logging and Debugging\n\nThe API provides several endpoints for viewing logs and debugging information:\n\n1. **Admin Logs**: View application logs (`/admin/logs`)\n2. **Station Logs**: View station-specific logs (`/station/{station_id}/logs`)\n\nThese endpoints can help diagnose issues with the API and understand how it's functioning.\n\nSources:\n[backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/LogsAction.php:1-210]()\n[backend/src/Controller/Api/Stations/LogsAction.php:1-151]()\n[backend/src/Entity/Api/LogType.php:1-30]()\n[backend/src/Entity/Api/LogContents.php:1-30]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"25:T2800,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# [web/static/openapi.yml](web/static/openapi.yml)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document details the authentication and authorization mechanisms in AzuraCast. It explains how users are authenticated, how API keys work, and how the permission system controls access to various parts of the application.\n\nFor information about the API endpoints themselves, see [API Overview](#4.1).\n\n## Authentication Methods\n\nAzuraCast provides two primary authentication methods: user login for the web interface and API key authentication for programmatic access.\n\n### User Authentication\n\nWeb users authenticate through the standard username/password login form. AzuraCast implements several security features to protect user accounts:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    User[\"User\"]\n    Login[\"Login Form\"]\n    TwoFactor[\"Two-Factor Authentication\"]\n    Session[\"User Session\"]\n    \n    User --\u003e Login\n    Login --\u003e |\"Successful login\"| CheckTwoFactor[\"Check for 2FA\"]\n    CheckTwoFactor --\u003e |\"2FA enabled\"| TwoFactor\n    CheckTwoFactor --\u003e |\"2FA disabled\"| Session\n    TwoFactor --\u003e |\"Valid code\"| Session\n    \n    subgraph \"Password Security\"\n        ChangePassword[\"Change Password Functionality\"]\n        PasswordReset[\"Password Reset\"]\n    end\n    \n    User --\u003e PasswordReset\n    User --\u003e ChangePassword\n```\n\nSources:\n- backend/src/Entity/Api/Account/ChangePassword.php\n- backend/src/Entity/Api/Account/TwoFactorStatus.php\n\n### API Key Authentication\n\nFor programmatic access to the API, AzuraCast uses API keys as the primary authentication method. API keys are passed via the `X-API-Key` HTTP header.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    User[\"User\"]\n    CreateKey[\"Create API Key\"]\n    ApiKey[\"API Key Entity\"]\n    Api[\"API Endpoint\"]\n    \n    User --\u003e CreateKey\n    CreateKey --\u003e |\"Generates\"| ApiKey\n    ApiKey --\u003e |\"X-API-Key header\"| Api\n    \n    subgraph \"API Key Management\"\n        ListKeys[\"List Keys\"]\n        DeleteKey[\"Delete Key\"]\n        User --\u003e ListKeys\n        User --\u003e DeleteKey\n        DeleteKey --\u003e ApiKey\n    end\n```\n\nAPI keys are implemented using a split token mechanism for security:\n- The public part of the token is used as an identifier\n- The verifier part is hashed and stored in the database\n- Both parts together are required for authentication\n\nSources:\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n- backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasSplitTokenFields.php\n- backend/src/Controller/Api/Frontend/Account/ApiKeysController.php\n- backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/ApiKeysController.php\n\n## Authorization System\n\nAzuraCast uses a role-based access control (RBAC) system to manage permissions throughout the application.\n\n### Permission Structure\n\nPermissions in AzuraCast are divided into two categories:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Permission Types\"\n        GlobalPermissions[\"Global Permissions\"]\n        StationPermissions[\"Station Permissions\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Role Assignment\"\n        Role[\"Role\"]\n        User[\"User\"]\n        User --\u003e |\"Assigned to\"| Role\n        Role --\u003e |\"Contains\"| GlobalPermissions\n        Role --\u003e |\"Contains\"| StationPermissions\n    end\n    \n    Permission[\"Permission\"]\n    GlobalPermissions --\u003e Permission\n    StationPermissions --\u003e Permission\n    \n    Permission --\u003e Action[\"Authorized Action\"]\n```\n\n1. **Global Permissions**: These apply system-wide and control access to administrative functions.\n2. **Station Permissions**: These are scoped to specific radio stations and control operations within those stations.\n\nSources:\n- backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/PermissionsAction.php\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n\n### Roles and Permissions Management\n\nAdministrators can manage roles and permissions through the web interface or API:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    Admin[\"Administrator\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Roles Management\"\n        ListRoles[\"List Roles\"]\n        CreateRole[\"Create Role\"]\n        EditRole[\"Edit Role\"]\n        DeleteRole[\"Delete Role\"]\n    end\n    \n    Admin --\u003e ListRoles\n    Admin --\u003e CreateRole\n    Admin --\u003e EditRole\n    Admin --\u003e DeleteRole\n    \n    ListRoles --\u003e Role[\"Role Entity\"]\n    CreateRole --\u003e Role\n    EditRole --\u003e Role\n    DeleteRole --\u003e Role\n    \n    ListPermissions[\"List Permissions\"]\n    Admin --\u003e ListPermissions\n    \n    Role --\u003e |\"Contains\"| Permissions[\"Permissions\"]\n    ListPermissions --\u003e Permissions\n```\n\nThe API endpoints for roles management are defined in the OpenAPI specification and implemented in the relevant controllers.\n\nSources:\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n- backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/PermissionsAction.php\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Authentication Implementation\n\n#### API Key Handling\n\nAPI keys use a secure split token mechanism implemented through the `HasSplitTokenFields` trait. When a new API key is created:\n\n1. A secure random token is generated\n2. The token is split into an identifier and a verifier\n3. The identifier is stored publicly and used for lookups\n4. The verifier is hashed and stored securely\n\nWhen authenticating API requests, the system:\n1. Extracts the API key from the X-API-Key header\n2. Looks up the API key entity by its identifier\n3. Verifies the provided key against the stored hash\n4. If verified, associates the user with the request\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API as \"API Middleware\"\n    participant DB as \"Database\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: Request with X-API-Key header\n    API-\u003e\u003eDB: Look up API key\n    DB--\u003e\u003eAPI: Return API key entity\n    API-\u003e\u003eAPI: Verify token\n    alt Valid token\n        API-\u003e\u003eAPI: Associate user with request\n        API-\u003e\u003eClient: Process request normally\n    else Invalid token\n        API-\u003e\u003eClient: Return 401 Unauthorized\n    end\n```\n\nSources:\n- backend/src/Entity/Traits/HasSplitTokenFields.php\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n\n### Authorization Implementation\n\n#### Permission Checking\n\nThe permission system is implemented through the ACL (Access Control List) class, which is injected into controllers that need to check permissions.\n\nThe primary permissions groups include:\n\n| Permission Type | Description | Example Permissions |\n|----------------|-------------|---------------------|\n| Global Permissions | System-wide administrative capabilities | administer settings, manage users, view reports |\n| Station Permissions | Station-specific operations | manage media, schedule broadcasts, view listeners |\n\nSources:\n- backend/src/Controller/Api/Admin/PermissionsAction.php\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n\n#### Role Assignment\n\nRoles serve as collections of permissions that can be assigned to users. The system comes with predefined roles, and administrators can create custom roles with specific permission sets.\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class User {\n        +id: int\n        +email: string\n        +roles: Role[]\n    }\n    \n    class Role {\n        +id: int\n        +name: string\n        +permissions: Permission[]\n    }\n    \n    class Permission {\n        +id: string\n        +name: string\n    }\n    \n    User \"many\" --\u003e \"many\" Role : has\n    Role \"many\" --\u003e \"many\" Permission : contains\n```\n\nSources:\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n- backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/Permissions.php (implied from API responses)\n\n## Security Considerations\n\n### API Key Best Practices\n\n1. **Limited Scope**: Create API keys with the minimum permissions required for the task\n2. **Rotation**: Regularly rotate API keys to limit exposure in case of compromise\n3. **Monitoring**: Monitor API key usage for unusual patterns that might indicate compromise\n\n### Permission Management Best Practices\n\n1. **Least Privilege**: Assign users the minimum permissions needed for their role\n2. **Regular Audits**: Periodically review user permissions and roles\n3. **Custom Roles**: Create specific roles for different job functions rather than using generic roles\n\nAzuraCast provides an audit log feature that records permission-related changes, helping administrators track security-relevant actions.\n\nSources:\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n\n## API Authentication in Client Applications\n\nWhen building applications that integrate with AzuraCast's API, authentication is handled by including the API key in the `X-API-Key` HTTP header:\n\n```\nX-API-Key: your_api_key_here\n```\n\nAll secured API endpoints in AzuraCast use this authentication method, as defined in the OpenAPI specification security scheme:\n\n```\nsecurity:\n  - ApiKey: []\n```\n\nPublic endpoints (like Now Playing data) don't require authentication.\n\nSources:\n- web/static/openapi.yml\n- backend/src/OpenApi.php"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"26:T5087,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Available \"1\" --\u003e \"*\" Podcast : has\n    StationPlaylist \"*\" --\u003e \"*\" StationMedia : contains\n    Podcast \"1\" --\u003e \"*\" PodcastEpisode : has\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/Entity/Api/Admin/StorageLocation.php:11-164]()\n\n## API Security and Authentication\n\nThe AzuraCast API supports authentication via API keys that are passed in the `X-API-Key` header. Different endpoints require different permissions, which are enforced based on the API key's associated user role.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API as \"AzuraCast API\"\n    participant Auth as \"Authentication System\"\n    participant Controller as \"API Controller\"\n    participant Data as \"Data Storage\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: Request with X-API-Key header\n    API-\u003e\u003eAuth: Validate API Key\n    Auth--\u003e\u003eAPI: API Key valid, User Role determined\n    \n    alt Has Required Permissions\n        API-\u003e\u003eController: Process Request\n        Controller-\u003e\u003eData: Data Operation\n        Data--\u003e\u003eController: Operation Result\n        Controller--\u003e\u003eAPI: Format Response\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: Success Response (200 OK)\n    else Missing Permissions\n        Auth--\u003e\u003eAPI: Permission Denied\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: Access Denied (403 Forbidden)\n    else Invalid API Key\n        Auth--\u003e\u003eAPI: Authentication Failed\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: Unauthorized (401 Unauthorized)\n    end\n```\n\nSources: [backend/src/OpenApi.php:161-166]()\n\n## Rate Limiting and API Usage\n\nAzuraCast implements rate limiting to prevent API abuse. Users should be aware of these limits and design their applications accordingly.\n\nFor detailed information about specific endpoints and their request/response formats, refer to the OpenAPI specification, which can be accessed via the `/api` endpoint on your AzuraCast installation."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"27:T36d4,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"#
