[vite.config.js](vite.config.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides a comprehensive overview of the AzuraCast frontend architecture, its components, build system, and integration with the backend. The frontend is responsible for the user interface and user interactions within the AzuraCast system. For detailed information about API functionality and integration, see [API and Integration](#4).\n\n## Core Technology Stack\n\nAzuraCast's frontend is built with modern JavaScript technologies:\n\n| Technology | Purpose |\n|------------|---------|\n| Vue.js 3 | Main frontend framework using the Composition API |\n| TypeScript | Type-safety and improved developer experience |\n| Vite | Build tool and development server |\n| Bootstrap 5 | CSS framework for styling and components |\n| Axios | HTTP client for API communication |\n| vue-router | Client-side routing |\n| vue3-gettext | Internationalization |\n\nThe frontend uses a component-based architecture with TypeScript for type safety throughout the codebase.\n\nSources: [package.json:4-55](), [vite.config.js:1-97]()\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Entry Points\"\n        Layout[\"Layout.js - Global Component\"]\n        Pages[\"Pages (AdminIndex, StationsProfile, etc.)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Core Libraries\"\n        VueLib[\"Vue.js\"]\n        Router[\"vue-router\"]\n        Axios[\"Axios HTTP Client\"]\n        Gettext[\"vue3-gettext Translations\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Components\"\n        CommonComponents[\"Common Components\"] --\u003e Modal[\"Modal\"]\n        CommonComponents --\u003e DataTable[\"DataTable\"]\n        CommonComponents --\u003e Forms[\"Form Components\"]\n        CommonComponents --\u003e AudioPlayer[\"AudioPlayer\"]\n        CommonComponents --\u003e Icons[\"Icons\"]\n        \n        FeatureComponents[\"Feature Components\"] --\u003e StationsComp[\"Station Components\"]\n        FeatureComponents --\u003e AdminComp[\"Admin Components\"]\n        FeatureComponents --\u003e PublicComp[\"Public Components\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"State Management\"\n        Stores[\"Composable Store Functions\"]\n        Stores --\u003e PlayerStore[\"usePlayerStore\"]\n        Stores --\u003e NotifyStore[\"useNotify\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Services\"\n        APIClient[\"API Client\"]\n        NProg[\"NProgress Loading Indicator\"]\n        AzuraCast[\"AzuraCast Context\"]\n    end\n\n    Layout --\u003e CommonComponents\n    Pages --\u003e FeatureComponents\n    Pages --\u003e CommonComponents\n    \n    FeatureComponents --\u003e APIClient\n    CommonComponents --\u003e APIClient\n    \n    APIClient --\u003e Axios\n    \n    VueLib --\u003e Layout\n    VueLib --\u003e Pages\n    Router --\u003e Pages\n    Gettext --\u003e CommonComponents\n    Gettext --\u003e FeatureComponents\n```\n\nAzuraCast's frontend is organized around Vue.js components that are loaded through specific entry points. The main layout contains global elements, while individual page components are loaded as needed.\n\nSources: [frontend/vendor/axios.ts:1-112](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:1-217]()\n\n## Build System\n\nAzuraCast uses Vite for its frontend build system, which provides fast development and optimized production builds.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    NPM[\"npm scripts\"] --\u003e Build[\"npm run build\"]\n    NPM --\u003e Dev[\"npm run serve\"]\n    NPM --\u003e Lint[\"npm run lint\"]\n    NPM --\u003e TypeCheck[\"npm run tsc\"]\n    NPM --\u003e GenAPI[\"npm run generate-api\"]\n    NPM --\u003e GenLocales[\"npm run generate-locales\"]\n    NPM --\u003e GenIcons[\"npm run generate-icons\"]\n    \n    Build --\u003e Vite[\"Vite Build Process\"]\n    Dev --\u003e ViteDev[\"Vite Dev Server\"]\n    \n    GenAPI --\u003e SwaggerTS[\"swagger-typescript-api\"]\n    GenAPI --\u003e APIInterfaces[\"frontend/entities/ApiInterfaces.ts\"]\n    \n    Vite --\u003e EntryPoints[\"Multiple Entry Points Detection\"]\n    Vite --\u003e ChunkSplitting[\"Chunk Splitting\"]\n    Vite --\u003e AssetGeneration[\"Asset Generation\"]\n    \n    EntryPoints --\u003e PagesJS[\"frontend/js/pages/**/*.js\"]\n    EntryPoints --\u003e LayoutJS[\"frontend/js/layout.js\"]\n    \n    ChunkSplitting --\u003e VendorChunks[\"Vendor Chunks (vue, lodash, etc.)\"]\n    \n    AssetGeneration --\u003e DistDir[\"web/static/vite_dist\"]\n```\n\nThe build system handles:\n\n1. TypeScript compilation and type checking\n2. Component compilation (Vue SFC)\n3. SCSS processing\n4. Code splitting and chunking\n5. API interface generation from OpenAPI specs\n6. Translation extraction\n\nSources: [package.json:4-13](), [vite.config.js:6-64](), [frontend/genapi.mjs:1-14]()\n\n## Component Structure\n\nThe frontend uses a hierarchical component structure:\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class VueApp {\n        Global Application Instance\n    }\n    \n    class Layout {\n        Global Layout Component\n    }\n    \n    class Pages {\n        Page-specific Components\n    }\n    \n    class CommonComponents {\n        Reusable UI Components\n    }\n    \n    class FeatureComponents {\n        Feature-specific Components\n    }\n    \n    class AudioPlayer {\n        Media Playback Component\n    }\n    \n    class Modal {\n        Dialog Component\n    }\n    \n    class DataTable {\n        Data Display Component\n    }\n\n    VueApp -- Layout\n    VueApp -- Pages\n    Pages -- CommonComponents\n    Pages -- FeatureComponents\n    CommonComponents \u003c|-- AudioPlayer\n    CommonComponents \u003c|-- Modal\n    CommonComponents \u003c|-- DataTable\n    FeatureComponents -- CommonComponents\n```\n\nComponents are organized into:\n- Common components (reusable across the application)\n- Feature-specific components (for stations, admin, etc.)\n- Page components (entry points for specific views)\n\nSources: [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:1-217](), [frontend/components/Stations/Playlists/ReorderModal.vue:1-199]()\n\n## Frontend-Backend Integration\n\nAzuraCast's frontend integrates with the backend through:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Browser as Browser\n    participant Vue as Vue Components\n    participant PHP as PHP Templates\n    participant API as REST API\n    participant Backend as Backend Logic\n\n    User-\u003e\u003eBrowser: Visit AzuraCast Page\n    Browser-\u003e\u003ePHP: Request Page\n    PHP-\u003e\u003eBrowser: Return HTML with Vue Component Reference\n    Browser-\u003e\u003eBrowser: Load Vue Component (JS)\n    Browser-\u003e\u003eVue: Initialize with Props\n    Vue-\u003e\u003eAPI: Make API Requests\n    API-\u003e\u003eBackend: Process Request\n    Backend-\u003e\u003eAPI: Return Data\n    API-\u003e\u003eVue: Return Response\n    Vue-\u003e\u003eBrowser: Update UI\n    Browser-\u003e\u003eUser: Display Updated Page\n```\n\nThe integration works through:\n\n1. PHP templates that include Vue components with initial props\n2. Vue components that make API requests to fetch additional data\n3. Axios interceptors that handle errors and display notifications\n4. Real-time updates through WebSocket or polling mechanisms\n\nSources: [backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml:1-53](), [backend/templates/partials/head.phtml:1-54](), [frontend/vendor/axios.ts:14-111]()\n\n## API Communication\n\nAzuraCast uses Axios for API communication with the backend:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Frontend Components\"\n        Components[\"Vue Components\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"API Communication\"\n        UseAxios[\"useAxios() Composable\"]\n        AxiosInstance[\"Axios Instance\"]\n        AxiosSilent[\"Silent Axios Instance (No Progress)\"]\n        CSRF[\"CSRF Token\"]\n        ErrorHandling[\"Error Handling\"]\n        Progress[\"NProgress Indicator\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Backend API\"\n        APIEndpoints[\"API Endpoints\"]\n        APIControllers[\"API Controllers\"]\n    end\n    \n    Components --\u003e UseAxios\n    UseAxios --\u003e AxiosInstance\n    UseAxios --\u003e AxiosSilent\n    \n    AxiosInstance --\u003e CSRF\n    AxiosInstance --\u003e ErrorHandling\n    AxiosInstance --\u003e Progress\n    \n    AxiosSilent --\u003e CSRF\n    AxiosSilent --\u003e ErrorHandling\n    \n    AxiosInstance --\u003e APIEndpoints\n    AxiosSilent --\u003e APIEndpoints\n    APIEndpoints --\u003e APIControllers\n```\n\nThe API communication layer includes:\n\n1. Axios instances with interceptors for handling requests and responses\n2. Error handling that displays user-friendly notifications\n3. CSRF token management for security\n4. Loading indicators using NProgress\n5. TypeScript interfaces generated from OpenAPI specifications\n\nSources: [frontend/vendor/axios.ts:14-111](), [frontend/genapi.mjs:1-14]()\n\n## Media Playback\n\nA key component of AzuraCast's frontend is the audio player which handles streaming media:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    AudioPlayer[\"AudioPlayer Component\"] --\u003e HTMLAudio[\"HTML5 Audio Element\"]\n    AudioPlayer --\u003e HLS[\"HLS.js Client\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Player State\"\n        PlayerStore[\"usePlayerStore\"]\n        Current[\"Current Playing Item\"]\n        IsPlaying[\"Playback State\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Playback Controls\"\n        Play[\"play()\"]\n        Stop[\"stop()\"]\n        Volume[\"Volume Control\"]\n        Progress[\"Progress Control\"]\n    end\n    \n    AudioPlayer --\u003e PlayerStore\n    PlayerStore --\u003e Current\n    PlayerStore --\u003e IsPlaying\n    \n    AudioPlayer --\u003e Play\n    AudioPlayer --\u003e Stop\n    AudioPlayer --\u003e Volume\n    AudioPlayer --\u003e Progress\n    \n    Play --\u003e HTMLAudio\n    Stop --\u003e HTMLAudio\n    \n    BroadcastChannel[\"Broadcast Channel API\"] \u003c--\u003e AudioPlayer\n    MediaSession[\"Media Session API\"] \u003c--\u003e AudioPlayer\n    \n    HTMLAudio --\u003e StandardStreams[\"Standard Audio Streams\"]\n    HLS --\u003e HLSStreams[\"HLS Streams\"]\n```\n\nThe audio player features:\n\n1. Support for both standard audio streams and HLS streams\n2. Volume and playback controls\n3. Cross-tab synchronization using the Broadcast Channel API\n4. Media Session API integration for OS-level media controls\n5. Error handling and automatic reconnection\n\nSources: [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:10-216]()\n\n## Modal System\n\nAzuraCast implements a reusable modal system:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Component[\"Vue Component\"] --\u003e UseHasModal[\"useHasModal() Composable\"]\n    UseHasModal --\u003e ModalRef[\"Modal Template Ref\"]\n    \n    ModalRef --\u003e Show[\"show()\"]\n    ModalRef --\u003e Hide[\"hide()\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Modal Component API\"\n        OpenModal[\"open()\"]\n        CloseModal[\"close()\"]\n        SubmitForm[\"submit()\"]\n    end\n    \n    Component --\u003e OpenModal\n    OpenModal --\u003e Show\n    CloseModal --\u003e Hide\n```\n\nThe modal system provides:\n\n1. A consistent interface for modal dialogs\n2. Composition functions for managing modal state\n3. Easy integration with forms for data submission\n\nSources: [frontend/functions/useHasModal.ts:1-23](), [frontend/components/Stations/Playlists/ReorderModal.vue:2-193]()\n\n## Build and Development Process\n\nThe AzuraCast frontend development workflow includes:\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---------|---------|\n| `npm run serve` | Start the development server |\n| `npm run build` | Build for production |\n| `npm run lint` | Run ESLint to check code quality |\n| `npm run tsc` | Run TypeScript compiler for type checking |\n| `npm run generate-api` | Generate TypeScript interfaces from OpenAPI spec |\n| `npm run generate-locales` | Extract translation strings |\n| `npm run generate-icons` | Generate icon components |\n\nThe development environment supports:\n\n1. Hot module replacement for fast development\n2. TypeScript type checking\n3. ESLint for code quality\n4. Integration with backend API during development\n\nSources: [package.json:5-13](), [eslint.config.mjs:1-30]()\n\n## Internationalization\n\nAzuraCast supports multiple languages through vue3-gettext:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Components[\"Vue Components\"] --\u003e GetText[\"$gettext()\"]\n    GetText --\u003e TranslationKeys[\"Translation Keys\"]\n    TranslationKeys --\u003e ExtractScript[\"Translation Extraction\"]\n    ExtractScript --\u003e POFiles[\"PO Translation Files\"]\n    POFiles --\u003e CompileStep[\"Compile Translations\"]\n    CompileStep --\u003e JSONTranslations[\"JSON Translation Files\"]\n    JSONTranslations --\u003e Runtime[\"Runtime Translation\"]\n```\n\nThe i18n system includes:\n\n1. Translation functions in components (`$gettext`)\n2. Extraction of translation strings from code\n3. PO files for translators\n4. Compiled JSON translations loaded at runtime\n\nSources: [package.json:12](), [frontend/components/Admin/Backups.vue:13-147]()\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe AzuraCast frontend provides a responsive, modern user interface built on Vue.js and TypeScript. It integrates closely with the backend through a REST API while providing an intuitive user experience for managing radio stations. The component-based architecture allows for code reuse and maintainability, while the build system ensures optimized production builds.\n\nFor developers, the frontend codebase is organized around Vue components, with TypeScript providing type safety and ESLint ensuring code quality. The system supports internationalization, responsive design, and modern features like audio streaming and visualization.\n\nSources: [package.json:1-84](), [vite.config.js:1-97]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"28:T35eb,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Vue Components\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [backend/templates/partials/head.phtml](backend/templates/partials/head.phtml)\n- [backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml](backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml)\n- [eslint.config.mjs](eslint.config.mjs)\n- [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue](frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue)\n- [frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/useMixerValue.ts](frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/useMixerValue.ts)\n- [frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/usePassthroughSync.ts](frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/usePassthroughSync.ts)\n- [frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/useWebDjNode.ts](frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/useWebDjNode.ts)\n- [frontend/components/Stations/Playlists/ReorderModal.vue](frontend/components/Stations/Playlists/ReorderModal.vue)\n- [frontend/functions/createRequiredInjectionState.ts](frontend/functions/createRequiredInjectionState.ts)\n- [frontend/functions/injectRequired.ts](frontend/functions/injectRequired.ts)\n- [frontend/functions/theme.ts](frontend/functions/theme.ts)\n- [frontend/functions/useHasModal.ts](frontend/functions/useHasModal.ts)\n- [frontend/genapi.mjs](frontend/genapi.mjs)\n- [frontend/vendor/axios.ts](frontend/vendor/axios.ts)\n- [package-lock.json](package-lock.json)\n- [package.json](package.json)\n- [vite.config.js](vite.config.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers the Vue.js frontend component architecture used in AzuraCast, including component organization, composition patterns, state management, and integration with the build system. For information about the build process and asset compilation, see [Build Process](#5.2). For details about the audio player implementation specifically, see [Audio Player](#5.3).\n\n## Component Architecture Overview\n\nAzuraCast's frontend is built using Vue 3 with TypeScript and the Composition API. Components are organized in a hierarchical structure and use modern Vue patterns including `\u003cscript setup\u003e`, composition functions, and dependency injection.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Frontend Application Layer\"\n        ViteConfig[\"vite.config.js\u003cbr/\u003eBuild Configuration\"]\n        PackageJson[\"package.json\u003cbr/\u003eDependencies\"]\n        LayoutJs[\"layout.js\u003cbr/\u003eBase Application\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Component Categories\"\n        CommonComponents[\"Common Components\u003cbr/\u003eIcon, Modal, AudioPlayer\"]\n        StationComponents[\"Station Components\u003cbr/\u003ePlaylists, Media Management\"]\n        PublicComponents[\"Public Components\u003cbr/\u003eWebDJ, Player Controls\"]\n        AdminComponents[\"Admin Components\u003cbr/\u003eSystem Management\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Composition Layer\"\n        Composables[\"Composables\u003cbr/\u003euseAxios, usePlayerStore\u003cbr/\u003euseNotify, useTranslate\"]\n        InjectionState[\"Injection State\u003cbr/\u003ecreateRequiredInjectionState\u003cbr/\u003euseProvideWebDjNode\"]\n        VendorUtils[\"Vendor Utilities\u003cbr/\u003eaxios.ts, gettext\u003cbr/\u003eazuracast.ts\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Backend Integration\"\n        PHPTemplates[\"PHP Templates\u003cbr/\u003evue_body.phtml\u003cbr/\u003ehead.phtml\"]\n        ComponentRegistry[\"Component Registry\u003cbr/\u003ePage Registration\u003cbr/\u003eAsset Loading\"]\n    end\n    \n    ViteConfig --\u003e CommonComponents\n    ViteConfig --\u003e StationComponents\n    ViteConfig --\u003e PublicComponents\n    ViteConfig --\u003e AdminComponents\n    \n    CommonComponents --\u003e Composables\n    StationComponents --\u003e Composables\n    PublicComponents --\u003e Composables\n    AdminComponents --\u003e Composables\n    \n    Composables --\u003e InjectionState\n    Composables --\u003e VendorUtils\n    \n    LayoutJs --\u003e PHPTemplates\n    CommonComponents --\u003e ComponentRegistry\n    ComponentRegistry --\u003e PHPTemplates\n```\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:1-98](), [package.json:1-85](), [backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml:1-53](), [backend/templates/partials/head.phtml:1-54]()\n\n## Component Organization Structure\n\nComponents are organized into logical categories within the `frontend/components/` directory, following a domain-driven approach that mirrors AzuraCast's feature areas.\n\n| Category | Purpose | Examples |\n|----------|---------|----------|\n| `Common/` | Reusable UI components | `Icon.vue`, `Modal.vue`, `AudioPlayer.vue` |\n| `Stations/` | Station management features | `Playlists/ReorderModal.vue` |\n| `Public/` | Public-facing features | `WebDJ/` components |\n| `Admin/` | Administrative interfaces | System configuration components |\n\nThe component hierarchy follows Vue 3 patterns with TypeScript:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    BaseComponent[\"Vue Component\u003cbr/\u003escript setup + TypeScript\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Component Structure\"\n        Template[\"\u0026lt;template\u0026gt;\u003cbr/\u003eHTML Structure\"]\n        Script[\"\u0026lt;script setup lang='ts'\u0026gt;\u003cbr/\u003eComposition Logic\"]\n        Style[\"\u0026lt;style lang='scss'\u0026gt;\u003cbr/\u003eComponent Styles\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Script Composition\"\n        Props[\"defineProps\u0026lt;Interface\u0026gt;()\"]\n        Emits[\"defineEmits\u0026lt;Events\u0026gt;()\"]\n        Refs[\"ref(), computed(), watch()\"]\n        Composables[\"useAxios(), useNotify()\u003cbr/\u003eusePlayerStore()\"]\n        TemplateRefs[\"useTemplateRef()\"]\n        Expose[\"defineExpose()\"]\n    end\n    \n    BaseComponent --\u003e Template\n    BaseComponent --\u003e Script\n    BaseComponent --\u003e Style\n    \n    Script --\u003e Props\n    Script --\u003e Emits\n    Script --\u003e Refs\n    Script --\u003e Composables\n    Script --\u003e TemplateRefs\n    Script --\u003e Expose\n```\n\nSources: [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:1-217](), [frontend/components/Stations/Playlists/ReorderModal.vue:1-199]()\n\n## Vue 3 Composition API Patterns\n\nAzuraCast extensively uses Vue 3's Composition API with TypeScript for component logic. The codebase follows consistent patterns for component definition and data management.\n\n### Component Definition Pattern\n\nComponents use the `\u003cscript setup\u003e` syntax with TypeScript interfaces for props and events:\n\n```typescript\n// Type-safe props definition\nconst props = withDefaults(\n    defineProps\u003c{\n        title?: string,\n        volume?: number,\n        isMuted?: boolean\n    }\u003e(),\n    {\n        volume: 55,\n        isMuted: false\n    }\n);\n\n// Type-safe event emissions\nconst emit = defineEmits\u003c{\n    (e: 'update:duration', value: number): void,\n    (e: 'update:currentTime', value: number): void,\n    (e: 'update:progress', value: number): void\n}\u003e();\n```\n\n### Reactivity and Lifecycle\n\nComponents use Vue 3's reactivity system with refs, computed properties, and watchers:\n\n```typescript\n// Reactive state\nconst duration = ref\u003cnumber\u003e(0);\nconst currentTime = ref\u003cnumber\u003e(0);\n\n// Computed properties\nconst progress = computed(() =\u003e {\n    return (duration.value !== 0)\n        ? +((currentTime.value / duration.value) * 100).toFixed(2)\n        : 0;\n});\n\n// Template refs\nconst $audio = useTemplateRef('$audio');\n\n// Watchers with throttling\nwatchThrottled(\n    progress,\n    (newValue) =\u003e {\n      emit('update:progress', newValue);\n    },\n    {throttle: 500}\n);\n```\n\nSources: [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:16-26](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:28-32](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:169-181]()\n\n## State Management and Dependency Injection\n\nAzuraCast implements a custom state management system using Vue 3's provide/inject mechanism combined with composition functions, avoiding the need for external state management libraries.\n\n### Injection State Pattern\n\nThe application uses a custom `createRequiredInjectionState` function to create type-safe dependency injection:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    CreateInjection[\"createRequiredInjectionState()\"]\n    Provider[\"useProvideX()\"]\n    Consumer[\"useInjectX()\"]\n    \n    CreateInjection --\u003e Provider\n    CreateInjection --\u003e Consumer\n    \n    subgraph \"Provider Component\"\n        ProviderLogic[\"State Logic\u003cbr/\u003eBusiness Rules\"]\n        ProvideCall[\"provide(key, state)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Consumer Components\"\n        InjectCall[\"inject(key)\"]\n        TypeSafety[\"Type-safe Access\u003cbr/\u003eRuntime Validation\"]\n    end\n    \n    Provider --\u003e ProviderLogic\n    ProviderLogic --\u003e ProvideCall\n    \n    Consumer --\u003e InjectCall\n    InjectCall --\u003e TypeSafety\n```\n\n### Core Composables\n\nThe application provides several core composables for shared functionality:\n\n| Composable | Purpose | Key Features |\n|------------|---------|--------------|\n| `useAxios()` | HTTP client management | CSRF handling, error notification, loading states |\n| `usePlayerStore()` | Audio player state | Cross-tab synchronization, media session integration |\n| `useNotify()` | User notifications | Toast messages, error handling |\n| `useTranslate()` | Internationalization | Vue3-gettext integration |\n\n### Custom Injection Examples\n\n```typescript\n// WebDJ audio node management\nexport const [useProvideWebDjNode, useInjectWebDjNode] = createRequiredInjectionState(\n    (webcaster) =\u003e {\n        const doPassThrough = ref(false);\n        const bitrate = ref(128);\n        const context = computed(() =\u003e {\n            return new AudioContext({\n                sampleRate: sampleRate.value\n            });\n        });\n        \n        return {\n            doPassThrough,\n            bitrate,\n            context,\n            startStream,\n            stopStream\n        };\n    }\n);\n```\n\nSources: [frontend/functions/createRequiredInjectionState.ts:1-23](), [frontend/components/Public/WebDJ/useWebDjNode.ts:6-116](), [frontend/vendor/axios.ts:14-23]()\n\n## Component Lifecycle and Template Integration\n\nAzuraCast integrates Vue components with its PHP backend through a template system that handles component registration, asset loading, and server-side rendering preparation.\n\n### PHP Template Integration\n\nThe backend renders Vue components using PHP templates that handle asset dependencies and component initialization:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    PHPBackend[\"PHP Backend\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Template System\"\n        VueBody[\"vue_body.phtml\u003cbr/\u003eComponent Container\"]\n        HeadPartial[\"head.phtml\u003cbr/\u003eAsset Loading\"]\n        ComponentInfo[\"getVueComponentInfo()\u003cbr/\u003eDependency Resolution\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Asset Management\"\n        ViteDist[\"vite_dist/\u003cbr/\u003eBuilt Assets\"]\n        Manifest[\"manifest.json\u003cbr/\u003eAsset Mapping\"]\n        Prefetch[\"Module Preload\u003cbr/\u003eCSS Dependencies\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Component Initialization\"\n        ScriptTag[\"\u0026lt;script type='module'\u0026gt;\"]\n        ComponentMount[\"window.vueComponent()\u003cbr/\u003eMount Function\"]\n        PropsInjection[\"Global Props\u003cbr/\u003eComponent Props\"]\n    end\n    \n    PHPBackend --\u003e VueBody\n    PHPBackend --\u003e HeadPartial\n    \n    VueBody --\u003e ComponentInfo\n    HeadPartial --\u003e ComponentInfo\n    ComponentInfo --\u003e ViteDist\n    ViteDist --\u003e Manifest\n    \n    VueBody --\u003e ScriptTag\n    ScriptTag --\u003e ComponentMount\n    ComponentMount --\u003e PropsInjection\n    \n    HeadPartial --\u003e Prefetch\n```\n\n### Component Registration Flow\n\n1. **Asset Resolution**: PHP resolves component dependencies using Vite manifest\n2. **Head Injection**: CSS and preload links are added to the document head\n3. **Component Container**: A mounting div is created with a unique ID\n4. **Script Loading**: Module scripts are loaded with component initialization\n5. **Props Binding**: Server-side data is passed as component props\n\n### Modal and Interaction Patterns\n\nComponents use consistent patterns for modal management and user interactions:\n\n```typescript\n// Modal management pattern\nconst $modal = useTemplateRef('$modal');\nconst {show, hide} = useHasModal($modal);\n\n// Expose methods for parent components\ndefineExpose({\n    open: (url: string) =\u003e {\n        reorderUrl.value = url;\n        show();\n    }\n});\n```\n\nSources: [backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml:11-49](), [backend/templates/partials/head.phtml:13-35](), [frontend/functions/useHasModal.ts:1-23](), [frontend/components/Stations/Playlists/ReorderModal.vue:116-130]()\n\n## Build Integration and Asset Management\n\nThe Vue component system integrates with Vite for efficient building and asset management. Components are compiled into optimized chunks with proper dependency management.\n\n### Vite Configuration for Components\n\nThe build system creates multiple entry points based on the page structure:\n\n```javascript\n// Dynamic entry point generation\nconst inputs = glob.sync('./frontend/js/pages/**/*.js').reduce((acc, path) =\u003e {\n    const entry = path.replace(/\\.js$/g, '')\n        .replace(/^frontend\\/js\\/pages\\//g, '')\n        .replace(/\\//g, '');\n    \n    acc[entry] = resolve(__dirname, path)\n    return acc\n}, {});\n\ninputs['Layout'] = resolve(__dirname, './frontend/js/layout.js');\n```\n\n### Chunk Strategy\n\nComponents are organized into logical chunks for optimal loading:\n\n```javascript\nmanualChunks: {\n    vue: ['vue'],\n    lodash: ['lodash'],\n    leaflet: ['leaflet'],\n    hlsjs: ['hls.js'],\n    zxcvbn: ['zxcvbn']\n}\n```\n\n### Asset Loading Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    ViteConfig[\"vite.config.js\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Build Process\"\n        PageEntry[\"Page Entry Points\u003cbr/\u003epages/**/*.js\"]\n        LayoutEntry[\"Layout Entry\u003cbr/\u003elayout.js\"]\n        ComponentScan[\"Component Discovery\u003cbr/\u003eDynamic Imports\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Output Generation\"\n        Manifest[\"manifest.json\u003cbr/\u003eAsset Map\"]\n        ChunkedJS[\"Chunked JS Files\u003cbr/\u003evue-[hash].js\"]\n        ChunkedCSS[\"Chunked CSS Files\u003cbr/\u003ecomponent-[hash].css\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Runtime Loading\"\n        PHPResolver[\"PHP Asset Resolver\"]\n        Preload[\"Module Preload\"]\n        LazyLoad[\"Lazy Component Loading\"]\n    end\n    \n    ViteConfig --\u003e PageEntry\n    ViteConfig --\u003e LayoutEntry\n    ViteConfig --\u003e ComponentScan\n    \n    PageEntry --\u003e Manifest\n    LayoutEntry --\u003e Manifest\n    ComponentScan --\u003e ChunkedJS\n    ComponentScan --\u003e ChunkedCSS\n    \n    Manifest --\u003e PHPResolver\n    PHPResolver --\u003e Preload\n    PHPResolver --\u003e LazyLoad\n```\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:7-17](), [vite.config.js:29-35](), [vite.config.js](vite.config.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document explains the Vite-based frontend build system used in AzuraCast, covering asset compilation, TypeScript integration, and the CI/CD pipeline for frontend development.\n\n## Overview\n\nAzuraCast uses Vite as its primary build tool for compiling Vue.js components, TypeScript files, and SCSS stylesheets. The build system supports both development and production environments with hot module replacement, code splitting, and optimized asset delivery.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Frontend Development\"\n        VueComponents[\"Vue Components (.vue)\"]\n        TypeScriptFiles[\"TypeScript Files (.ts)\"]\n        SCSSFiles[\"SCSS Stylesheets (.scss)\"]\n        JavaScriptEntries[\"JavaScript Entry Points\"]\n        \n        VueComponents --\u003e ViteDevServer[\"vite dev server\"]\n        TypeScriptFiles --\u003e ViteDevServer\n        SCSSFiles --\u003e ViteDevServer\n        JavaScriptEntries --\u003e ViteDevServer\n        \n        ViteDevServer --\u003e HMR[\"Hot Module Replacement\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Production Build\"\n        VueComponents --\u003e ViteBuild[\"vite build\"]\n        TypeScriptFiles --\u003e ViteBuild\n        SCSSFiles --\u003e ViteBuild\n        JavaScriptEntries --\u003e ViteBuild\n        \n        ViteBuild --\u003e CompiledJS[\"Compiled JavaScript\"]\n        ViteBuild --\u003e CompiledCSS[\"Compiled CSS\"]\n        ViteBuild --\u003e AssetManifest[\"Asset Manifest\"]\n        \n        CompiledJS --\u003e ViteDistDir[\"web/static/vite_dist/\"]\n        CompiledCSS --\u003e ViteDistDir\n        AssetManifest --\u003e ViteDistDir\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Template Integration\"\n        ViteDistDir --\u003e getVueComponentInfo[\"getVueComponentInfo()\"]\n        getVueComponentInfo --\u003e TemplateAssets[\"Template Asset Loading\"]\n    end\n```\n\nSources: [vite.config.js](), [package.json](), [frontend/]()\n\n## Vite Configuration\n\nThe build system is configured in `vite.config.js` with automatic entry point discovery, optimized chunking, and plugin integration.\n\n### Entry Point Discovery\n\nVite automatically discovers page entry points using glob patterns:\n\n```javascript\nconst inputs = glob.sync('./frontend/js/pages/**/*.js').reduce((acc, path) =\u003e {\n    const entry = path.replace(/\\.js$/g, '')\n        .replace(/^frontend\\/js\\/pages\\//g, '')\n        .replace(/\\//g, '');\n    acc[entry] = resolve(__dirname, path)\n    return acc\n}, {});\ninputs['Layout'] = resolve(__dirname, './frontend/js/layout.js');\n```\n\nThis creates entry points for each page component and includes the main layout file.\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:7-17]()\n\n### Manual Chunking Strategy\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Code Splitting Strategy\"\n        EntryPoints[\"Page Entry Points\"]\n        \n        EntryPoints --\u003e VueChunk[\"vue chunk\"]\n        EntryPoints --\u003e LodashChunk[\"lodash chunk\"] \n        EntryPoints --\u003e LeafletChunk[\"leaflet chunk\"]\n        EntryPoints --\u003e HlsJsChunk[\"hlsjs chunk\"]\n        EntryPoints --\u003e ZxcvbnChunk[\"zxcvbn chunk\"]\n        EntryPoints --\u003e TranslationChunks[\"translations-{locale}-{hash}.js\"]\n        \n        VueChunk --\u003e OptimizedLoading[\"Optimized Loading\"]\n        LodashChunk --\u003e OptimizedLoading\n        LeafletChunk --\u003e OptimizedLoading\n        HlsJsChunk --\u003e OptimizedLoading\n        ZxcvbnChunk --\u003e OptimizedLoading\n        TranslationChunks --\u003e OptimizedLoading\n    end\n```\n\nThe manual chunking configuration optimizes loading by separating frequently used libraries into their own chunks, enabling better caching and parallel loading.\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:29-58]()\n\n### Build Commands\n\nThe npm scripts provide comprehensive development and build tooling:\n\n| Command | Purpose | Description |\n|---------|---------|-------------|\n| `vite build` | Production build | Compiles optimized assets to `web/static/vite_dist/` |\n| `vite` | Development server | Starts dev server with HMR on port 5173 |\n| `eslint frontend` | Code linting | Runs ESLint on frontend TypeScript/Vue files |\n| `vue-tsc` | Type checking | Validates TypeScript types without emitting code |\n| `node frontend/genicons.mjs` | Icon generation | Generates icon components |\n| `vue-gettext-extract` | Locale extraction | Extracts translatable strings |\n| `node frontend/genapi.mjs` | API interface generation | Generates TypeScript interfaces from OpenAPI |\n\nSources: [package.json:5-13]()\n\n### Asset Output Configuration\n\nVite outputs compiled assets to `web/static/vite_dist/` with specific naming strategies:\n\n- Translation chunks: `translations-{locale}-{hash}.js`\n- Vue component chunks: `{componentName}-{hash}.js`\n- General chunks: `{name}-{hash}.js`\n- Manifest file for template integration\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:64](), [vite.config.js:36-58]()\n\n### Development Mode Integration\n\nDevelopment mode includes HMR client integration in templates:\n\n```php\nif ($environment-\u003eisDevelopment()) {\n    echo \u003c\u003c\u003cHTML\n        \u003cscript type=\"module\" src=\"/static/vite_dist/@vite/client\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\n    HTML;\n}\n```\n\nThis enables hot module replacement for Vue components, TypeScript files, and stylesheets during development.\n\nSources: [backend/templates/partials/head.phtml:7-11]()\n\n## TypeScript Integration\n\nAzuraCast uses TypeScript extensively throughout its Vue.js frontend with comprehensive type checking and generation tools.\n\n### TypeScript Configuration\n\nThe project uses `vue-tsc` for type checking Vue single-file components and TypeScript files. Type checking is integrated into both development workflow and CI/CD pipeline.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"TypeScript Ecosystem\"\n        VueSFC[\"Vue Single File Components (.vue)\"]\n        TSFiles[\"TypeScript Files (.ts)\"]\n        TypeDefinitions[\"Type Definitions (@types/*)\"]\n        \n        VueSFC --\u003e VueTsc[\"vue-tsc\"]\n        TSFiles --\u003e VueTsc\n        TypeDefinitions --\u003e VueTsc\n        \n        VueTsc --\u003e TypeChecking[\"Type Validation\"]\n        \n        OpenAPISpec[\"OpenAPI Specification\"] --\u003e SwaggerTypeScriptAPI[\"swagger-typescript-api\"]\n        SwaggerTypeScriptAPI --\u003e GeneratedInterfaces[\"Generated API Interfaces\"]\n        \n        GeneratedInterfaces --\u003e TypeChecking\n    end\n```\n\n### API Interface Generation\n\nFrontend TypeScript interfaces are automatically generated from the OpenAPI specification:\n\n```javascript\nvoid generateApi({\n    fileName: \"ApiInterfaces.ts\",\n    output: path.resolve(__dirname, \"./entities\"),\n    url: \"http://localhost/api/openapi.yml\",\n    generateClient: false,\n    generateUnionEnums: false,\n    addReadonly: true\n});\n```\n\nThis ensures type safety between frontend and backend API contracts.\n\nSources: [frontend/genapi.mjs:6-13]()\n\n### Type Checking in Development\n\nTypeScript validation runs in multiple contexts:\n\n- **Development**: `vue-tsc` provides real-time type checking\n- **CI/CD**: Type checking runs as part of the Node test suite\n- **Build**: Vite performs type checking during compilation\n\nThe ESLint configuration includes TypeScript-specific rules for code quality and consistency.\n\nSources: [package.json:9](), [eslint.config.mjs]()\n\n## Asset Compilation Process\n\nThe build system compiles various frontend assets through specialized processing pipelines optimized for performance and developer experience.\n\n### Vue Component Processing\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Vue SFC Processing\"\n        VueSFC[\"Vue Single File Components\"]\n        VueSFC --\u003e ScriptSetup[\"\u003cscript setup\u003e blocks\"]\n        VueSFC --\u003e Template[\"\u003ctemplate\u003e blocks\"]  \n        VueSFC --\u003e StyleBlocks[\"\u003cstyle\u003e blocks\"]\n        \n        ScriptSetup --\u003e TypeScriptCompiler[\"TypeScript Compiler\"]\n        Template --\u003e VueTemplateCompiler[\"Vue Template Compiler\"]\n        StyleBlocks --\u003e SassProcessor[\"Sass Processor\"]\n        \n        TypeScriptCompiler --\u003e CompiledJS[\"Compiled JavaScript\"]\n        VueTemplateCompiler --\u003e CompiledJS\n        SassProcessor --\u003e CompiledCSS[\"Compiled CSS\"]\n        \n        CompiledJS --\u003e CodeSplitting[\"Code Splitting\"]\n        CompiledCSS --\u003e AssetOptimization[\"Asset Optimization\"]\n    end\n```\n\nVue components are processed through the `@vitejs/plugin-vue` plugin, which handles template compilation, script processing, and style extraction.\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:94](), [package.json]()\n\n### SCSS Preprocessing\n\nThe build system includes SCSS preprocessing with specific configurations:\n\n- `quietDeps: true` - Suppresses dependency warnings\n- `silenceDeprecations` - Handles legacy API deprecations\n- Bootstrap integration through `bootstrap` dependency\n\nThis enables use of advanced SCSS features while maintaining compatibility with Bootstrap's styling system.\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:66-73]()\n\n### Code Splitting and Optimization\n\nThe build process implements strategic code splitting:\n\n1. **Library Chunks**: Separate chunks for `vue`, `lodash`, `leaflet`, `hls.js`, and `zxcvbn`\n2. **Translation Chunks**: Dynamic chunks for each locale: `translations-{locale}-{hash}.js`\n3. **Component Chunks**: Individual chunks for large Vue components\n4. **Entry Point Chunks**: Separate chunks for each page entry point\n\nThis strategy enables efficient caching, parallel loading, and reduced bundle sizes.\n\nSources: [vite.config.js:29-58]()\n\n## Frontend CI/CD Pipeline\n\nThe GitHub Actions workflow includes specialized frontend testing and build processes integrated with the overall deployment pipeline.\n\n### Frontend Testing Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Frontend Test Suite\"\n        NodeSetup[\"setup-node@v4\"]\n        NodeSetup --\u003e NodeCache[\"npm cache\"]\n        NodeCache --\u003e NpmCi[\"npm ci --include=dev\"]\n        \n        NpmCi --\u003e TypeScriptCheck[\"npm run tsc\"]\n        NpmCi --\u003e ESLintCheck[\"npm run lint\"]\n        \n        TypeScriptCheck --\u003e TestResults[\"Test Results\"]\n        ESLintCheck --\u003e TestResults\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Asset Build Process\"\n        NodeEnvProduction[\"NODE_ENV=production\"]\n        NodeEnvProduction --\u003e NpmCiBuild[\"npm ci --include=dev\"]\n        NpmCiBuild --\u003e LocaleGeneration[\"locale:generate\"]\n        LocaleGeneration --\u003e CrowdinSync[\"Crowdin Translation Sync\"]\n        CrowdinSync --\u003e LocaleImport[\"locale:import\"]\n        LocaleImport --\u003e NpmBuild[\"npm run build\"]\n        \n        NpmBuild --\u003e StaticAssets[\"web/static/vite_dist\"]\n        NpmBuild --\u003e TranslationFiles[\"translations/\"]\n    end\n```\n\n### Node Test Suite Configuration\n\nThe Node test suite runs on every pull request and push:\n\n```yaml\nnode-test:\n  runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n  steps:\n    - uses: actions/checkout@master\n    - name: Setup Node\n      uses: actions/setup-node@v4\n      with:\n        node-version: \"lts/jod\"\n        cache: \"npm\"\n    - run: npm ci --include=dev\n    - name: Check TypeScript types\n      run: npm run tsc\n    - name: Generate linting report\n      run: npm run lint\n```\n\nThis ensures TypeScript type safety and code quality standards before merging.\n\nSources: [.github/workflows/default.yml:66-84]()\n\n### Asset Preparation Process\n\nThe prepare job builds production assets:\n\n1. **Environment Setup**: Node LTS with npm caching\n2. **Translation Generation**: Extracts strings from Vue components and TypeScript\n3. **Crowdin Integration**: Syncs translations with external service\n4. **Asset Compilation**: Runs `npm run build` for production assets\n5. **Artifact Upload**: Packages built assets for deployment\n\nThe build outputs are uploaded as GitHub Actions artifacts:\n\n```yaml\n- name: Upload built static assets and translations\n  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4\n  with:\n    name: assets\n    path: |\n      .gitinfo\n      translations\n      web/static/vite_dist\n      web/static/api/openapi.yml\n```\n\nSources: [.github/workflows/default.yml:147-170]()\n\n### Build Optimization\n\nThe CI/CD pipeline optimizes builds through:\n\n- **Dependency Caching**: npm cache for faster installs\n- **Parallel Execution**: Frontend and backend tests run simultaneously\n- **Artifact Reuse**: Built assets passed between jobs\n- **Conditional Execution**: Build jobs only run on pushes, not PRs\n\nSources: [.github/workflows/default.yml:24-26](), [.github/workflows/default.yml:95]()\n\n## Template Integration\n\nThe build process generates assets that are integrated into templates to serve the application.\n\n### Asset Loading\n\nTemplates load the compiled assets based on the current environment:\n\n```php\n$componentDeps = $this-\u003egetVueComponentInfo('js/layout.js');\n\necho \u003c\u003c\u003cHTML\n    \u003cscript type=\"module\" src=\"{$componentDeps['js']}\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\nHTML;\n\nforeach ($componentDeps['prefetch'] as $prefetchDep) {\n    echo \u003c\u003c\u003cHTML\n        \u003clink rel=\"modulepreload\" href=\"{$prefetchDep}\" /\u003e\n    HTML;\n}\n\nforeach ($componentDeps['css'] as $cssDep) {\n    echo \u003c\u003c\u003cHTML\n        \u003clink rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"{$cssDep}\" /\u003e\n    HTML;\n}\n```\n\nSources: [backend/templates/partials/head.phtml](), [backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml]()\n\n### Vue Component Loading\n\nFor Vue components, the templates load and initialize the components with their props:\n\n```php\n$componentDeps = $this-\u003egetVueComponentInfo('js/pages/' . $component . '.js');\n\n$scriptLines[] = \u003c\u003c\u003cHTML\n    \u003cscript type=\"module\" src=\"{$componentDeps['js']}\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\n    \u003cscript type=\"text/javascript\"\u003e\n        let {$componentName};\n        ready(() =\u003e {\n            {$componentName} = window.vueComponent('#{$id}', {$propsJson});\n        });\n    \u003c/script\u003e\nHTML;\n```\n\nSources: [backend/templates/partials/vue_body.phtml:39-47]()\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe AzuraCast build process is a comprehensive system that manages frontend and backend dependencies, compiles assets, and packages everything for both development and production environments. The use of modern tools like Vite, TypeScript, Vue.js, and Docker ensures that the application is both developer-friendly and production-ready.\n\nFor information about the frontend Vue components, [vite.config.js](vite.config.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe Audio Player system provides comprehensive audio playback functionality for AzuraCast's frontend, supporting both standard audio streams and HTTP Live Streaming (HLS). This component handles stream playback, volume control, progress tracking, and cross-tab synchronization to ensure consistent audio playback across the entire application.\n\nFor information about the streaming engine that generates the audio content, see [Streaming Engine](#3). For details about the Vue.js component architecture, see [Vue Components](#5.1).\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nThe audio player system is built around a central Vue component that integrates with multiple browser APIs and external libraries to provide robust audio streaming capabilities.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"AudioPlayer Component\"\n        AudioElement[\"HTML Audio Element\"]\n        HlsInstance[\"HLS.js Instance\"]\n        VolumeControl[\"Volume Control\"]\n        ProgressTracking[\"Progress Tracking\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"State Management\"\n        PlayerStore[\"usePlayerStore\"]\n        LocalState[\"Component State\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Browser APIs\"\n        MediaSession[\"MediaSession API\"]\n        BroadcastChannel[\"BroadcastChannel API\"]\n        AudioContext[\"Web Audio API\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"External Libraries\"\n        HlsJs[\"hls.js Library\"]\n        LogarithmicVolume[\"getLogarithmicVolume\"]\n    end\n    \n    AudioElement --\u003e HlsInstance\n    AudioElement --\u003e VolumeControl\n    AudioElement --\u003e ProgressTracking\n    \n    PlayerStore --\u003e AudioElement\n    LocalState --\u003e AudioElement\n    \n    AudioElement --\u003e MediaSession\n    AudioElement --\u003e BroadcastChannel\n    \n    HlsInstance --\u003e HlsJs\n    VolumeControl --\u003e LogarithmicVolume\n    \n    MediaSession --\u003e AudioElement\n    BroadcastChannel --\u003e PlayerStore\n```\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:1-216](), [frontend/functions/usePlayerStore.ts]()\n\n## Core Audio Player Component\n\nThe main audio player component is implemented as a Vue 3 composition API component that manages audio playback state and interactions with various browser APIs.\n\n### Component Structure\n\n| Property | Type | Purpose |\n|----------|------|---------|\n| `title` | string | Audio element title attribute |\n| `volume` | number | Playback volume (0-100) |\n| `isMuted` | boolean | Mute state |\n\n| Event | Payload | Description |\n|-------|---------|-------------|\n| `update:duration` | number | Total audio duration |\n| `update:currentTime` | number | Current playback position |\n| `update:progress` | number | Playback progress percentage |\n\nThe component uses template refs to access the HTML audio element and manages HLS instances for adaptive streaming support.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:16-32](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:34-40]()\n\n## Audio Stream Support\n\nThe audio player supports multiple streaming formats through conditional loading based on stream type.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    PlayRequest[\"play() Function Called\"]\n    CheckStreamType[\"Check current.value.isHls\"]\n    \n    HlsPath[\"HLS Stream Path\"]\n    StandardPath[\"Standard Stream Path\"]\n    \n    HlsSupported{\"Hls.isSupported()\"}\n    NativeHls{\"canPlayType('application/vnd.apple.mpegurl')\"}\n    \n    CreateHlsInstance[\"Create HLS Instance\"]\n    LoadHlsSource[\"hls.loadSource()\"]\n    AttachMedia[\"hls.attachMedia()\"]\n    \n    SetAudioSrc[\"Set audio.src\"]\n    FirefoxWorkaround[\"Add cache-busting parameter\"]\n    \n    LoadAndPlay[\"audio.load() + audio.play()\"]\n    \n    PlayRequest --\u003e CheckStreamType\n    CheckStreamType --\u003e|true| HlsPath\n    CheckStreamType --\u003e|false| StandardPath\n    \n    HlsPath --\u003e HlsSupported\n    HlsSupported --\u003e|true| CreateHlsInstance\n    HlsSupported --\u003e|false| NativeHls\n    NativeHls --\u003e|true| SetAudioSrc\n    NativeHls --\u003e|false| LoadAndPlay\n    \n    CreateHlsInstance --\u003e LoadHlsSource\n    LoadHlsSource --\u003e AttachMedia\n    AttachMedia --\u003e LoadAndPlay\n    \n    StandardPath --\u003e SetAudioSrc\n    SetAudioSrc --\u003e FirefoxWorkaround\n    FirefoxWorkaround --\u003e LoadAndPlay\n```\n\n### HLS Stream Handling\n\nFor HLS streams, the player uses the `hls.js` library to provide adaptive bitrate streaming support across browsers that don't natively support HLS.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:114-134]()\n\n### Standard Stream Handling\n\nStandard audio streams are handled directly by the HTML audio element, with Firefox-specific cache-busting to prevent playback issues.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:125-134]()\n\n## Volume Control and Audio Processing\n\nThe audio player implements logarithmic volume scaling to provide more natural volume adjustment that matches human hearing perception.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    VolumeProps[\"Volume Props (0-100)\"]\n    LogarithmicFunction[\"getLogarithmicVolume()\"]\n    AudioElementVolume[\"audio.volume (0.0-1.0)\"]\n    \n    VolumeProps --\u003e LogarithmicFunction\n    LogarithmicFunction --\u003e AudioElementVolume\n    \n    MuteProps[\"isMuted Props\"]\n    AudioElementMuted[\"audio.muted\"]\n    \n    MuteProps --\u003e AudioElementMuted\n```\n\nVolume changes are watched reactively and applied immediately to the audio element when the component props change.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:44-54](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:111-112]()\n\n## Cross-Tab Synchronization\n\nThe audio player implements cross-tab synchronization using the BroadcastChannel API to ensure only one instance plays audio at a time across multiple browser tabs.\n\n### BroadcastChannel Implementation\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Tab1 as \"Audio Player Tab 1\"\n    participant BC as \"BroadcastChannel 'audio_player'\"\n    participant Tab2 as \"Audio Player Tab 2\"\n    \n    Tab1-\u003e\u003eBC: postMessage('played')\n    BC-\u003e\u003eTab2: message event\n    Tab2-\u003e\u003eTab2: storeStop()\n    Tab2-\u003e\u003eTab2: Audio stops\n```\n\nWhen audio starts playing in one tab, it broadcasts a message that causes other tabs to stop their audio playback.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:139-141](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:197-202]()\n\n## Media Session Integration\n\nThe player integrates with the browser's Media Session API to provide native media controls and metadata display.\n\n### Media Session Features\n\n| Feature | Implementation | Purpose |\n|---------|----------------|---------|\n| Pause Handler | `mediaSession.setActionHandler('pause')` | Handle hardware/OS pause buttons |\n| Metadata Display | Native browser media notification | Show currently playing information |\n\nThe Media Session API allows users to control playback using hardware media keys, lock screen controls, and system media notifications.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:191-195]()\n\n## Player State Management\n\nThe audio player integrates with a centralized player store for application-wide state management.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"usePlayerStore\"\n        IsPlaying[\"isPlaying: Ref\u003cboolean\u003e\"]\n        Current[\"current: Ref\u003cPlaybackInfo\u003e\"]\n        StopFunction[\"stop(): Function\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"AudioPlayer Component\"\n        LocalDuration[\"duration: Ref\u003cnumber\u003e\"]\n        LocalCurrentTime[\"currentTime: Ref\u003cnumber\u003e\"]\n        LocalProgress[\"computed progress\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"External Events\"\n        PropsChange[\"Props Change\"]\n        CurrentChange[\"current.value Change\"]\n        AudioEvents[\"Audio Element Events\"]\n    end\n    \n    IsPlaying --\u003e AudioPlayer\n    Current --\u003e AudioPlayer\n    AudioPlayer --\u003e StopFunction\n    \n    AudioEvents --\u003e LocalDuration\n    AudioEvents --\u003e LocalCurrentTime\n    LocalDuration --\u003e LocalProgress\n    LocalCurrentTime --\u003e LocalProgress\n    \n    PropsChange --\u003e AudioPlayer\n    CurrentChange --\u003e AudioPlayer\n```\n\n### State Synchronization\n\nThe component watches the `current` value from the player store and automatically starts or stops playback based on whether a URL is provided.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:40-41](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:145-151]()\n\n## Error Handling and Recovery\n\nThe audio player implements automatic recovery for network interruptions and provides graceful error handling.\n\n### Network Error Recovery\n\nWhen a network error occurs during streaming, the player automatically attempts to reconnect after a 5-second delay.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:88-98]()\n\n### Cleanup and Resource Management\n\nThe component properly cleans up resources when stopping playback or when the component is unmounted.\n\n**Sources:** [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:56-70](), [frontend/components/Common/AudioPlayer.vue:205-209]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2b:T2edd,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Development environment setup, development workflows, testing procedures, and CI/CD pipeline information. For installation and deployment information, see [Installation and Deployment](#2). For API development details, see [API and Integration](#4).\n\n## Development Environment Overview\n\nAzuraCast uses a containerized development environment built on Docker with comprehensive tooling for code quality, testing, and automated deployment. The development setup supports both local development and automated CI/CD workflows.\n\n### Development Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Local Development Environment\"\n        DevContainer[\"development Docker container\u003cbr/\u003eAPPLICATION_ENV=development\u003cbr/\u003ePROFILING_EXTENSION_ENABLED=1\"]\n        LocalDB[\"MariaDB 11.8.2\u003cbr/\u003eLocal Database\"]\n analyze\u003cbr/\u003e--memory-limit=-1\"]\n        Testing[\"Testing Framework\u003cbr/\u003ecodeception\u003cbr/\u003ephpunit\"]\n    end\n    \n    DevContainer --\u003e LocalDB\n    DevContainer --\u003e LocalRedis\n    DevContainer --\u003e NodeTools\n    DevContainer --\u003e Composer\n    Composer --\u003e PHPTools\n    NodeTools --\u003e BuildTools\n    PHPTools --\u003e Linting\n    PHPTools --\u003e StaticAnalysis\n    PHPTools --\u003e Testing\n```\n\n**Development Environment Components**\n\nThe development environment is configured through the `development` stage in the Dockerfile, which enables debugging tools and development-specific configurations.\n\nSources: [Dockerfile:135-167](), [docker-compose.testing.yml:1-11](), [composer.json:116-138]()\n\n### Development Dependencies and Scripts\n\nAzuraCast includes extensive development tooling managed through Composer and npm:\n\n| Tool Category | Tools | Purpose |\n|---------------|-------|---------|\n| **Static Analysis** | PHPStan, Doctrine PHPStan Extension | Type checking and code analysis |\n| **Code Quality** | PHP_CodeSniffer, Slevomat Coding Standard | Code style enforcement |\n| **Testing** | Codeception, PHPUnit, Mockery | Unit and integration testing |\n| **Build Tools** | Vite, TypeScript, npm | Frontend asset compilation |\n| **Development** | Whoops, PsySh, Symfony VarDumper | Debugging and development |\n\n**Composer Development Scripts**\n\nThe `composer.json` defines several development workflow scripts:\n\n- `dev-test`: Runs linting, static analysis, and code style checks\n- `cleanup-and-test`: Fixes code style issues and runs tests\n- `phplint`: PHP syntax validation using `parallel-lint`\n- `phpstan`: Static analysis with memory optimization\n- `phpcs`/`phpcbf`: Code style checking and fixing\n- `codeception`: Full test suite with coverage\n\nSources: [composer.json:176-201](), [phpstan.neon:1-43]()\n\n## CI/CD Pipeline Architecture\n\nAzuraCast uses GitHub Actions for continuous integration and deployment with a multi-stage pipeline that handles testing, building, and publishing.\n\n### GitHub Actions Workflow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Trigger Events\"\n        PullRequest[\"Pull Request\u003cbr/\u003eto main branch\"]\n        PushEvent[\"Push to branches\u003cbr/\u003emain, stable, ci-testing\"]\n        TagEvent[\"Tag Creation\u003cbr/\u003eVersion releases\"]\n        ScheduleEvent[\"Scheduled Build\u003cbr/\u003eWeekly on Sundays\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Test --include=dev\"]\n        PHPLint[\"PHP Linting\u003cbr/\u003eparallel-lint --checkstyle\"]\n        PHPStan[\"PHPStan Analysis\u003cbr/\u003e--memory-limit=-1 --error-format=checkstyle\"]\n        PHPCS[\"PHP CodeSniffer\u003cbr/\u003e--report=checkstyle\"]\n        TypeScript[\"TypeScript Check\u003cbr/\u003enpm run tsc\"]\n        ESLint[\"ESLint Check\u003cbr/\u003enpm run lint\"]\n    end\n DockerPush\n```\n\n**Pipeline Configuration Details**\n\nThe CI/CD pipeline is configured in `.github/workflows/default.yml` with specific job dependencies and build matrices. The pipeline only runs the prepare and build stages for push events and scheduled builds, while pull requests only run the testing stage.\n\nSources: [.github/workflows/default.yml:1-227]()\n\n### Build and Deployment Process\n\nThe build process creates multi-platform Docker images using Depot for enhanced build performance:\n\n1. **Asset Preparation**: Frontend assets are built using Vite and npm\n2. **Translation Management**: Crowdin integration for internationalization\n3. **Documentation Generation**: OpenAPI specifications are generated automatically\n4. **Docker Image Building**: Multi-platform images for `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64`\n5. **Registry Publishing**: Images pushed to Docker Hub and GitHub Container Registry\n\nThe build process uses Docker's multi-stage builds with separate stages for development and production environments.\n\nSources: [Dockerfile:171-188](), [.github/workflows/default.yml:172-227]()\n\n## Development Workflow and Code Quality\n\n### Local Development Setup\n\nThe development environment runs in a Docker container with all necessary services and development tools pre-installed:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Development Container Services\"\n        WebService[\"Web Service\u003cbr/\u003ePHP-FPM 8.4\u003cbr/\u003enginx\"]\n        Database[\"MariaDB Database\u003cbr/\u003eLocal instance\"]\n        Cache[\"Redis Composer[\"Composer\u003cbr/\u003eDevelopment dependencies\"]\n        NPM[\"npm\u003cbr/\u003eFrontend tools\"]\n        Debugging[\"Debugging Tools\u003cbr/\u003eXdebug enabled\u003cbr/\u003eWhoops error handler\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Code Quality Gates\"\n        PreCommit[\"Pre-commit Checks\u003cbr/\u003ephplint\u003cbr/\u003ephpstan\u003cbr/\u003ephpcs\"]\n        Testing[\"Test Execution\u003cbr/\u003ecodeception\u003cbr/\u003eUnit tests\u003cbr/\u003eIntegration tests\"]\n        Coverage[\"Code Coverage\u003cbr/\u003e--coverage options\"]\n    end\n    \n    WebService --\u003e Composer\n    WebService --\u003e NPM\n    WebService --\u003e Debugging\n    Composer --\u003e PreCommit\n    PreCommit --\u003e Testing\n    Testing --\u003e Coverage\n```\n\n**Development Environment Configuration**\n\nThe development Docker stage enables debugging features and development-specific settings:\n\n- `APPLICATION_ENV=development`\n- `PROFILING_EXTENSION_ENABLED=1`\n- `ENABLE_WEB_UPDATER=false`\n- Development dependencies included via Composer\n- Source code mounted for live editing\n\nSources: [Dockerfile:135-167](), [backend/config/services.php:125-128]()\n\n### Code Quality Standards\n\nAzuraCast enforces strict code quality standards through automated tooling:\n\n**PHPStan Configuration**\n- Level 8 static analysis\n- Doctrine ORM integration\n- Custom stubs for dependency injection\n- Parallel processing with job size optimization\n\n**PHP CodeSniffer Standards**\n- Slevomat Coding Standard\n- Custom rule configurations\n- Automated fix capabilities via `phpcbf`\n\n**Testing Framework**\n- Codeception for acceptance and functional testing\n- PHPUnit for unit testing\n- Mockery for mocking dependencies\n- Code coverage reporting\n\nSources: [phpstan.neon:1-43](), [composer.json:189-200]()\n\n## Contributing Guidelines\n\n### Issue Reporting and Feature Requests\n\nAzuraCast uses structured issue templates to ensure proper bug reporting and feature requests:\n\n**Bug Report Requirements**\n- Installation method (Docker/Ansible/One-click)\n- Release channel (Rolling/Stable)\n- Current version information\n- Detailed reproduction steps\n- Relevant log output\n\n**Feature Request Process**\n- Feature requests handled via FeatureUpvote platform\n- GitHub issues reserved for bug reports only\n- Community voting system for prioritization\n\nSources: [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml:1-64](), [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml:1-23]()\n\n### Development Standards and Practices\n\n**Code Standards**\n- PHP 8.4+ requirement with strict typing\n- Symfony component integration patterns\n- Doctrine ORM entity management\n- PSR compliance for HTTP handling\n\n**Testing Requirements**\n- Unit test coverage for new functionality\n- Integration tests for API endpoints\n- Functional tests for user workflows\n- Performance testing for resource-intensive operations\n\n**Documentation Standards**\n- Inline code documentation\n- API documentation via OpenAPI\n- Wiki documentation for major features\n- Changelog maintenance for releases\n\nSources: [composer.json:12-98](), [backend/src/Console/Command/MigrateDbCommand.php:1-91]()\n\n### Development Security Considerations\n\nAzuraCast implements several security measures in the development workflow:\n\n- Security advisory monitoring via `roave/security-advisories`\n- Dependency vulnerability scanning\n- Code security analysis through static analysis tools\n- Container security best practices in Docker builds\n\nThe development environment includes security-focused tooling to identify potential vulnerabilities during the development process.\n\nSources: [composer.json:134](), AzuraCast\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n-
