comprehensive guidelines for contributing to the AzuraCast project, including bug reporting, feature requests, code contributions, and community participation. It covers the structured processes, templates, and channels used to manage contributions and support within the AzuraCast ecosystem.\n\nFor information about setting up a development environment, see [Development Environment Setup](#6.2). For details about testing and CI/CD processes, see [Testing and CI/CD](#6.3).\n\n## Getting Started\n\nAzuraCast is a free and open-source project built and maintained by volunteers. The project welcomes contributions from developers, translators, documentation writers, and community members. All contributions are managed through GitHub and follow established workflows to ensure quality and consistency.\n\nThe project operates under the [Affero GNU General Public License (AGPL) version 3.0]()[README.md:45-46] and follows ethical open-source principles. Contributors are expected to be kind and patient in their interactions, recognizing that the core team consists of volunteers working on donations.\n\n## Community Guidelines and Support Channels\n\n### Code of Conduct\n\nContributors are expected to maintain professional and respectful interactions. The project emphasizes patience and kindness, particularly given the volunteer nature of the development team [README.md:68-70]. \n\n### Support Channels\n\nAzuraCast maintains multiple community channels for different types of interactions:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Community Channels\"\n        Discord[\"Discord Community\u003cbr/\u003ediscord.gg/azuracast\u003cbr/\u003eGeneral Discussion\u003cbr/\u003eStation Sharing\"]\n        Discussions[\"GitHub Discussions\u003cbr/\u003eHelp \u0026 Support\u003cbr/\u003eCommunity Q\u0026A\"]\n        \n        Social[\"Social Media\u003cbr/\u003e@azuracast.com (Bluesky)\u003cbr/\u003e@AzuraCast@floss.social (Mastodon)\u003cbr/\u003eUpdates \u0026 Announcements\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Development Channels\"\n        Issues[\"GitHub Issues\u003cbr/\u003eBug Reports\u003cbr/\u003eError Tracking\u003cbr/\u003eTechnical Problems\"]\n        FeatureUpvote[\"FeatureUpvote\u003cbr/\u003efeatures.azuracast.com\u003cbr/\u003eFeature Requests\u003cbr/\u003eCommunity Voting\"]\n        Translations[\"CrowdIn\u003cbr/\u003etranslate.azuracast.com\u003cbr/\u003eLocalization\u003cbr/\u003eLanguage Support\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Documentation\"\n        TroubleshootingGuide[\"Support Documentation\u003cbr/\u003edocs.azuracast.com\u003cbr/\u003eCommon Solutions\u003cbr/\u003eLog Analysis\"]\n        KnownIssues[\"Known Issues\u003cbr/\u003eKNOWN-ISSUES.md\u003cbr/\u003eUpstream Problems\u003cbr/\u003eWorkarounds\"]\n    end\n    \n    Discord --\u003e Discussions\n    Issues --\u003e TroubleshootingGuide\n    Issues --\u003e KnownIssues\n    FeatureUpvote --\u003e Issues\n```\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:72-89](), [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml:3-22]()\n\n## Bug Reporting Process\n\n### Issue Templates and Structure\n\nBug reports are managed through GitHub Issues using structured templates to ensure consistent information collection. The project provides specific templates for different types of reports:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    BugReport[\"Bug Encountered\"]\n    CheckDocs[\"Check Documentation\u003cbr/\u003edocs.azuracast.com/troubleshooting\"]\n    CheckKnown[\"Check Known Issues\u003cbr/\u003eKNOWN-ISSUES.md\"]\n    SearchIssues[\"Search Existing Issues\u003cbr/\u003eGitHub Issues Board\"]\n    \n    CreateIssue[\"Create New Issue\u003cbr/\u003ebug_report.yml Template\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Required Information\"\n        InstallMethod[\"Installation Method\u003cbr/\u003eDocker/Ansible/One-Click\"]\n        ReleaseChannel[\"Release Channel\u003cbr/\u003eRolling/Stable\"]\n        Version[\"Current Version\u003cbr/\u003eFooter Display\"]\n        Description[\"Detailed Description\u003cbr/\u003eExpected vs Actual Behavior\"]\n        Logs[\"Relevant Log Output\u003cbr/\u003eError Messages\"]\n    end\n    \n    IssueCreated[\"Issue Created\u003cbr/\u003eTeam Investigation\"]\n    Resolution[\"Resolution \u0026 Tracking\"]\n    \n    BugReport --\u003e CheckDocs\n    CheckDocs --\u003e CheckKnown\n    CheckKnown --\u003e SearchIssues\n    SearchIssues --\u003e CreateIssue\n    CreateIssue --\u003e InstallMethod\n    CreateIssue --\u003e ReleaseChannel\n    CreateIssue --\u003e Version\n    CreateIssue --\u003e Description\n    CreateIssue --\u003e Logs\n    InstallMethod --\u003e IssueCreated\n    ReleaseChannel --\u003e IssueCreated\n    Version --\u003e IssueCreated\n    Description --\u003e IssueCreated\n    Logs --\u003e IssueCreated\n    IssueCreated --\u003e Resolution\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml:1-64](), [README.md:52-66]()\n\n### Bug Report Requirements\n\nThe bug reporting template [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml:15-35]() requires specific information:\n\n| Field | Options | Purpose |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| Installation Method | Docker, Ansible, One-Click | Determines support level |\n| Release Channel | Rolling, Stable | Version context |\n| Current Version | Specific version string | Update status verification |\n| Description | Detailed explanation | Problem understanding |\n| Log Output | Formatted shell output | Technical diagnosis |\n\n### Known Issues and Limitations\n\nBefore reporting bugs, contributors should review [KNOWN-ISSUES.md:1-48]() which documents acknowledged problems:\n\n- **Metadata Updates with Live DJs**: Upstream Liquidsoap issue affecting first song detection\n- **WebDJ Audio Quality**: Browser-specific crackling issues \n- **Playlist Scheduling**: Intermittent AutoDJ scheduler problems\n- **Ansible Support**: Limited official support for Ansible installations\n\n**Sources:** [KNOWN-ISSUES.md:6-47]()\n\n## Feature Request Process\n\nFeature requests are managed through a dedicated FeatureUpvote platform rather than GitHub Issues. This system allows community voting and prioritization of new features.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    FeatureIdea[\"Feature Idea\"]\n    FeatureUpvote[\"FeatureUpvote Platform\u003cbr/\u003efeatures.azuracast.com\"]\n    CommunityVoting[\"Community Voting\u003cbr/\u003ePriority Ranking\"]\n    Development[\"Development Planning\u003cbr/\u003eTeam Review\"]\n    Implementation[\"Implementation\u003cbr/\u003eGitHub Pull Request\"]\n    \n    FeatureIdea --\u003e FeatureUpvote\n    FeatureUpvote --\u003e CommunityVoting\n    CommunityVoting --\u003e Development\n    Development --\u003e Implementation\n```\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:58-59](), [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml:12-14]()\n\n## Code Contributions\n\n### Pull Request Guidelines\n\nCode contributions follow standard GitHub workflows with specific requirements for the AzuraCast project:\n\n1. **Fork Repository**: Create personal fork of the main repository\n2. **Feature Branch**: Create focused branches for specific changes\n3. **Code Quality**: Follow existing code standards and patterns\n4. **Testing**: Ensure changes don't break existing functionality\n5. **Documentation**: Update relevant documentation for changes\n\n### Development Tools and Standards\n\nThe project uses modern development tools and practices:\n\n- **PHP Backend**: Symfony components with Doctrine ORM\n- **Frontend**: Vue.js with TypeScript and Vite build system\n- **Containerization**: Docker-based development and deployment\n- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions for automated testing and building\n\n## Translation Contributions\n\nAzuraCast supports internationalization through CrowdIn, allowing community members to contribute translations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    CrowdIn[\"CrowdIn Platform\u003cbr/\u003etranslate.azuracast.com\"]\n    Languages[\"Language Support\u003cbr/\u003eMultiple Locales\"]\n    Integration[\"Application Integration\u003cbr/\u003eAutomatic Updates\"]\n    \n    CrowdIn --\u003e Languages\n    Languages --\u003e Integration\n```\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:97-98](), [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml:16-18]()\n\n## Community Support and Recognition\n\n### Acknowledgments\n\nThe project recognizes several organizations that support AzuraCast development [README.md:93-106]():\n\n- **JetBrains**: Development tools (PhpStorm)\n- **CrowdIn**: Translation platform\n- **Netlify**: Website hosting\n- **BrowserStack**: Cross-platform testing\n- **Depot**: Docker image builds\n\n### Financial Support\n\nWhile AzuraCast remains free, the project accepts donations to support the lead developer through Ko-fi and Patreon platforms [README.md:110-115]().\n\n## Support Process and Expectations\n\n### Response Times and Volunteer Nature\n\nContributors and users should understand that AzuraCast is maintained by volunteers [README.md:68-70](). Response times may vary, and patience is appreciated when seeking support or contributing to the project.\n\n### Log Requirements\n\nAll support requests and bug reports require relevant log output [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml:59-64](). The troubleshooting documentation provides instructions for accessing and interpreting system logs.\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:50-90](), [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml:1-64](), [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml:1-22](), [KNOWN-ISSUES.md:1-48]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2d:T2ca5,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Development Environment Setup\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.gitignore](.gitignore)\n- [.gitpod.yml](.gitpod.yml)\n- [Makefile](Makefile)\n- [crowdin.yaml](crowdin.yaml)\n- [depot.json](depot.json)\n- [docker-compose.dev.yml](docker-compose.dev.yml)\n- [docker-compose.installer.yml](docker-compose.installer.yml)\n- [docker-compose.sample.yml](docker-compose.sample.yml)\n- [docker-compose.testing.yml](docker-compose.testing.yml)\n- [docker.sh](docker.sh)\n- [util/setup_gitpod.sh](util/setup_gitpod.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers setting up a local development environment for AzuraCast using Docker. It includes installation procedures, configuration management, development workflows, and debugging tools specifically for contributors and developers working on the AzuraCast codebase.\n\nFor information about production deployment, see [Docker Installation](#2.1). For testing and CI/CD setup, see [Testing and CI/CD](#6.3). For general contributing guidelines, see [Contributing to AzuraCast](#6.1).\n\n## Overview and Prerequisites\n\nAzuraCast development environment uses Docker containers to provide a consistent development experience across different operating systems. The development setup differs from production by including additional debugging tools, direct database access, source code mounting, and development-specific configurations.\n\n### System Requirements\n\nThe development environment supports Linux and macOS systems with specific architecture requirements:\n\n| Component | Requirement |\n|-----------|-------------|\n| Operating System | Linux (x86_64, aarch64) or macOS |\n| Container Runtime | Docker or Podman |\n| Architecture | x86_64 (AMD64) or aarch64 (ARM64) |\n| Required Commands | `curl`, `awk`, `sudo` (if not root) |\n\nThe installation script automatically validates these requirements through the `check-install-requirements()` function.\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:266-346]()\n\n## Development Environment Architecture\n\nThe development environment consists of multiple Docker containers and volumes that provide isolated services while allowing direct access to source code and debugging tools.\n\n### Container and Volume Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Docker Host\"\n        subgraph \"Source Code Mount\"\n            SourceCode[\"Host: /path/to/azuracast\u003cbr/\u003eContainer: /var/azuracast/www\"]\n            VendorDir[\"Host: ./vendor\u003cbr/\u003eContainer: /var/azuracast/www/vendor\"]\n            NodeModules[\"Host: ./node_modules\u003cbr/\u003eContainer: /var/azuracast/www/node_modules\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"azuracast_web Container\"\n            WebService[\"Web Service\u003cbr/\u003ePort 80/443\"]\n            MariaDB[\"MariaDB\u003cbr/\u003ePort 3306 (exposed)\"]\n            Redis[\"Redis\u003cbr/\u003ePort 6379 (exposed)\"]\n            Centrifugo[\"Centrifugo\u003cbr/\u003ePort 6025 (exposed)\"]\n            Liquidsoap[\"Liquidsoap Engine\"]\n            PHP[\"PHP-FPM + Nginx\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Docker Volumes\"\n            StationData[\"azuracast_station_data\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast/stations\"]\n            DBData[\"azuracast_db_data\u003cbr/\u003e/var/lib/mysql\"]\n            WWWUploads[\"azuracast_www_uploads\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast/storage/uploads\"]\n            Backups[\"azuracast_backups\u003cbr/\u003e/var/azuracast/backups\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Configuration Files\"\n            EnvFile[\".env\u003cbr/\u003eDocker Compose Config\"]\n            AzuraEnv[\"azuracast.env\u003cbr/\u003eApplication Config\"]\n            DockerCompose[\"docker-compose.yml\u003cbr/\u003eService Definitions\"]\n            DockerOverride[\"docker-compose.override.yml\u003cbr/\u003eDevelopment Overrides\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    SourceCode --\u003e WebService\n    VendorDir --\u003e PHP\n    NodeModules --\u003e WebService\n    StationData --\u003e Liquidsoap\n    DBData --\u003e MariaDB\n    WWWUploads --\u003e WebService\n    EnvFile --\u003e WebService\n    AzuraEnv --\u003e WebService\n    DockerCompose --\u003e WebService\n    DockerOverride --\u003e WebService\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docker-compose.dev.yml:1-25](), [docker-compose.sample.yml:177-187]()\n\n## Installation Methods\n\n### Local Development Installation\n\nThe primary method for setting up a development environment uses the `install-dev` function which configures the system with development-specific settings:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"./docker.sh install-dev\"] --\u003e CheckDocker[\"Check Docker Installation\u003cbr/\u003einstall-docker()\"]\n    CheckDocker --\u003e CheckCompose[\"Check Docker Compose\u003cbr/\u003einstall-docker-compose()\"]\n    CheckCompose --\u003e CopyConfigs[\"Copy Development Configs\u003cbr/\u003edocker-compose.sample.yml → docker-compose.yml\u003cbr/\u003edocker-compose.dev.yml → docker-compose.override.yml\u003cbr/\u003edev.env → .env\u003cbr/\u003eazuracast.dev.env → azuracast.env\"]\n    CopyConfigs --\u003e SetPerms[\"Set PUID/PGID\u003cbr/\u003eAZURACAST_PUID=$(id -u)\u003cbr/\u003eAZURACAST_PGID=$(id -g)\"]\n    SetPerms --\u003e BuildImages[\"docker compose build\"]\n    BuildImages --\u003e RunInstaller[\"docker compose run --rm web\u003cbr/\u003eazuracast_dev_install\"]\n    RunInstaller --\u003e StartServices[\"docker compose up -d\"]\n    StartServices --\u003e Complete[\"Development Environment Ready\"]\n```\n\nThe installation process performs the following steps:\n\n1. **Docker Validation**: Ensures Docker and Docker Compose are installed and functional\n2. **Configuration Setup**: Copies development-specific configuration files\n3. **Permission Configuration**: Sets user/group IDs for file system permissions\n4. **Image Building**: Builds the development Docker image with debugging tools\n5. **Database Initialization**: Runs initial setup and database migrations\n6. **Service Startup**: Starts all containers in detached mode\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:511-562](), [Makefile:7-8]()\n\n### Cloud Development (GitPod)\n\nFor cloud-based development, AzuraCast provides GitPod integration with automatic SSL certificate generation and environment configuration:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    GitPod[\"GitPod Workspace Launch\"] --\u003e SetupScript[\"util/setup_gitpod.sh\"]\n    SetupScript --\u003e MkCert[\"Generate SSL Certificate\u003cbr/\u003emkcert\"]\n    MkCert --\u003e ConfigEnv[\"Configure Base URL\u003cbr/\u003eINIT_BASE_URL\"]\n    ConfigEnv --\u003e SSHKeys[\"Setup SSH Keys\u003cbr/\u003eGit Signing\"]\n    SSHKeys --\u003e InstallDev[\"./docker.sh install-dev\"]\n    InstallDev --\u003e Ready[\"Development Environment Ready\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.gitpod.yml:35-52](), [util/setup_gitpod.sh:117-143]()\n\n## Configuration Management\n\n### Environment Files\n\nThe development environment uses multiple configuration files to separate concerns:\n\n| File | Purpose | Source |\n|------|---------|--------|\n| `.env` | Docker Compose variables | `dev.env` |\n| `azuracast.env` | Application configuration | `azuracast.dev.env` |\n| `docker-compose.yml` | Service definitions | `docker-compose.sample.yml` |\n| `docker-compose.override.yml` | Development overrides | `docker-compose.dev.yml` |\n\n### Development-Specific Configurations\n\nThe development override file provides additional capabilities not present in production:\n\n```yaml\n# Exposed service ports for direct access\nports:\n  - \"127.0.0.1:3306:3306\"  # MariaDB\n  - \"127.0.0.1:6025:6025\"  # Centrifugo\n  - \"127.0.0.1:6379:6379\"  # Redis\n\n# Source code mounting for live editing\nvolumes:\n  - $PWD:/var/azuracast/www\n  - $PWD/vendor:/var/azuracast/www/vendor\n  - $PWD/node_modules:/var/azuracast/www/node_modules\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docker-compose.dev.yml:7-22]()\n\n## Development Workflow\n\n### Common Development Commands\n\nThe `Makefile` provides convenient shortcuts for common development tasks:\n\n| Command | Purpose | Docker Equivalent |\n|---------|---------|-------------------|\n| `make install` | Initial development setup | `./docker.sh install-dev` |\n| `make up` | Start services | `docker compose up -d` |\n| `make down` | Stop services | `docker compose down` |\n| `make build` | Rebuild and restart | `docker compose build \u0026\u0026 make restart` |\n| `make update` | Update after code changes | `docker compose build \u0026\u0026 make post-update` |\n| `make test` | Run test suite | `docker compose exec --user=azuracast web composer run cleanup-and-test` |\n| `make bash` | Access container shell | `docker compose exec --user=azuracast web bash` |\n\n**Sources:** [Makefile:1-54]()\n\n### Update and Build Cycle\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    CodeChange[\"Code Changes\"] --\u003e Build[\"make build\u003cbr/\u003edocker compose build\"]\n    Build --\u003e PostUpdate[\"make post-update\u003cbr/\u003eazuracast_dev_install --update\"]\n    PostUpdate --\u003e StartServices[\"make up\u003cbr/\u003edocker compose up -d\"]\n    StartServices --\u003e Test[\"make test\u003cbr/\u003ecomposer run cleanup-and-test\"]\n    Test --\u003e Verify[\"Verify Changes\"]\n    Verify --\u003e CodeChange\n    \n    subgraph \"Alternative Flows\"\n        QuickRestart[\"make restart\u003cbr/\u003edown + up\"]\n        HotReload[\"Source Code Changes\u003cbr/\u003eAuto-reloaded via volume mount\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [Makefile:18-38]()\n\n## Development Tools and Debugging\n\n### Container Access and Debugging\n\nThe development environment provides multiple ways to access and debug the running system:\n\n#### CLI Access\n```bash\n# Access AzuraCast CLI as azuracast user\n./docker.sh cli [command]\n\n# Direct shell access\n./docker.sh bash\nmake bash\n\n# Root shell access (for system debugging)\nmake bash-root\n```\n\n#### Database Access\n```bash\n# Direct MariaDB access\n./docker.sh db\n\n# External connection (via exposed port)\nmysql -h 127.0.0.1 -P 3306 -u azuracast -p\n```\n\n#### Service Debugging\nThe development configuration exposes internal service ports for direct access:\n\n| Service | Port | Purpose |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| MariaDB | 3306 | Database administration |\n| Redis | 6379 | Cache inspection |\n| Centrifugo | 6025 | WebSocket debugging |\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:678-698](), [docker-compose.dev.yml:8-10]()\n\n### Development Image Features\n\nThe development Docker image includes additional debugging capabilities:\n\n- **SYS_PTRACE capability**: Enables debugging tools and profilers\n- **Host gateway access**: Allows connection to host-based debugging tools\n- **Source code mounting**: Enables live code editing without rebuilds\n- **Development dependencies**: Includes additional PHP extensions and tools\n\n**Sources:** [docker-compose.dev.yml:11-24]()\n\n## Environment Variables and Configuration\n\n### Key Development Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default | Notes |\n|----------|---------|---------|--------|\n| `AZURACAST_PUID` | User ID for file permissions | `$(id -u)` | Set automatically for non-root users |\n| `AZURACAST_PGID` | Group ID for file permissions | `$(id -g)` | Set automatically for non-root users |\n| `AZURACAST_PODMAN_MODE` | Enable Podman compatibility | `false` | Set to `true` when Podman detected |\n| `APPLICATION_ENV` | Application environment | `development` | Enables debug features |\n| `LOG_LEVEL` | Logging verbosity | `debug` | Increased logging in development |\n\nThe Docker utility script automatically configures these variables during development installation.\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:548-556](), [docker-compose.testing.yml:5-7]()\n\n### Configuration File Management\n\nThe development environment uses a sophisticated `.env` file management system implemented in the Docker utility script:\n\n```bash\n# Set environment variable\n.env --file .env set VARIABLE_NAME=value\n\n# Get environment variable\n.env --file .env get VARIABLE_NAME\n\n# Generate missing variables\n.env generate VARIABLE_NAME command_to_generate_value\n```\n\nThis system ensures consistent configuration management across development and production environments.\n\n**Sources:** [docker.sh:27-135]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2e:T2433,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Testing and CI/CD\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.dockerignore](.dockerignore)\n- [.github/workflows/default.yml](.github/workflows/default.yml)\n- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile)\n- [backend/config/services.php](backend/config/services.php)\n- [backend/src/Console/Command/MigrateDbCommand.php](backend/src/Console/Command/MigrateDbCommand.php)\n- [backend/src/Normalizer/BigNumberNormalizer.php](backend/src/Normalizer/BigNumberNormalizer.php)\n- [composer.json](composer.json)\n- [composer.lock](composer.lock)\n- [crowdin.yaml](crowdin.yaml)\n- [docker-compose.testing.yml](docker-compose.testing.yml)\n- [phpstan.neon](phpstan.neon)\n- [util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh](util/docker/mariadb/setup/mariadb.sh)\n- [util/docker/web/setup/php.sh](util/docker/web/setup/php.sh)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers AzuraCast's automated testing infrastructure and continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline. It details the GitHub Actions workflows, testing frameworks, code quality tools, and build processes that ensure code reliability and automate deployments.\n\nFor information about setting up a local development environment, see [Development Environment Setup](#6.2). For contribution guidelines and code standards, see [Contributing to AzuraCast](#6.1).\n\n## CI/CD Pipeline Overview\n\nAzuraCast uses GitHub Actions for its CI/CD pipeline, with a comprehensive workflow that handles testing, building, and publishing Docker images across multiple platforms.\n\n### Pipeline Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    PR[\"Pull Request\"] --\u003e Trigger[\"Workflow Trigger\"]\n    Push[\"Push to main/stable\"] --\u003e Trigger\n    Schedule[\"Weekly Schedule\"] --\u003e Trigger\n    \n    Trigger --\u003e PHPTest[\"php-test Job\"]\n    Trigger --\u003e NodeTest[\"node-test Job\"]\n    \n    PHPTest --\u003e PHPLint[\"parallel-lint\"]\n    PHPTest --\u003e PHPStan[\"phpstan analyze\"]\n    PHPTest --\u003e PHPCS[\"phpcs\"]\n    \n    NodeTest --\u003e TSCheck[\"npm run tsc\"]\n    NodeTest --\u003e ESLint[\"npm run lint\"]\n    \n    PHPTest --\u003e Prepare[\"prepare Job\"]\n    NodeTest --\u003e Prepare\n    \n    Prepare --\u003e LocaleGen[\"locale:generate\"]\n    Prepare --\u003e Crowdin[\"Crowdin Sync\"]\n    Prepare --\u003e LocaleImport[\"locale:import\"]\n    Prepare --\u003e AssetBuild[\"npm run build\"]\n    Prepare --\u003e OpenAPI[\"azuracast:api:docs\"]\n    Prepare --\u003e GitInfo[\"write_git_info.sh\"]\n    \n    Prepare --\u003e Build[\"build Job\"]\n    Build --\u003e DockerBuild[\"depot/build-push-action\"]\n    DockerBuild --\u003e DockerHub[\"DockerHub Registry\"]\n    DockerBuild --\u003e GHCR[\"GitHub Container Registry\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:1-227]()\n\n### Workflow Configuration\n\nThe main workflow is defined in `.github/workflows/default.yml` and includes:\n\n- **Triggers**: Pull requests, pushes to main/stable branches, weekly schedule\n- **Concurrency control**: Cancels previous runs when new commits are pushed\n- **Multi-platform builds**: Supports `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` architectures\n- **Conditional execution**: Prepare and build jobs only run on push events, not PRs\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:24-26](), [.github/workflows/default.yml:224]()\n\n## PHP Testing Suite\n\nThe PHP testing infrastructure combines multiple tools to ensure code quality and reliability.\n\n### Testing Framework Components\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    PHPTest[\"php-test Job\"] --\u003e Composer[\"composer install\"]\n    Composer --\u003e Lint[\"parallel-lint\"]\n    Composer --\u003e StaticAnalysis[\"PHPStan\"]\n    Composer --\u003e CodeStyle[\"PHP_CodeSniffer\"]\n    \n    Lint --\u003e CS2PR[\"cs2pr Reporter\"]\n    StaticAnalysis --\u003e CS2PR\n    CodeStyle --\u003e CS2PR\n    \n    DevTest[\"dev-test Script\"] --\u003e Lint\n    DevTest --\u003e StaticAnalysis  \n    DevTest --\u003e CodeStyle\n    \n    Codeception[\"codeception\"] --\u003e Coverage[\"Coverage Reports\"]\n    Codeception --\u003e Integration[\"Integration Tests\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:29-65](), [composer.json:176-201]()\n\n### Static Analysis Configuration\n\nPHPStan is configured at level 8 with Doctrine integration:\n\n| Configuration | Value |\n|---------------|-------|\n| Analysis Level | 8 (Maximum) |\n| Doctrine Support | Enabled via `phpstan-doctrine` |\n| Paths Analyzed | `backend/bin`, `backend/config`, `backend/src`, `backend/templates`, `web` |\n| Bootstrap Files | `util/phpstan.php` |\n| Parallel Jobs | 20 jobs, 300s timeout |\n\n**Sources:** [phpstan.neon:7](), [phpstan.neon:12-17](), [phpstan.neon:40-42]()\n\n### Testing Dependencies\n\nKey testing packages defined in `composer.json`:\n\n- **codeception/codeception**: Acceptance and functional testing\n- **phpunit/phpunit**: Unit testing framework  \n- **phpstan/phpstan**: Static analysis\n- **php-parallel-lint/php-parallel-lint**: Syntax checking\n- **squizlabs/php_codesniffer**: Code style enforcement\n- **mockery/mockery**: Mocking framework\n\n**Sources:** [composer.json:117-137]()\n\n## Frontend Testing\n\nFrontend testing focuses on TypeScript compilation and code linting using Node.js tools.\n\n### Node.js Testing Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    NodeSetup[\"Setup Node LTS/jod\"] --\u003e NPMInstall[\"npm ci --include=dev\"]\n    NPMInstall --\u003e TypeScriptCheck[\"npm run tsc\"]\n    NPMInstall --\u003e ESLintCheck[\"npm run lint\"]\n    \n    TypeScriptCheck --\u003e TSResults[\"TypeScript Validation\"]\n    ESLintCheck --\u003e LintResults[\"Code Style Validation\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:67-84]()\n\nThe frontend testing verifies:\n- TypeScript type checking across the Vue.js application\n- ESLint rules for code consistency and best practices\n- Dependency installation and cache management\n\n## Code Quality Tools\n\n### PHP Code Standards\n\nThe project uses PHP_CodeSniffer for enforcing coding standards with these script aliases:\n\n| Script | Purpose |\n|--------|---------|\n| `phpcbf` | Automatically fix code style issues |\n| `phpcs` | Check code against style standards |\n| `phplint` | Validate PHP syntax |\n| `phpstan` | Run static analysis |\n\n**Sources:** [composer.json:196-199]()\n\n### Composite Testing Scripts\n\nComposer defines combined testing workflows:\n\n- **`dev-test`**: Runs `phplint`, `phpstan`, and `phpcs`\n- **`cleanup-and-test`**: Runs `phpcbf` then `dev-test`\n- **`cleanup-and-test-full`**: Adds Codeception tests without coverage\n\n**Sources:** [composer.json:180-194]()\n\n## Build and Deployment Process\n\n### Asset Preparation Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    PrepareJob[\"prepare Job\"] --\u003e EnvSetup[\"Setup PHP 8.4 + Node LTS\"]\n    EnvSetup --\u003e ClearAssets[\"Clear Static Assets\"]\n    ClearAssets --\u003e ComposerInstall[\"composer install\"]\n    ComposerInstall --\u003e NPMInstall[\"npm ci --include=dev\"]\n    \n    NPMInstall --\u003e LocaleGenerate[\"locale:generate\"]\n    LocaleGenerate --\u003e CrowdinSync[\"Crowdin Translation Sync\"]\n    CrowdinSync --\u003e LocaleImport[\"locale:import\"]\n    LocaleImport --\u003e ViteBuild[\"npm run build\"]\n    ViteBuild --\u003e OpenAPIBuild[\"azuracast:api:docs\"]\n    OpenAPIBuild --\u003e GitInfoWrite[\"write_git_info.sh\"]\n    \n    GitInfoWrite --\u003e ArtifactUpload[\"Upload Artifacts\"]\n    ArtifactUpload --\u003e BuildJob[\"build Job\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:86-171]()\n\n### Docker Build Process\n\nThe Docker build uses a multi-stage approach defined in the `Dockerfile`:\n\n1. **Dependencies stage**: Collects external binaries (MariaDB, Icecast, PHP extensions)\n2. **Pre-final stage**: Base PHP-FPM image with all dependencies installed\n3. **Development stage**: Includes dev tools and source code mounting\n4. **Final stage**: Production-optimized image with compiled assets\n\n**Sources:** [Dockerfile:25-188]()\n\n### Multi-Platform Publishing\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    BuildJob[\"build Job\"] --\u003e DepotSetup[\"Setup Depot Builder\"]\n    DepotSetup --\u003e DockerLogin[\"Login to Registries\"]\n    DockerLogin --\u003e MetaBuild[\"Build Docker Metadata\"]\n    MetaBuild --\u003e MultiPlatform[\"depot/build-push-action\"]\n    \n    MultiPlatform --\u003e AMD64[\"linux/amd64\"]\n    MultiPlatform --\u003e ARM64[\"linux/arm64\"]\n    \n    AMD64 --\u003e DockerHub[\"azuracast/azuracast\"]\n    ARM64 --\u003e DockerHub\n    AMD64 --\u003e GHCR[\"ghcr.io/azuracast/azuracast\"]\n    ARM64 --\u003e GHCR\n```\n\n**Sources:** [.github/workflows/default.yml:172-227]()\n\n## Development Testing Environment\n\n### Local Testing Setup\n\nFor development testing, AzuraCast provides a Docker Compose configuration:\n\n```yaml\n# docker-compose.testing.yml\nservices:\n  web:\n    build:\n      context: .\n    environment:\n      APPLICATION_ENV: 'testing'\n      LOG_LEVEL: 'debug'\n```\n\n**Sources:** [docker-compose.testing.yml:1-11]()\n\n### Database Migration Testing\n\nThe migration system includes safety mechanisms for testing database schema changes:\n\n- **Backup creation**: Automatic database dumps before migrations\n- **Rollback capability**: Emergency restore functionality via `tryEmergencyRestore()`\n- **Migration validation**: Checks if database is up-to-date before proceeding\n\n**Sources:** [backend/src/Console/Command/MigrateDbCommand.php:43-48](), [backend/src/Console/Command/MigrateDbCommand.php:71-82]()\n\n### Testing Dependencies Integration\n\nThe dependency injection container is configured to use different cache adapters during testing:\n\n- **Production**: Redis-based caching\n- **Testing**: Array-based in-memory cache for isolation\n- **CLI**: Array adapter to prevent cache pollution\n\n**Sources:** [backend/config/services.php:174-199]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"5:[\"$\",\"$L15\",null,{\"repoName\":\"AzuraCast/AzuraCast\",\"hasConfig\":false,\"canSteer\":true,\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L16\",null,{\"wiki\":{\"metadata\":{\"repo_name\":\"AzuraCast/AzuraCast\",\"commit_hash\":\"f9539ff5\",\"generated_at\":\"2025-08-05T23:56:01.093081\",\"config\":null,\"config_source\":\"none\"},\"pages\":[{\"page_plan\":{\"id\":\"1\",\"title\":\"Overview
